replaces_summary: Alignment; Suggestion; Inspire Greatness; Mass Suggestion; Well-Versed; 2nd-level Versatile Performance

name: Solacer
description: Solacers are experts in the healing arts as well as creators of performances that console the distraught, rally the stricken, raise spirits, and vanquish sorrow.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Bardic Knowledge; Lore Master; Countersong; Versatile Performance; Jack-of-All-Trades

name: Songhealer
description: The songhealer brings peace and surcease of pain, calming wild emotions and providing a balm for the wounded body.
replaces_summary: Versatile Performance; Frightening Tune; Deadly Performance

name: Sorrowsoul
description: While most bards, on occasion, sing mournful tunes or craft elegies that depict the deepest sadness or the most profound suffering, the sorrowsoul has known tragedy and loss on a level so intimate, he has bound it to his soul.
replaces_summary: Soothing Perfrmance; Versatile Performance; Well-Versed; Lore Master

name: Sound Striker
description: They say that words can cut deeper than any blade, and the sound striker proves this true.
replaces_summary: Inspire Competence; Suggestion

name: Speaker of the Palatine Eye
description: speaker of the Palatine Eye influences others using his occult knowledge rather than the boisterous enthusiasm of most bards.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Spells; Countersong; Jack-of-all-Trades

name: Stonesinger
description: Stonesingers are rare bards capable of vocalizing their bardic performances through subsonic harmonies, allowing their performances to carry as subtle vibrations through stone, rather than through the air.
replaces_summary: Spellcasting; Bardic Performance; Countersong; Dirge of Doom

name: Street Performer
description: Whether acrobat, troubadour, or thespian, the street performer mixes with the masses, singing for his supper.
replaces_summary: Inspire Courage; Inspire Competence; Inspire Greatness; Inspire Heroics; Countersong; Bardic Knowledge; Lore Master

name: Studious Librarian
description: Studious librarians are bards that have studied long in great libraries of the Inner Sea region.
replaces_summary: Distraction; Suggestion; Jack-of-All-Trades; Dirge of Doom; Mass Suggestion; Deadly Performance

name: Thundercaller
description: Thundercallers bear the knowledge of the ancients, wielding it like a weapon to protect the land for which they care so deeply.
replaces_summary: Bardic Knowledge; Inspire Competence; Suggestion; Mass Suggestion; Dirge of Doom; Frightening Tune

name: Voice of Brigh
description: The Whisper in Bronze sometimes sends inspiration to her favorite followers in the form of music
replaces_summary: Fascinate; Dirge of Doom; Soothing Performance; Frightening Tune

name: Wasteland Chronicler
description: Wasteland chroniclers explore the farthest reaches of desolate wastelands, seeking to unlock the mysteries found there and meet the inhabitants of such regions
replaces_summary: Bardic Knowledge; Inspire Competence

name: Watersinger
description: (Undine Only) The watersinger’s song reaches from the depths of his soul into the elemental waters from which life first sprang.
replaces_summary: Fascinate, Suggestion, Mass Suggestion, Inspire Competence, 5th-level Lore Master

name: Wit
description: The wit is a master of clever repartee and verbal dueling, moving through noble courts like a fish through water.
replaces_summary: Bardic Knowledge; Inspire Competence; Dirge of Doom; Frightening Tune; Versatile Performance; Lore Master; Jack-of-All-Trades

name: Ancestral Harbinger
description: While all bloodragers tap into their bloodlines to fuel the arcane energies they use in battle, some have the ability to call directly to the spirits of their ancestors to aid them, summoning spirits to fight for them or inspire their allies.
replaces_summary: Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge; 6th, 12th, and 18th-level Bloodline Feats

name: Bloody-Knuckled Rowdy
description: The bloody-knuckled rowdy focuses on tricks and maneuvers that are quick and effective, duplicating combat styles from professional brawlers, street thugs, and even trapped animals.
replaces_summary: Damage Reduction; Spellcasting; Fast Movement; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Enlightened Bloodrager
description: Troubled by overwhelming rage and mystic forces beyond their comprehension, bloodragers have been traveling to the Magaambya for generations, seeking the peace and enlightenment promised by students of Old-Mage Jatembe.
replaces_summary: Spellcasting; 1st, 4th-level Bloodline Power; Bloodrage; Bonus Spells; 7th, 13th, 19th-level Damage Reduction

name: Hag-Riven
description: All changelings have the potential to transform into hags, but the process is a slow one, requiring several nights to complete. When left incomplete—by virtue of a wily changeling’s escape or her mother’s death—the creature is scarred and saturated in arcane power.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Fast Movement; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge; Damage Reduction

name: Id Rager
description: An id rager lacks a supernatural taint to his blood, instead drawing power from pure emotion.
replaces_summary: Bloodline; Bloodline Spells; Bloodline Powers; Spellcasting; Eschew Materials; Bloodline Feats

name: Prowler at World's End
description: Many catfolk bloodragers capable of communing with the ancient spirits of creation take on the burden of protecting the world from the sinister forces that exist at the world’s fringes.
replaces_summary: Bloodline Powers; 9th, 12th, 15th-level Bloodline Feats; Greater Bloodrage; Mighty Bloodrage

name: Symbol Striker
description: Symbol strikers are unique form of rune-wielding berserker spellcasters whose tradition originates from Dongun Hold, a Sky Citadel in Alkenstar
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Fast Movement; 6th, 9th, 12th, 18th-level Bloodline Feats;

name: Urban Bloodrager
description: Like the urban barbarian, the urban bloodrager has learned to control her rage in so-called polite society.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Bloodrage; Blood Sanctuary; Damage Reduction

name: Battle Dancer
description: Battle dancers practice a martial art that emphasizes constant motion
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Armor Proficiencies; Brawler's Cunning; Brawler's Flurry; AC Bonus; Knockout

name: Bouncer
description: Some taverns employ bouncers to keep out undesirables or for when their customers get too rowdy.
replaces_summary: Martial Flexibility; 2nd-level Bonus Feat; Close Weapon Mastery

name: Constructed Pugilist
description: Brawlers that develop combat skills that use their prostheses so they always have a weapon at hand
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Martial Flexibility; Bonus Combat Feats

name: Feral Striker
description: A feral striker taps into a reservoir of druidic power hidden inside herself, allowing her to take animalistic characteristics.
replaces_summary: Martial Flexibility

name: Hinyasi
description: The hinyasi's martial traditions are centered on the use of farming tools and on other improvised weapons.
replaces_summary: Armor Proficiencies; 2nd-level Bonus Feat; Knockout; Close Weapon Mastery

name: Living Avalanche
description: When a living avalanche is on the move, no one can stand in her way.
replaces_summary: 2nd and 5th-level Bonus Feats; Maneuver Training; AC Bonus; Awesome Blow; Improved Awesome Blow

name: Strong-Side Boxer
description: A strong-side boxer employs a unique fighting style that incorporates a rope or cord wound around her hand.
replaces_summary: AC Bonus; Maneuver Training; Close Weapon Mastery

name: Turfer
description: A turfer has a mastery over particular types of terrain.
replaces_summary: Maneuver Training; Knockout

name: Ulfen Beast-Wrestler
description: For these mighty grapplers, wrestling normal opponents has lost its challenge—they seek greater targets for glory.
replaces_summary: Maneuver Training; AC Bonus

name: Venomfist
description: Thanks to alchemical experiments and rigorous study of venomous creatures, a venomfist has toxic unarmed strikes.
replaces_summary: Unarmed Strike; Knockout; Close Weapon Mastery

name: Verdant Grappler
description: By accepting spirits of nature into her soul, a verdant grappler can entangle her foes with tangled undergrowth.
replaces_summary: 2nd and 11th-level Bonus Feats; Maneuver Training; Close Weapon Mastery

name: Winding Path Renegade
description: Winding path renegades studied at the Houses of Perfection in Jalmeray, but while they grasped the mechanical aspects of the forms, they lacked the mental discipline required by the masters of the school.
replaces_summary: 2nd, 8th, 14th-level Bonus Feat; AC Bonus

name: Beast Rider
description: Where some cavaliers are simply skilled with horses or well-trained knights, the beast rider spends his life in constant pursuit of the most perfect mount, forming bonds with greater, more powerful, and more exotic creatures.
replaces_summary: Mount; Expert Trainer

name: Castellan
description: A castellan is a cavalier who dedicates himself to the defense of a castle or walled city.
replaces_summary: Mount; Cavalier’s Charge; Expert Trainer; Mighty Charge; Demanding Challenge; Supreme Charge

name: Charger
description: (Centaur Only) No warrior better epitomizes the centaur’s devastating martial power than the charger.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Mount; Cavalier’s Charge; Expert Trainer; Mighty Charge

name: Circuit Judge
description: A circuit judge claims a specific region, becoming an expert in the local laws and, perhaps, becoming a fearsome executioner.
replaces_summary: Tactician; Greater Tactician; Master Tactician; Challenge; Demanding Challenge

name: Constable
description: Constables keep order in the narrow streets and dark alleys of settlements.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Mount; Cavalier’s Charge; Expert Trainer; Banner; Mighty Charge; Supreme Charge; Greater Banner

name: Courtly Knight
description: These courtly knights are equally at home in a duel with words or swords.
replaces_summary: Tactician; Greater Tactician; Master Tactician

name: Daring Champion
description: While many cavaliers are the champions of old fighting forms, some younger, more daring cavaliers mix a martial style influenced by the lighter armored and more flamboyant swashbuckler forms with the dedication of cavalier orders.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Mount; Cavalier’s Charge; Expert Trainer; Mighty Charge; Supreme Charge

name: Daring General
description: Those cavaliers most gifted at command often become daring generals.
replaces_summary: 6th, 12th, 18th-level Bonus Feats; Demanding Challenge; Supreme Charge

name: Disciple of the Pike
description: The Hellknight Order of the Pike are renowned monster hunters, taking on large game using ancient weapon arts combined with modern equipment.
replaces_summary: Mount; Cavalier's Charge; Expert Trainer; Banner; Greater Banner; Mighty Charge; Supreme Charge

name: Drakerider
description: While many cavaliers dream of riding a dragon into battle, drakeriders come to learn that the reality involves far more effort than they had expected.
replaces_summary: Mount; Tactcian; Expert Trainer; Banner; Greater Tactician; Greater Banner; Master Tactician;

name: Emissary
description: Some cavaliers focus more on speed and mobility than they do on the raw power of the mounted charge.
replaces_summary: Tactician; Banner; Greater Tactician; Greater Banner; Supreme Charge

name: Esquire
description: The esquire is a roving knight who acquires the services of a faithful follower—an aide-de-camp—who serves as the esquire’s assistant, spear carrier, and guard.
replaces_summary: Mount; Cavalier’s Charge; Expert Trainer; Banner; Mighty Charge; Supreme Charge

name: Fell Rider
description: (Hobgoblin Only) The fell rider rides a bestial steed, a mount mastered by him alone. He tramples his enemies down, leaving twisted bodies in his wake, and fear rides with him.
replaces_summary: Mount; Cavalier’s Charge; Expert Trainer; Banner; Mighty Charge; Greater Banner; Supreme Charge

name: First Mother's Fang
description: A first mother’s fang acts as a servant of his nation, a skilled general and tactician in times of war and a noble governor during times of peace.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Weapon Proficiencies; Mount; Order

name: Gallant
description: Gallants embody the virtues of honor, generosity, and civility. Their personal symbol serves as an inspiration to others in a courtly setting as much as on a battlefield.
replaces_summary: Order; Banner; Greater Banner

name: Gendarme
description: The gendarme cares less for the finer points of tactical precision than he does for the exhilaration of the charge: the rush of wind through the visor of his helmet, the feel of his couched lance, the satisfying shriek of armor giving way before his weapon’s force as the point drives past metal into his foes.
replaces_summary: Tactician; Greater Tactician; Master Tactician; Supreme Charge

name: Ghost Rider
description: Ghost riders are conflicted warriors who use the powers of the phantasmal dead even while seeking out and banishing the corruption of undeath from plagued communities.
replaces_summary: Mount; Tactician; Greater Tactician; Master Tactician; Cavalier’s Charge; Expert Trainer; Banner; Mighty Charge; Greater Banner

name: Green Knight
description: Some knights serve nature itself and can be found protecting unspoiled natural places or serving fey beings.
replaces_summary: Tactician; Mount; Order; Cavalier’s Charge; Expert Trainer; Banner; Greater Tactician; Mighty Charge; Greater Banner; Master Tactician; Supreme Charge

name: Herald Squire
description: The herald squire is the eyes and ears of a traveling knight.
replaces_summary: Tactician; 2nd-level Order Ability; Cavalier's Charge

name: Honor Guard
description: Certain cavaliers are trained not as advance combatants, but as loyal guards, standing as firm defenders in the face of threats to their chosen charge.
replaces_summary: Challenge; Cavalier’s Charge; Mighty Charge; Demanding Challenge

name: Hooded Knight
description: Some cavaliers wander the lonely roads of the world—any world, including the First World—protecting travelers and acting as their honor guard.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Mount; Tactician; Greater Tactician; Master Tactician

name: Huntmaster
description: Huntmasters train the beasts favored by lordly castes into swift and deadly trackers.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Challenge; Mount; Tactician; Greater Tactician; Master Tactician; Cavalier’s Charge; Expert Trainer; Banner; Bonus Feats; Mighty Charge; Greater Banner; Supreme Charge

name: Hussar
description: Agile and maneuverable cavalry, hussars make up for their relatively light armor with their speed and cunning.
replaces_summary: Armor Proficiencies; Class Skills; Tactician; Banner; Greater Banner; 6th, 12th, 18th-level Bonus Feats; Greater Tactician; Master Tactician

name: Knight of Arnisant
description: The cavaliers known as knights of Arnisant revere the famous general who was crucial in the defeat of the Whispering Tyrant, the powerful wizard-king. Knights of Arnisant call upon Arnisant to grant them protection from evil magic, just as the Shield of Aroden protected him.
replaces_summary: Tactician; Expert Trainer; Banner; Greater Tactician; Demanding Challenge; Greater Tactician

name: Luring Cavalier
description: Those who study the perfection of strategy and tactics know that picking the battlefield can grant advantages that only overwhelming numbers of allies can eclipse.
replaces_summary: Challenge; Cavalier’s Charge; Mighty Charge; Demanding Challenge; Supreme Charge

name: Musketeer
description: Some cavaliers are entrusted by their masters with the care and use of expensive and powerful oddities—firearms.
replaces_summary: Mount; Expert Trainer

name: Oceanrider
description: Oceanriders are underwater cavaliers who ride dolphins, orcas, or seahorses.
replaces_summary: Armor Proficiency; Mount; Expert Trainer

name: Qabarat Outrider
description: (Lashunta Only) The war colleges of the Castrovelian city-state of Qabarat produce a distinctive breed of cavalier known as the Qabarat outrider. In defense of the lashunta city-states, outriders lead small units of regular troops, the members of which can link minds with one another to form bonds in battle few can match.
replaces_summary: Armor Proficiency; Tactician; Greater Tactician; Master Tactician; Banner; Greater Banner

name: Qadiran Horselord
description: Much like the breed of horses they ride, Qadiran horselords pride themselves on their mobility and endurance.
replaces_summary: Weapon and Armor Proficiency; Mount; Tactician; Cavalier's Charge; 6th-level Bonus Feat; Greater Tactician; Master Tactician

name: Saurian Champion
description: These primeval cavaliers idolize dinosaurs as apex predators and value them as powerful, oversized mounts.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Challenge; Order; Cavalier’s Mount; Tactician; Greater Tactician; Master Tactician; Banner; Greater Banner

name: Sister-in-Arms
description: Some of the most charismatic Gray Maidens combine their devotion to their sisters with the intense tactical training they received, learning to perfectly direct their companions and inspiring them to survive and emerge victorious.
replaces_summary: Challenge; Order; Mount; Cavalier's Charge; Expert Trainer; Mighty Charge; Supreme Charge

name: Spellscar Drifter
description: Spellscar drifters are self-reliant and always keep their firearms loaded—in the Spellscar Desert, a rider with a slow draw doesn’t live long.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Tactician; Cavalier’s Charge; Banner Cavalier; Bonus Feats; Greater Tactician; Mighty Charge; Demanding Challenge; Master Tactician; Supreme Charge

name: Standard Bearer
description: Some cavaliers prefer to stand away from the fray, their banners a beacon shining brightly over the battlefield, rallying their troops to victory.
replaces_summary: Mount; Banner; Mighty Charge; Supreme Charge

name: Strategist
description: Some cavaliers make a lifelong mission out of their exceptional ability to direct troops on the battlefield, combining tactical insight with a preternatural skill at improving the teamwork of their allies in order to win the day.
replaces_summary: Expert Trainer; Greater Banner; 18th-level Bonus Feat

name: Verdivant
description: Some vine leshys’ spirits are vibrant enough to project this animus beyond their own bodies
replaces_summary: Expert Trainer; Tactician; Banner; Greater Tactician; Greater Banner; Master Tactician; Bonus Feats; Cavalier's Charge

name: Vermin Tamer
description: Due to the strange terrain, the lack of sunlight, and numerous other hazards, keeping a traditional mount in the Darklands can prove difficult. Cavaliers native to or traveling the region may choose to tame local creatures instead.
replaces_summary: Mount; Expert Trainer; Demanding Challenge

name: Wave Rider
description: Throughout the Inner Sea region, wave riders patrol harbors, keeping them safe from dangerous sea creatures and watching for smugglers. The most famous of these is the hippocampus-riding Sea Cavalry of Absalom.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Mount

name: Angelfire Apostle
description: Angelfire apostles use the powers of good to avoid violence when possible and cleanse both maladies and evil creatures with blinding flames.
replaces_summary: Armor Proficiency; Spellcasting; Alignment; Channel Energy

name: Appeaser
description: A rare few seek the power of dark forces for brighter ends by focusing on the utilitarian aspects of a god’s portfolio, appeasing their deities through high praise and glorifying their chosen gods’ unaligned aspects.
replaces_summary: Aura; Channel Energy; Alignment; Spells; Domains

name: Asmodean Advocate
description: For the faithful of Asmodeus, the words used in a negotiation or contract matter more than their intent.
replaces_summary: Domains

name: Blossoming Light
description: Blossoming lights are clerics who take their pursuit of purity and light to extremes.
replaces_summary: Armor Proficiency; Alignment; Channel Energy; Domains

name: Cardinal
description: Cardinals wield significant political power, engaging in the game of intrigue on behalf of their churches
replaces_summary: Armor Proficieny; Spontaneous Casting; Domain; Class Skills; Skill Ranks; Base Attack Bonus

name: Channeler of the Unknown
description: (Ex-cleric archetype) Channelers of the unknown are those faithless clerics who channel the power of an unknown entity or force of the universe
replaces_summary: Weapon and Armor Proficiency; Spells; Aura; Channel Energy; Domains; Spontaneous Casting

name: Cloistered Cleric
description: Cloistered clerics typically live in a temple and rarely interact with the outside world. They are bookish and well learned in the lore of the faith, paying less attention to its magical and martial aspects.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Domains; Spells

name: Crashing Wave
description: Clerics who live in or near the sea sometimes embrace the watery aspect of Gozreh over the deity’s other dimensions and take the title of crashing wave; such clerics understand the tempestuousness and cruelty of the sea, but also its generosity and bounty
replaces_summary: Deity; Bonus Languages; Channel Energy; Spontaneous Casting

name: Crusader
description: Crusaders serve the militant arm of a church, ready to stand guard over the religion’s holy places and to be its swift, avenging arm against those who resist its truth.
replaces_summary: Domains; Spells

name: Demonic Apostle
description: (Drow Only) Demon worship is common among the drow, and so are ranks of demonic apostles, who gain magical insight from their dark lords and crush their chaotic masters’ enemies by channeling demonic energy.
replaces_summary: Domains; Channel Energy

name: Divine Paragon
description: Divine paragons strive to emulate their god’s ideals as closely as possible.
replaces_summary: Domain; Aura

name: Divine Scourge
description: Divine scourges take on the role of dealing out unique punishments on behalf of their deities, taking pleasure in carrying out their sacrosanct duties.
replaces_summary: Domains; Channel Energy

name: Divine Strategist
description: The divine strategist leads the armies of the faithful, not from the front lines but through her clever strategy and tactical acumen.
replaces_summary: Domains; Channel Energy

name: Elder Mythos Cultist
description: Typically grasping, secretive, and thoroughly mad, Elder Mythos cultists open their bodies and minds to horrifying realities not meant for the sane as they strive to prepare the world for the eventual return of their alien masters.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Domains; Channel Energy; Spontaneous Casting; 5th, 11th, and 19th-level Channel Energy increase

name: Evangelist
description: The evangelist is the voice of her religion in the world.
replaces_summary: Domains; Channel Energy; Spontaneous Casting

name: Fiendish Vessel
description: (Tiefling Only) Fiendish vessels, through their fiendish heritage, share an innate connection with their patron, and that connection grants them understanding and power.
replaces_summary: Domains; Channel Energy

name: Forgemaster
description: (Dwarf Only) Forgemasters are priestly dwarves who are ritual casters and expert enchanters, able to produce their rune-graven armaments with astonishing speed.
replaces_summary: Domains; Channel Energy

name: Foundation of Faith
description: Whether aiding the faithful or defending against the depredations of unbelievers, a foundation of faith is unshakable in her convictions.
replaces_summary: Channel Energy

name: Herald Caller
description: Unlike warpriests or paladins, who charge headlong into battle in the name of their patron deities, herald callers are adept at calling powerful outsiders to aid their brethren in battle.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Domains; Armor Proficiencies;

name: Hidden Priest
description: When practicing their religion is outlawed, the hidden priest learns to hide their true nature and practice magic in secret.
replaces_summary: 1st-level Domain Power, 8th-level Domain Power

name: Idealist
description: Idealist clerics are personifications of belief, each embracing the purest ideal of his deity’s realm.
replaces_summary: Spontaneous Casting; Channel Energy

name: Iron Priest
description: Iron priests preach of the messengers from beyond the stars, envoys of the divine brought to Golarion in a falling star.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Channel Energy; Spellcasting

name: Lawspeaker
description: Lawspeakers spread their philosophy of certain and unwavering justice throughout the Inner Sea region.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Domains

name: Mendevian Priest
description: Crusade-minded clerics of Iomedae, Gorum, and other churches come to Mendev to learn battlefield tactics and the weaknesses of demons.
replaces_summary: Domains

name: Merciful Healer
description: The merciful healer is a master of battlefield revivification, sustaining and restoring allies to keep them in the fight.
replaces_summary: Domains; Channel Energy

name: Roaming Exorcist
description: The roaming exorcist travels far and wide to root out possessions, hauntings, and hidden evils. The roaming exorcist extracts unruly spirits from not only victims of possession, but also haunted sites and accursed items.
replaces_summary: Skill Ranks; Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Domains; Channel Energy

name: Sacred Attendant
description: Sacred attendants bring out the varied beauty in everyone they help, and they typically worship deities of beauty, love, and sex.
replaces_summary: Armor Proficiency; Channel Energy; Domains

name: Scroll Scholar
description: Those who trade some of their potential to better understand ancient texts and scrolls can become learned scroll scholars.
replaces_summary: 1st Level Domain Power, Channel Energy 5th level increase, 4th-level spell slot

name: Separatist
description: A radical cleric, unsatisfied with the orthodoxy of her deity’s teachings, forges her own path of defiant divine expression.
replaces_summary: Domains

name: Stoic Caregiver
description: Stoic caregivers are champions of life in defiance of untimely death as well as undeath.
replaces_summary: Channel Energy; Domains;

name: Theologian
description: A theologian is an expert on one particular area of her religion
replaces_summary: Domains

name: Triadic Priest
description: Power comes not just from communion with one’s deity, but also from the formation of divine triumvirates. At the head of these groups are triadic priests.
replaces_summary: Domains

name: Undead Lord
description: An undead lord is a cleric focused on using necromancy to control undead.
replaces_summary: Domains

name: Varisian Pilgrim
description: While most clerics are associated with a particular temple, adventuring clerics spend much of their time away from their favored place of worship, and there are those who worship primarily on the road and during the journey.
replaces_summary: Altered Domain Abilities; Medium Armor/Shield Proficiency; 8th-level Domain Power

name: Aerie Protector
description: The aerie protector guards nature’s nests and lairs in the highest mountains, and she is only ever truly at ease when under vast expanses of open sky.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Nature Bond; Wild Empathy; Woodland Stride; Resist Nature’s Lure; Wild Shape; Venom Immunity

name: Ancient Guardian
description: Ancient guardians revere nature and draw power from its divine energies, but also protect the customs, histories, and lore of their tribe and race.
replaces_summary: Weapon Proficiencies; Nature Bond; Wild Empathy; Trackless Step; Woodland Stride; Spontaneous Casting; Resist Nature’s Lure; A Thousand Faces

name: Ape Shaman
description: A shaman with this totem calls upon the mighty ape, a peaceful but powerful simian whose strength is beyond compare.
replaces_summary: A Thousand Faces; Venom Immunity

name: Aquatic Druid
description: Shepherds of the lakes and seas, aquatic druids guard ecosystems ranging from shallows streams to deep ocean trenches, ministering to their residents and communing with the tides.
replaces_summary: Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces

name: Arctic Druid
description: An arctic druid watches over the stark landscape of the far frozen reaches of the world.
replaces_summary: Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces

name: Ashvawg Tamer
description: Ashvawg Tamers are druids who have chosen to bond with a strange and monstrous creature.
replaces_summary: Resist Nature's Lure, one less Wild Shape/day, Venom Immunity

name: Bat Shaman
description: The bat shaman’s totem is the agile bat, flitting and turning with incredible speed through even the most convoluted mazes.
replaces_summary: A Thousand Faces; Venom Immunity

name: Bear Shaman
description: A shaman with this focus calls upon the mighty bear, titan of the woodlands and mountains, a paragon of strength and ferocity, and yet also a quiet protector rich in wisdom.
replaces_summary: A Thousand Faces; Venom Immunity

name: Blight Druid
description: The devoted servants of nature corrupted, ruined, and destroyed, blight druids are the caretakers of lands ravaged by natural disaster.
replaces_summary: Wild Empathy; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces

name: Boar Shaman
description: A boar shaman chooses the stolid and ferocious boar as her totem.
replaces_summary: A Thousand Faces; Venom Immunity

name: Cave Druid
description: A few druids seek to preserve the hidden underdark and purge it of the fell horrors that creep up from below.
replaces_summary: Nature Sense; Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure

name: Death Druid
description: Some druids believe that birth and death are the most important parts of the natural cycle.
replaces_summary: Nature Bond; Wild Shape; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity

name: Defender of the True World
description: Some druids specialize in protecting Golarion from the threat of First World inhabitants.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Wild Empathy; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces

name: Desert Druid
description: Desert druids come to the desert to pay homage, protect and maintain the few habitable locales, and witness nature's majesty in all its burning, merciless glory.
replaces_summary: Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces

name: Devolutionist
description: Devolutionists believe that the world would be better off without any sentient life at all.
replaces_summary: Nature Bond; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity

name: Dinosaur Druid
description: A dinosaur druid speaks for the spirit of prehistoric nature, even taking the form of great beasts of legend.
replaces_summary: Spontaneous Casting; Nature’s Bond; Wild Shape; Resist Nature’s Lure

name: Draconic Druid
description: Some druids believe that dragons are the ultimate expression of nature and eventually transform into draconic forms.
replaces_summary: Nature Bond; Wild Empathy; Woodland Stride; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces; Timeless Body; Nature Sense; Resist Nature's Lure

name: Dragon Shaman
description: Your totem is the legendary dragon, fearsome and deadly yet cunning and wise, a creature born of pure magic and raw elemental fury, bound within a shell of fangs, claws, and scales that few dare to challenge.
replaces_summary: A Thousand Faces; 8th-level Wild Shape; Venom Immunity

name: Drovier
description: Droviers view their allies and adventuring companions as a herd or pack to be protected and directed.
replaces_summary: Nature's Bond; Wild Shape

name: Eagle Shaman
description: A shaman with this totem calls upon the noble eagle, stern and proud, soaring high above the world with keen and pitiless eyes that miss nothing.
replaces_summary: A Thousand Faces; Venom Immunity

name: Elemental Ally
description: An elemental ally is a druid who has crafted powerful bonds of trust and friendship with one ally from each of the four primary elemental planes.
replaces_summary: Nature’s Bond; Wild Shape; Wild Empathy; Resist Nature’s Lure

name: Feral Child
description: (Human Only) Some youths, abandoned in the wilderness and then raised by animals, are so connected with their adoptive home and family that they become feral.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Class Skills; Nature Bond; Trackless Step; A Thousand Faces; Wild Shape; Resist Nature’s Lure; Venom Immunity; Timeless Body

name: Feyspeaker
description: Some druids hear the whispers of the natural world like fey creatures do, and learn to mimic those tantalizing murmurs in order to influence the minds of both wild beasts and civilized people.
replaces_summary: Spontaneous Casting; Wild Shape; Nature Sense; Base Attack Bonus; Class Skills; Skill Ranks; Armor Proficiency

name: Fungal Pilgrim
description: Fungal pilgrims are druids seeking transformation into a more plantlike existence.
replaces_summary: Nature Bond; Wild Shape; Timeless Body

name: Goliath Druid
description: Goliath druids hone their spiritual and magical connections to nature’s largest creatures, especially dinosaurs, giants, and megafauna, revering these massive creatures as living relics of a primeval time when all creatures were more in harmony with nature.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Nature Sense; Nature Bond; Wild Empathy; Resist Nature’s Lure; Wild Shape; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces

name: Green Faith Initiate
description: Druids who are initiated into the magic of nature as strict students of the Green Faith’s traditions sometimes demonstrate abilities unique to their organization.
replaces_summary: Trackless Step; 6th, 10th-level use of Wild Shape; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces

name: Green Scourge
description: Green scourges are militant druids sworn to restore natural order.
replaces_summary: Spontaneous Casting; Nature Sense; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure

name: Halcyon Druid
description: Though they revere the natural world, halcyon druids are less focused on emulating its inhabitants. Instead, like Old-Mage Jatembe, they treat with beings from the Outer Sphere.
replaces_summary: Nature Bond; Nature Sense; Class Skills; Wild Empathy; Spontaneous Casting; Wild Shape; Resist Nature's Lure; A Thousand Faces

name: Jungle Druid
description: The jungle druid waches over the fecund jungles of the equatorial regions, rich in life and ancient tradition.
replaces_summary: Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; A Thousand Faces

name: Kraken Caller
description: Some druids draw upon power irreverently stolen from one of the most dangerous creatures of the depths: the kraken.
replaces_summary: Bonus Language; Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature’s Lure; Wild Shape; A Thousand Faces

name: Leshy Warden
description: The natural world is full of bodiless nature spirits connected to the forces of glades, springs, and individual plants. Some druids hear their call keenly and are able to effortlessly incarnate them as the miniature creatures known as leshys.
replaces_summary: Nature Bond; Wild Empathy; Spontaneous Casting; Resist Nature’s Lure; Wild Shape; A Thousand Faces

name: Life Channeler
description: By sacrificing the life of a sentient creature, life channelers can generate energy that guarantees fertility and prosperity for lower creatures like plants and animals.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Nature Bond; Wild Shape; Venom Immunity

name: Lion Shaman
description: A shaman with this totem calls upon the proud lion, imposing and majestic, the mighty leader of deadly hunters.
replaces_summary: A Thousand Faces; Venom Immunity

name: Mantella
description: (Grippli) A mantella focuses on exploring her poisonous heritage, whether or not it has manifested in her own personal biology.
replaces_summary: Wild Shape; Venom Immunity; Spontaneous Casting

name: Menhir Savant
description: Some druids study the paths of nature’s power through the nodes and ley lines that connect standing stones and megalithic circles, learning to tap into their energies.
replaces_summary: Nature Sense; Wild Empathy; Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; A Thousand Faces

name: Mooncaller
description: A mooncaller is bound to the subtle influences of the ever-changing moon and its endless cycles from light to dark and back again.
replaces_summary: Woodland Stride; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces

name: Mountain Druid
description: As more and more of the soft, easy lands become cultivated and civilized, many druids look for refuge and solitude among the eternal peaks of the highest mountains.
replaces_summary: Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces

name: Naga Aspirant
description: (Nagaji Only) The naga aspirant follows the ancient beliefs and engages in the rituals of a druidic sect dedicated to the transcendence of her nagaji form through absolute devotion to nagas and naga gods.
replaces_summary: Spontaneous Casting; Resist Nature’s Lure; Wild Shape; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces; Timeless Body

name: Nature Priest
description: Nature priests serve two masters: their deities and nature itself, and can call upon the power of both to fuel their divine abilities.
replaces_summary: Deity; Class Skills; Weapon Proficiencies; Nature Sense; Nature Bond; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity

name: Nithveil Adept
description: Nithveil adepts learn the secret magic of the First World fey in the moving, reality-phasing city of Nithveil
replaces_summary: Nature Sense; Nature Bond; Spontaneous Casting; Resist Nature's Lure

name: Pack Lord
description: Some druids bond with many animal companions rather than just one, achieving a level of communion rare even in druidic circles and leading their pack brothers and pack sisters with total authority.
replaces_summary: Nature Bond; 6th-level Wild Shape

name: Plains Druid
description: Out upon the wide and rolling prairies and savannahs, plains druids stand guard over the grasslands.
replaces_summary: Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces

name: Planar Extremist
description: (Ex-druid archetype) Planar extremists are those druids that have lost their neutral stance and find themselves gravitating toward one of the four most extreme alignments
replaces_summary: Alignment; Spells; Nature's Bond; Spontaneous Casting; Resist Nature's Lure; Wild Shape

name: Progenitor
description: Progenitors are druids who tap into the vibrant power of the First World to propagate natural and supernatural wonders.
replaces_summary: Spontaneous Casting; Nature Bond; Wild Shape

name: Reincarnated Druid
description: Spun off into the endless circle of life, an incarnate druid is an embodiment of nature’s eternal renewal.
replaces_summary: Woodland Stride; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity; Timeless Body

name: Restorer
description: Restorers carefully tend to the health of both the environment and creatures in it.
replaces_summary: Nature Sense; Spontanteous Casting; Resist Nature's Lore; A Thousand Faces

name: River Druid
description: These druids are the guardians of rivers and the creatures that depend on them.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Nature Sense; Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature’s Lure; Wild Shape; Timeless Body

name: Road Keeper
description: Guardians of the lonely paths between cities, road keepers protect the wild from travelers, and travelers from the wild.
replaces_summary: Spontaneous Casting; Nature Bond; Wild Empathy; Woodland Stride; Resist Nature’s Lure; Wild Shape

name: Rot Warden
description: Harbingers of decay, rot wardens live in damp lands where insects and rot break down the dead to feed the living.
replaces_summary: Spontaneous Casting; Nature Bond; Wild Empathy; Trackless Step; Venom Immunity; Resist Nature’s Lure; Wild Shape

name: Saurian Shaman
description: A shaman with this focus calls upon the primeval dinosaur, the archaic terror that lingers as a hungering, atavistic stranger at the fringes of the ecosystem, a destroyer and despoiler whose coming other animals dread.
replaces_summary: Venom Immunity

name: Season Keeper
description: (Triaxian Only) Imbued with the primeval potency of their planet’s seasons, those druids known as season keepers guide Triaxian communities through the stark transition from summer to winter (and vice versa).
replaces_summary: Nature Bond; Trackless Step; Venom Immunity; Timeless Body; Wild Shape

name: Season Sage
description: (Gathlain) Season sages wander as the seasons change, turning the leaves and transforming nature as they pass and taking pleasure in the transition between seasons.
replaces_summary: Wild Shape

name: Serpent Shaman
description: A shaman with this totem calls upon the cunning serpent, the stealthy deceiver who draws the weak minded in and strikes while they are unaware.
replaces_summary: A Thousand Faces; Venom Immunity

name: Shark Shaman
description: Some druids emulate the deadly shark, a remorseless hunter that marine dwellers dread.
replaces_summary: A Thousand Faces; Venom Immunity

name: Skinshaper
description: Using the same primal energy with which other druids assume animal forms, skinshapers can imitate some of the most dangerous beasts of all: humans and other humanoids.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Wild Shape; A Thousand Faces

name: Sky Druid
description: (Sylph Only) Some druids develop ties not to a particular landscape, but instead to the endless blue expanse of the skies.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Nature Bond; Woodland Stride; Resist Nature’s Lure; Trackless Step; Wild Shape; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces

name: Storm Druid
description: While most druids focus their attention upon the rich earth and the bounty of nature that springs forth from it, the storm druid’s eyes have ever been cast to the skies and the endless expanse of blue, channeling the most raw and untamed aspects of nature.
replaces_summary: Spontaneous Casting; Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity; A Thousand Faces

name: Sunrider
description: Sunriders help their allies, be they fellow tribe members or fellow adventurers, to survive in harsh desert terrain, while simultaneously making it even harsher for their foes.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Weapon and Armor Proficiencies; Nature Bond; Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Wild Shape

name: Supernaturalist
description: Supernaturalists wholly embrace paranormal phenomena as extensions of nature.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Nature Bond; Wild Empathy; Nature Sense; Trackless Step; Resist Nature’s Lure; Wild Shape

name: Survivor
description: The survivor embodies the neutral aspect of the natural world and eschews druidic magic in favor of more pragmatic and worldly survival methods.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Spellcasting; Nature Bond; Resist Nature’s Lure; Wild Shape; Venom Immunity

name: Swamp Druid
description: Some druids eschew pleasant glades and groves and instead seek out dank marshes, misty bogs and heaths, and trackless swamps as the place they call home and watch over with care, finding beauty and life in abundance in places few others would willingly enter.
replaces_summary: Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; A Thousand Faces

name: Swarm Monger
description: Whereas other druids commune with nature or even the spirit of a city, swarm mongers find beauty and strength in decay, and they draw their power from fungus, disease, and their own singular will to survive.
replaces_summary: Nature Bond; Woodland Stride; Nature Sense; Resist Nature's Lure; 12th-level Wild Shape options

name: Tempest Druid
description: Some druids have come to worship the Eye of Abendego as an aspect of pure elemental fury, channeling its mysteries into their own magic
replaces_summary: Alignment; Weapon and Armor Proficiencies; Nature Bond; Nature Sense; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity

name: Tempest Tamer
description:
Tempest Tamer
Source Blood of the Sea pg. 25
Druids draw upon the fiercest powers of nature, and tsunamis, typhoons, and whirlpools are among the most powerful forces in the ocean.
replaces_summary: Bonus Languages; Resist Nature's Lure; Wild Shape

name: Toxicologist
description: Toxicologists specialize in the stealthy application of insidious poisons to their targets, believing word of their foes’ horrifying deaths will deter others from attempting similar desecrations of the natural world.
replaces_summary: Spontaneous Casting; Wild Empathy; Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Wild Shape

name: Treesinger
description: (Elf and Vine Leshy Only) Some elves turn to the timeless growth of nature for solace, finding allies among the great trees themselves, and even leading the forest’s plants into combat.
replaces_summary: Nature Bond; Wild Empathy; Wild Shape

name: Troll Fury
description: (Troll Only) Troll furies combine a druid’s love of natural balance with a single-minded devotion to the welfare of the troll tribe.
replaces_summary: Wild Empathy; Resist Nature’s Lure; Venom Immunity

name: Undine Adept
description: (Undine Only) An undine adept dedicates herself to preserving the knowledge of the first undines and ensuring her people’s ancient connections to the natural world remain undisturbed.
replaces_summary: Domains; Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature’s Lure; Wild Shape; Venom Immunity

name: Urban Druid
description: While many druids keep to the wilderness, some make their way within settlements, communing with the animals and vermin who live there and speaking for the nature that runs rampant in civilization's very cradle.
replaces_summary: Spontaneous Casting; Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Resist Nature's Lure; Venom Immunity

name: Urushiol
description: When an urushiol druid forms a bond with nature, it takes a vastly different form than for most druids. His body becomes increasingly toxic, allowing him to secrete a deadly poison through his pores.
replaces_summary: Nature Bond; Resist Nature's Lure

name: Wolf Shaman
description: A shaman with this totem calls upon the clever wolf, capable of roaming alone yet wise enough to run with a pack when facing dangers too great for one alone.
replaces_summary: A Thousand Faces; Venom Immunity

name: World Walker
description: While all druids traverse the wilderness with ease, the world walkers take it upon themselves to travel the entire world.
replaces_summary: Trackless Step; Resist Nature’s Lure; Venom Immunity; Timeless Body

name: Aerial Assaulter
description: Aerial assaulters leap to great heights and create higher ground where there is none.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Bravery; 2nd, 12th-level Bonus Feat, Armor Mastery, Weapon Mastery

name: Airborne Ambusher
description: (Strix Only) Driven by suspicion and hatred, strix doggedly guard their territories, making deadly use of their flight.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Class Skills; Bravery; Weapon Training 1-4

name: Aldori Defender
description: Trained warrior in the art of the Aldori dueling sword.
replaces_summary: Armor Training; 6th, 8th, 10th-level Bonus Feat

name: Aquanaut
description: Aquanaut fighters master the movement of water, using its flow to their advantage in combat.
replaces_summary: Armor Proficiency; Bravery; Armor Training 1-4; Armor Mastery; Weapon Training 1-4

name: Archer
description: The archer is dedicated to the careful mastery of the bow, perfecting his skills with years of practice.
replaces_summary: Bravery; Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 1-4; Armor Mastery

name: Armiger
description: Fighters who focus their training to become Hellknights.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Skill Points; 1st, 10th-level Bonus Feats; Bravery

name: Armor Master
description: While many fighters hone their weapon skills to a point of inescapable grace and lethality, there are those who live under the maxim that a good offense can be accomplished though an impenetrable defense.
replaces_summary: Bravery; Weapon Training 1-4; Armor Mastery; Weapon Mastery

name: Brawler
description: All melee is up close and personal, but some warriors bring it as close as they can get.
replaces_summary: Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 1-4; Armor Mastery, Weapon Mastery

name: Cad
description: In combat, most fighters have some sort of code of honor. Some believe that one should not kick enemies when they are down, or should limit use of other such dirty tricks to the most dire of circumstances. The cad places no such limitations on himself.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Bravery; Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 1-4; Weapon Mastery

name: Calistrian Hunter
description: Like a bounty hunter following his own rules, a Calistrian hunter tracks down elusive prey using his wits and delivers long-overdue retribution while easily ignoring his own injuries thanks to the joy he receives from a job well done.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Class Skills; Bravery; Weapon Training 1-4; Weapon Mastery

name: Cavern Sniper
description: (Drow Only) Perfectly at home in the darkness, the cavern sniper capitalizes on stealth and ranged attacks imbued with his spell-like abilities to harass his opponents.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; 1st, 4th-level Fighter Bonus Feats; Bravery; Weapon Training 1-4; Weapon Mastery

name: Child of Acavna and Amaznen
description: The child of Acavna and Amaznen has trained in the nearly forgotten arts of Azlant passed down by Aroden, which combine martial prowess with elementary wizardry. Inspired by the deities Aroden once worshiped long ago, children of Acavna and Amaznen strive to understand the dangers of the world and overcome them with knowledge and strength of arms.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Skills; 1st, 2nd, 8th, 12th, 16th, and 20th-level Combat Feats; Armor Training; Weapon Training

name: Corsair
description: A corsair is a pirate who focuses on shipboard combat, relying on his strength of arms over his agility.
replaces_summary: 2nd-level Bonus Feat; Armor Training; 6th-level Bonus Feat

name: Crossbowman
description: The crossbowman has perfected the deadly use of the crossbow, a simple but cruelly efficient weapon, as a craftsman mastering a lethal tool.
replaces_summary: Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 1-4; Armor Mastery

name: Cyber-Soldier
description: Cyber-soldiers replace fallible flesh with precisioncrafted machinery.
replaces_summary: Weapon Training 1; Armor Training 2 and 4; Armor Mastery

name: Dirty Fighter
description: (Orc Only) The dirty fighter laughs at concepts like honor and fair play. He cares only for victory, no matter how he achieves it, and spends as much time mastering sneaky combat maneuvers as he does drilling with weapons or learning how to wear armor.
replaces_summary: Bravery; Weapon Training 1-4

name: Dragonheir Scion
description: Dragonheir scions are the martially inclined humanoid descendants of those influenced by draconic power.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; 1st, 3rd, 5th-level Bonus Feats; Bravery; Armor Training; Armor Mastery; Weapon Mastery

name: Dragoon
description: These gallant lancers serve in the vanguard of many armies or as knights-errant.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiency; 1st-level Bonus Feat; Weapon Training 1-4; Armor Training 2-4; Weapon Mastery

name: Drill Sergeant
description: Drill sergeants excel at training other combatants in fighting techniques.
replaces_summary: Bravery; Weapon Training 1-4

name: Druman Blackjacket
description: Known as the Blackjackets, the elite soldiers of Druma are and always have been mercenaries rather than a standing army.
replaces_summary: Bravery; 4th, 8th, 12th, 16th-level Bonus Feats;

name: Eldritch Guardian
description: Eldritch guardians are trained to detect and give warning about magic threats to the people and places they protect.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; 1st and 2nd-level Bonus Feats; Bravery

name: Foehammer
description: (Dwarf Only) While the axe is the most famous dwarven weapon, the hammer is at the heart of dwarves’ heritage as forgemasters and warriors alike.
replaces_summary: Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 2-4; Armor Mastery; Weapon Mastery

name: Free Hand Fighter
description: The free hand fighter specializes in the delicate art of handling a single weapon in one hand while using his free hand to balance, block, tip, and distract his opponents.
replaces_summary: Bravery; Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 1-4; Armor Mastery

name: Gladiator
description: The gladiator is both a cunning warrior and a consummate performer, knowing life and death are balanced not only on a sword’s edge, but also on the cheers or jeers of the crowd.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Bravery

name: Gloomblade
description: The Shadow Plane’s substance is legendary for its versatility. In shadow-shrouded lands, secretive martial practitioners long ago learned to shape supernatural weapons from ribbons of pure darkness.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Armor Proficiencies; Armor Training; Weapon Training

name: High Guardian
description: High guardians epitomize personal devotion, serving as the shield that protects his lord from the myriad dangers around every corner.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; 1st, 2nd, 4th-level Bonus Feats; Bravery

name: Lore Warden
description: Lore wardens are fighters who benefit from learning to outsmart and outmaneuver their foes rather than just overpower them.
replaces_summary: Armor Proficiencies; Bravery; 2nd-level Bonus Feat; Armor Training; Armor Mastery

name: Lore Warden (PFS Field Guide)
description: Lore wardens are fighters who benefit from learning to outsmart and outmaneuver their foes rather than just overpower them.
replaces_summary: Medium Armor, Heavy Armor, and Shield Proficiency; Bravery 1; Armor Training 1-4; Armor Mastery

name: Mobile Fighter
description: Where some fighters focus on strength and raw power, the mobile fighter relies on swiftness and mobility, gliding across the battlefield like a steel whirlwind and leaving destruction in his wake.
replaces_summary: Bravery; Weapon Training 1-4; Armor Training 3-4; Weapon Mastery

name: Molthuni Defender
description: Taking advantage of their weighty armor, Molthuni defenders can hold back an onslaught of enemies intent on breaking through their line.
replaces_summary: Armor Training

name: Opportunist
description: Opportunists believe every battle is one of wits rather than arms.
replaces_summary: Class Skils; Bravery; 1st, 4th, 8th, 12th, 16th, 20th-level Bonus Feat; Weapon Training 1

name: Pack Mule
description: Pack mules carry heavy loads with ease and small packages with discretion.
replaces_summary: Skill Ranks; 1st-level Bonus Feat; Bravery; Armor Training 1-4; Armor Mastery

name: Phalanx Soldier
description: The phalanx soldier specializes in defensive tactics, using his shield to guard himself and his allies and forming a shield wall like an unbreakable anvil against which his enemies break.
replaces_summary: Bravery; Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 1-4; Weapon Mastery

name: Polearm Master
description: The polearm master is schooled in the ancient wisdom that enemies are best faced at the end of long striking pole.
replaces_summary: Bravery; Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 1-4; Armor Mastery

name: Qadira: Dawnflower Dervish
description: Spinning warrior dervish of Sarenrae, wielding a scimitar with devastating consequences.
replaces_summary: Armor Training 1-4

name: Relic Master
description: Commonly trained in the well-funded temples of Osirion or Qadira, the relic master is skilled in magic item mastery.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Armor Training; Weapon Training; Armor Mastery

name: Roughrider
description: Roughriders study and practice the fine points of mounted combat, drilling endlessly with warbeasts—from noble thoroughbreds to trained monsters—to form a perfect synergy between rider and steed.
replaces_summary: Bravery; Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 1-4; Armor Mastery

name: Savage Warrior
description: Warriors' might is not measured only by their skill with steel, but also by their ability to inflict death with fang and claw, horn and hoof, and every exotic appendage the natural and unnatural world has to offer.
replaces_summary: Bravery; Weapon Training 1-4; Weapon Mastery

name: Scrapper
description: Scrappers are adept at collecting the leftovers from their enemy’s armor and using these scraps to improve their own armor.
replaces_summary: Armor Training; Weapon Training 1

name: Seasoned Commander
description: The seasoned commander excels at leading troops through inspiration and the use of unit tactics.
replaces_summary: Armor Proficiencies; Skill Ranks per Level; Class Skills; 1st-level Bonus Feat; Armor Training 1, 3, 4; Weapon Training

name: Sensate
description: A sensate perceives battle through senses beyond mortal ken, anticipating his opponents’ movements before they even begin to act.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Bravery; Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 2-4; Armor Mastery; Weapon Mastery

name: Shielded Fighter
description: A shielded fighter focuses on both offense and defense, blending weapon and shield in perfect balance to impede his enemies while delivering deadly blows, and even turning the shield itself into a formidable weapon.
replaces_summary: Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 1-4; Armor Mastery; Weapon Mastery

name: Siegebreaker
description: The siegebreaker is trained to break through lines of enemy soldiers.
replaces_summary: 1st, 2nd, 4th, and 8th-level Bonus Feats; Bravery; Weapon Mastery

name: Skirmisher
description: Not all battles are fought between armies; sometimes, a settlement or nation faces a threat too dire to fight in the open. In order to repel a much larger enemy force, some soldiers must abandon equipment and tactics designed for open-field warfare and instead rely on ambushes, hit-andrun tactics, and sabotage to whittle down their enemies’ strength and morale.
replaces_summary: Armor Proficiencies; Class Skills; Skill Ranks; 2nd-level Bonus Feat; Bravery; Armor Training; Armor Mastery

name: Spear Fighter
description: One of the oldest spear-fighting styles originates in Vudra, where many martial arts traditions consider the spear to be the ultimate weapon.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Armor Proficiencies; Bravery; Armor Training; Armor Mastery; Weapon Training; Weapon Mastery

name: Steelbound Fighter
description: A fighter who has impressive martial resolve and technique with a specific weapon as the result of a powerful relationship a similar weapon had with one of his ancestors.
replaces_summary: 1st level Bonus Feat; Weapon Training 1-4

name: Swarm Fighter
description: (Kobold Only) Scuttling between the legs of friend and foe alike, the swarm fighter is an unshakable combatant.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiency; 1st, 2nd, 6th, 10th, 14th, 18th-level Bonus Feats; Bravery; Weapon Training 1-4; Weapon Mastery

name: Tactician
description: While many fighters focus on the fundamentals of melee and ranged combat, there are those who are trained to view the bigger picture on the battlefield.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiency; 1st-level Bonus Feat; Bravery; Weapon Training 1; Armor Training 3, 4

name: Taldor: Rondelero Duelist
description: One who has perfected the art of rondelero, fighting with falcata and buckler.
replaces_summary: Armor Training 1-3, Bravery, Weapon Training 1

name: Thunderstriker
description: The thunderstriker adopts an unusual fighting style, gripping a heavy weapon with both hands and switching to a defensive posture with weapon and buckler, lashing out with the shield with surprising speed and power.
replaces_summary: Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 3-4; Armor Mastery

name: Titan Fighter
description: Titan fighters make use of enormous weapons others can barely lift.
replaces_summary: 1st-level Bonus Feat; Armor Training; Weapon Training

name: Tower Shield Specialist
description: Many fighters believe the tower shield is a tool suitable only for troops on the battlefield, claiming it is too large and bulky to use in skirmishes or within dungeon corridors.
replaces_summary: Bravery; Weapon Training 1-4; Weapon Mastery

name: Trench Fighter
description: Advances in technology have made archaic armors obsolete by the twentieth century, and modern soldiers concentrate training on firearms and swift feet.
replaces_summary: Armor Training 1-4

name: Tribal Fighter
description: A tribal fighter knows that it is not the weapon that matters but the hand that wields it. Instead of encasing himself in metal armor like the soldiers fighting and dying for the socalled civilized lands do, he prefers to wear something he or his ancestors have killed.
replaces_summary: 1st-level Bonus Feat; Weapon Training

name: Two-Handed Fighter
description: Some fighters focus their efforts on finding the biggest, heaviest, most imposing weapon they can find and training to manage and harness the weight of their massive weapons for maximum impact.
replaces_summary: Bravery; Armor Training 1-4; Armor Mastery

name: Two-Weapon Warrior
description: Trained under great masters who preached the simple truth that two are better than one when it comes to weapons, the two-weapon warrior is a terror when his hands are full.
replaces_summary: Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 1-4; Armor Mastery

name: Unarmed Fighter
description: The unarmed fighter picks up a weapon only rarely, and when he does, he prefers the weapons of the monk.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiency; 1st-level Bonus Feat; Bravery; Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 1-4; 8th, 12th-level Bonus Feat; Armor Mastery; Weapon Mastery

name: Unbreakable
description: The unbreakable is a warrior of indomitable will, unstoppable and implacable once he has set his mind upon a course of action.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiency; 1st-level Bonus Feat; Bravery; Weapon Training 1-4; Armor Training 3-4; Weapon Mastery

name: Ustalavic Duelist
description: The University of Lepidstadt has developed a reputation for churning out fearsome duelists. At the end of each academic year, the students all gather together and duel one another with light blades. Each student duels one opponent after another until he is marked on the cheek by an adversary’s blade. These “Lepidstadt scars” are recognized throughout the Inner Sea region and beyond as marks of their prowess. The Lepidstadt Style is one of fluid motion and precise thrusts.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiency; 1st-level Bonus Feat; Weapon Training 1-4

name: Varisian Free-Style Fighter
description: Free-style fighters are most common in Varisia, where their training—students’ traveling study with multiple teachers, or perhaps instruction in the anything-goes mentality of the Price of Freedom school in Kaer Maga—allows them to blend fighting styles into a greater whole.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; 1st, 6th, 10th, and 12th-level Bonus Feats; Weapon Training; Weapon Mastery; Armor Training

name: Venomblade
description: (Nagaji) The venomblade has learned to take full advantage of his toxic spittle, blinding his targets and then rushing in to cut them down where they stand.
replaces_summary: 1st, 6th, 12th-level Bonus Feat; Bravery

name: Viking
description: Vikings seek to raid “softer societies” and return with their longships filled with plunder. A viking strikes fear into the heart of her foes, and in battle she can fly into a terrible rage.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Bravery; Armor Training; Weapon Training; Bonus Feats

name: Warlord
description: Honorable, fearless experts in gun and blade, warlords eschew armor as impractical to their harsh and often desert-like environment
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Weapon and Armor Proficiency; Armor Training; Armor Mastery

name: Weapon Bearer Squire
description: The weapon bearer squire ensures her knight is equipped for any task.
replaces_summary: 1st-level Bonus Feat; 2nd-level Bonus Feat; Armor Training 1

name: Weapon Master
description: Devoted to the perfection of a single weapon, the weapon master's meditations upon his favored weapon border on the obsessive, but none can deny his consummate skill.
replaces_summary: Bravery; Armor Training 1-4; Weapon Training 1-4; Armor Mastery

name: Black Powder Vaulter
description: Black powder vaulters like to get into the thick of the battle, maneuvering among the fray to find the most advantageous positions from which to kill their enemies.
replaces_summary: Deadeye; Gunslinger's Dodge; Gunslinger Initiative; Startling Shot; Expert Loading

name: Blatherskite
description: The blatherskate has an extraordinary talent for retreating from harm’s way and fooling his enemies into thinking he is weaker than he is.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Gunslinger's Dodge; Gunslinger Initiative; Pistol-Whip; Startling Shot

name: Buccaneer
description: (Human Only) Freebooters who cling to the convoluted codes that rule independent ships, the buccaneer is a gunslinger of the high seas.
replaces_summary: Quick Clear Deed; Pistol Whip Deed; Dead Shot Deed; Lightning Reload Deed; Grit; Nimble; Gun Training 1,2, and 4

name: Bushwhacker
description: (Kobold Only) The bushwhacker specializes in the art of the ambush. For her, gunplay works best when it comes from a concealed position and is directed against a target that falls with the very first volley and is dead before the smoke clears.
replaces_summary: Grit; Utility Shot Deed; Targeting Deed; 4th, 8th, 12th, 16th, 20th-level Bonus Feats

name: Commando
description: Commandos are those rough and ready gunslingers who excel at hit-and-run tactics and leveraging their expertise in the wild against their enemies.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Utility Shot; Expert Loading; Lightning Reload; Slinger's Luck; 4th-level Bonus Feat; Gun Training

name: Experimental Gunsmith
description: (Gnome Only) The experimental gunsmith is obsessed with creating the ultimate firearm, and is willing to take risks to work the kinks out of her design.
replaces_summary: Gunsmith; Gunsmithing; Gun Training

name: Firebrand
description: Where other gunslingers rely on precision shooting, the firebrand instead masters explosive ordnance.
replaces_summary: Gunsmith; Grit; Deadeye; Dead Shot; Bonus Feats; Gun Training

name: Graveslinger
description: Graveslingers craft signature bullets to hunt down wandering undead. With this infused ammunition, they can take on the most resilient spectral foes with ease.
replaces_summary: Deadeye; Startling Shot; Bleeding Wound; Menacing Shot; 4th-level Bonus Feat

name: Gulch Gunner
description: (Ratfolk Only) These gulch gunners often specialize in proficiency with a single pistol (easily handled in tight spaces) and wander from warren to warren selling their tunnel-shooting skills to the highest bidder.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Grit; Deadeye Deed; Pistol Whip Deed; Menacing Shot Deed; 9th, 13th, 17th-level Gun Training

name: Gun Scavenger
description: A gun scavenger collects scraps, spare parts, and other components that might—if modified properly—give her firearm an extra edge.
replaces_summary: Gunsmith; Quick Clear Deed; Targeting Deed; Nimble

name: Gun Tank
description: Gunslingers usually don’t use heavy armor, but there are a few who use and modify their armor to protect themselves from both traditional weapons and gunfire.
replaces_summary: Armor Proficiency; Gunslinger’s Dodge Deed; Evasive Deed; Nimble; Bonus Feats (4th level and every 4 levels after)

name: Gunner Squire
description: The gunner squire is prepared to reload or repair her lord's firearms at a moment's notice.
replaces_summary: Gunslinger's Dodge; Gunslinger Initiative; Nimble +1

name: Maverick
description: Quick with her gun and no stranger to barroom brawls, a maverick can hold her own when bullets and punches start flying.
replaces_summary: Dodge; Pistol Whip; Gunslinger Initiative

name: Musket Master
description: Some gunslingers want a little more firepower and range. These are the musket masters; the best of these gunslingers can reload a musket with dazzling speed, potentially putting even their pistol-wielding brethren to shame.
replaces_summary: Weapon Proficiency; Gunsmith; Gunslinger’s Dodge Deed; Utility Shot Deed; Gun Training 1-4

name: Mysterious Stranger
description: A few rare gunslingers seem to accomplish their chosen tasks purely through willpower and an unwillingness to ever give up.
replaces_summary: Grit; Quick Clear Deed; Nimble; Gun Training 1

name: Pistolero
description: While most gunslingers have favorite firearms, there are those rare ones who choose to specialize in one-handed firearms exclusively.
replaces_summary: Weapon Proficiency; Gunsmith; Deadeye Deed; Startling Shot Deed; Menacing Shot Deed; Gun Training 1-4

name: Planar Rifter
description: Planar rifters harvest pieces of the Outer Planes, absorbing some of the energy into themselves and condensing other portions of it into bullets they can fire from their guns
replaces_summary: Grit; Deadeye; Dead Shot; Menacing Shot; Death's Shot; Nimble; Gun Training

name: Scatter Gunner
description: Punishing firearms such as the blunderbuss and dragon pistol can lay waste to invaders in tight corridors, and the dwarves of Dongun Hold have pioneered masterful methods for making the most of these indiscriminate weapons
replaces_summary: Gunsmith; Deadeye; Utility Shot; Dead Shot; Targeting

name: Siege Gunner
description: The Grand Duchy of Alkenstar has spawned all manner of firearm specialists, wielding small and large firearms alike. Siege gunners specialize in the very largest— cannons, bombards, and firedrakes.
replaces_summary: Grit; Nimble; Deadeye; Gunslinger Initiative; Bonus Feats

name: Techslinger
description: Techslingers spurn unreliable gunpowder weapons in favor of high-tech armaments.
replaces_summary: Deadeye; Quick Clear; Expert Loading; Gun Training

name: Thronewarden
description: As vigilant sentinels, thronewardens identify and head off trouble.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Nimble; 4th-level Bonus Feat; Startling Shot; Bleeding Wound

name: Wyrm Sniper
description: These long-shot artists are masters at taking down dragons with sharp shooting and light siege weaponry.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Starling Shot Deed; Lightning Reload Deed; Menacing Shot Deed

name: Aquatic Beastmaster
description: Hunters beneath the sea are just as committed as their surface-dwelling counterparts to working alongside their animal companions to eradicate threats.
replaces_summary: Animal Focus; Wild Empathy; Improved Empathic Link

name: Chameleon Adept
description: Some hunters are masters of blending into every situation, even making their companions appear humanoid for brief periods of time.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Teamwork Feats; Hunter’s Tactics; Wild Empathy; Precise Companion; Woodland Stride; Raise Animal Companion; One with the Wild

name: Colluding Scoundrel
description: The colluding scoundrel is a canny and conniving skirmisher, manipulating her enemies and leveraging her allies for her own benefit.
replaces_summary: Animal Focus; Second Animal Focus; Master Hunter

name: Courtly Hunter
description: While most hunters find themselves at ease in the wildest parts of the world, courtly hunters instead make their homes in cultured urban environments.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Animal Companion; Animal Focus; Precise Companion; Hunter Tactics; Teamwork Feats; Bonus Tricks

name: Feykiller
description: Some hunters in fey-plagued regions are dedicated to tracking down and eradicating these threats.
replaces_summary: Animal Focus; Wild Empathy; Speak with Master; Improed Empathic Link; One with the Wild

name: Flood Flourisher
description: Well adapted to their drenched surroundings, flood flourishers coordinate deadly ambushes with their loyal animal companions by capitalizing on their mastery of the soggy terrain.
replaces_summary: Hunter Tactics; Woodland Stride; Teamwork Feats; Swift Tracker

name: Forester
description: While all hunters have a bond with the natural world, a forester has a stronger tie to her environment than to the animals within it.
replaces_summary: Animal Focus; Animal Companion; Precise Companion; Hunter Tactics; Improved Empathic Link; Bonus Tricks; Raise Animal Companion; Speak with Master; Greater Empathic Link

name: Patient Ambusher
description: Wandering the wilds, some hunters eschew the bond of innate teamwork with their animal companions. Instead, these hunters master the creation and deployment of intricate ranger traps, often using themselves or their animal companions to lure foes into their snares.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Nature Training; Wild Empathy; Hunter Tactics; Teamwork Feats; Raise Animal Companion

name: Pelagic Hunter
description: Pelagic hunters form strong bonds that extend deep beneath the sea.
replaces_summary: Animal Companion; Animal Focus; Woodland Stride

name: Plant Master
description: Some hunters form a bond with plant life instead of an animal and take on those aspects instead.
replaces_summary: Animal Companion; Animal Focus; Wild Empathy; One with the Wild; Master Hunter

name: Roof Runner
description: Roof runners are skilled ambushers, skirmishers, and spies who eschew heavier types of armor in order to remain light on their feet.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Armor Proficieny; Track; Woodland Stride; Swift Tracker; Master Hunter

name: Scarab Stalker
description: The forgotten ruins in the desert sands have given rise to a breed of hunters who call upon the powers of the pyramids to protect and explore the ancient sites of their people.
replaces_summary: Animal Focus; Woodland Stride

name: Totem-Bonded
description: Totem-bonded are hunters who are able to form a true spiritual bond with the powerful fauna that dominate the wildlands.
replaces_summary: Animal Companion; Animal Focus

name: Treestrider
description: Some hunters are masters of the wild. Whether raised by apes or having some other link with simian creatures, these hunters glide through the wilderness with ease.
replaces_summary: Animal Companion; Animal Focus; Second Animal Focus; Precise Companion; Raise Animal Companion

name: Uprooter Scout
description: The Uprooters are an elite band of Kyonin elves skilled and brave enough to foray into the Tanglebriar and take the offensive in the fight against Treerazer.
replaces_summary: Improved Empathic Link; Speak with Master; Greater Empathic Link; Raise Animal Companion; One with the Wild

name: Urban Hunter
description: Urban hunters help guards track down and apprehend criminals or spies, find lost children and disaster victims, and protect animals from abuse in the city.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Animal Companion; Hunter Tactics; Teamwork Feat; Woodland Stride; One with the Wild

name: Abolisher
description: Abolishers are incorruptible inquisitors who weed out creatures of alien, unnatural origins, finding and exposing aberrations for what they are.
replaces_summary: Domains; Stern Gaze; Detect Alignment; Discern Lies

name: Cloaked Wolf
description: Some inquisitors are adept at appearing harmless until they throw off their unassuming facades and take their enemies by surprise.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Stern Gaze; Cunning Initiative; Monster Lore; Solo Tactics; Teamwork Feats

name: Cold Iron Warden
description: The natural enemies of demoniacs and other cultists of fiendish forces, cold iron wardens are inquisitors who dedicate their lives to eradicating the taint of demonkind from Golarion.
replaces_summary: 3rd, 6th, 9th, 12th, 15th, 18th-level Teamwork Feats; Bane; Discern Lies

name: Exarch
description: (Dwarf Only) The gruff traditionalism of most dwarves finds its apex in those who adhere to a strict orthodoxy rooted in ancient principles and practices and who are not amenable whatsoever to change or innovation.
replaces_summary: Monster Lore; Detect Alignment; Bane; Second Judgment; Greater Bane; Third Judgment

name: Exorcist
description: Some inquisitors, as they learn more about the threat of possession and the machinations of the planes, task themselves to expel possessing spirits and conniving outsiders from the world whenever possible.
replaces_summary: Second Judgment; Third Judgment; Slayer; True Judgment

name: Expulsionist
description: Expulsionists are inquisitors who seek to rid the material world of the corrupting influences of wicked spirits that twist mortals to their own ends.
replaces_summary: Domain; Monster Lore; Discern Lies

name: Faith Hunter
description: Certain relentless inquisitors burn with special hatred for an enemy of specific convictions, and they stop at nothing when obsessively hunting these foes down.
replaces_summary: Domain; Justice, Destruction Judgement; Detect Alignment

name: Green Faith Marshal
description: The Green Faith marshal serves as a literal force of nature, hunting down and punishing those who have committed sins against nature through enslavement and mistreatment of animals or destruction of wild places.
replaces_summary: Domain; Stern Gaze; Monster Lore; Discern Lies; Stalwart

name: Heretic
description: While all inquisitors hunt the enemies of the faith, sometimes, either through political maneuvering by her enemies or an unyielding tenacity that breaks her faith’s basic tenets, an inquisitor can find herself a heretic.
replaces_summary: Monster Lore

name: Hexenhammer
description: Hexenhammers learn to use the hexes and some spells of witches, but must pay the price for dealing with supernatural powers, as they slip ever closer to the evil they seek to destroy.
replaces_summary: Monster Lore; Stern Gaze; Domain; Cunning Initiative; Teamwork Feats; Solo Tactics; Spellcasting

name: Iconoclast
description: Some magic items are heretical by nature, enabling the unfaithful to spread wickedness. Iconoclasts seek out and remove such crutches, cleansing the taint these items exude.
replaces_summary: Monster Lore; Detect Alignment; Discern Lies; Exploit Weakness; True Judgment

name: Immolator
description: (Ifrit Only) The immolator puts her pyromaniacal urges to work in the service of a deity. She brings burning retribution down upon the enemies of her faith, consigning their souls to the sacrificial flames.
replaces_summary: Smiting Judgment; Bane; Greater Bane; True Judgment

name: Infiltrator
description: This inquisitor uses guile and deception to blend in among the enemies of the faith rather than confronting them head-on.
replaces_summary: Stern Gaze; Monster Lore; Track; Discern Lies

name: Keeper of Construct
description: Some of Brigh’s inquisitors, and occasionally those who follow Torag, track down malicious construct crafters and fight renegade constructs with unrivaled expertise.
replaces_summary: Domain; Stern Gaze; Monster Lore; Exploid Weakness

name: Keeper of the Current
description: Water-related deities all have organized churches beneath the waves that engage zealous followers to hunt down apostates and enemies. Regardless of their faith, these undersea zealots are known as keepers of the current.
