replaces_summary: Track; Spellcasting; Discern Lies

name: Kinslayer
description: (Dhampir Only) Appalled and guilt-ridden by the horrific circumstances of her birth, a kinslayer dedicates herself to eradicating the very creatures whose blood flows within her veins.
replaces_summary: Destruction Judgment; Teamwork Feats; Detect Alignment

name: Living Grimoire
description: The living grimoire literally wields the sacred word of his deity, using his holy tome to smite the foes of his god with divine might.
replaces_summary: Monster Lore; Spellcasting; Cunning Initiative; Orisons; Judgement; Bane; Greater Bane; Second Judgement; Third Judgement; True Judgement

name: Monster Tactician
description: While most inquisitors have learned to take advantage of the movements of their opponents and allies in combat, some instead summon creatures as a means of claiming strategic advantage, exploiting the mystic connection between themselves and their summoned creatures to impart a bit of their own tactical knowledge.
replaces_summary: Judgment; Discern Lies; Second Judgment; Third Judgment; Slayer; True Judgment

name: Oathkeeper
description: Oathkeepers formally oversee the creation of bargains and personally hunt down those who dare to violate them.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Class Skills; Monster Lore; Track

name: Preacher
description: Some inquisitors wander the land to spread the true word of their faith.
replaces_summary: Solo Tactics

name: Ravener Hunter
description: For generations, the catfolk of Murraseth have viewed the cults of Angazhan with loathing and hatred, and they believe it is their sacred duty to hunt down the followers of the Ravener King and expel them from the Material Plane
replaces_summary: Alignment; Domain; Spells; 3rd-level Teamwork Feat

name: Reaper of Secrets
description: Masters of mind tricks and deception, these divine assassins track down and kill anyone who has forbidden knowledge about their faiths.
replaces_summary: Monster Lore; Stern Gaze; Solo Tactics

name: Relic Hunter
description: Some inquisitors specialize in the use and recovery of long-lost relics of their faiths, drawing forth divine might from the recovered items in order to restore their sanctity and wield these artifacts against the enemies of their gods.
replaces_summary: Spellcasting; Judgment; Domain; Bane; Greater Bane; Second Judgment; Third Judgment

name: Royal Accuser
description: Royal accusers serve the prince of Ustalav, cutting through the nation’s baroque politics and quietly facing its many threats.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Stern Gaze; Detect Alignment; Solo Tactics; Teamwork Bonus Feats

name: Secret Seeker
description: Recognizing that torture is a poor method of securing truthful intelligence, secret seekers instead prefer magical means of inquiry.
replaces_summary: Monster Lore; Bane; Greater Bane; Exploit Weakness

name: Sin Eater
description: There is a sect of inquisitors in some religions that believes it is not enough to hunt the enemies of the church—one must also devour those enemies’ sins.
replaces_summary: Domain; 6th-level Teamwork Feat; Exploit Weakness

name: Spellbreaker
description: The world is full of dangerous magic, and many recoil in the face of such power. The spellbreaker, by contrast, learns to recognize and resist certain types of magic, wading through waves of magic to reach her foes.
replaces_summary: Monster Lore; Bonus Teamwork Feats; Solo Tactics; Final Judgment

name: Suit Seeker
description: Though surrounded by meaning and tradition revered by most harrowers, the harrow is a tool that could be abused. Those inquisitors known as suit seekers take grave offense at the misuse of this ancient power, and dedicate their considerable skills to hunting and eliminating any who would abuse the harrow’s magic and the heritage it represents.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Domain; Judgment; Second Judgment; Third Judgment; Detect Alignment; True Judgment

name: Sworn of the Eldest
description: Although the Eldest rarely have adherents as other deities do, they still occasionally require mortal agents to advance their aims.
replaces_summary: Deity; Domain; Monster Lore; Stern Gaze; Solo Tactics; Teamwork Feats

name: Tactical Leader
description: Rather than pursuing their holy missions alone, some inquisitors see the inherent value of working with like-minded allies to accomplish mutual goals.
replaces_summary: Stern Gaze; Solo Tactics; Teamwork Feats; Exploit Weakness

name: Traceless Operative
description: Traceless operatives practice subterfuge and careful preparation in order to accomplish their divine mandates.
replaces_summary: Monster Lore; Stern Gaze; Track; Bane; Greater Bane

name: Umbral Stalker
description: As a consummate lurker in the shadows, an umbral stalker observes the enemies of her faith and strikes before the unfortunate victims even know she is skulking nearby.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Domain; Justice Judgement

name: Urban Infiltrator
description: Masters of secretly gathering information to further their faith’s ends, urban infiltrators excel at operating within an unfriendly society or infiltrating an enemy cult.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Monster Lore; Stalwart

name: Vampire Hunter
description: The vampire hunter believes that the worst of undead are the ones with unnatural appetities for flesh and blood.
replaces_summary: Altered Judgments (Purity, Smiting); Detect Alignment; Altered Bane

name: Vigilant Defender
description: Some inquisitors focus more on protecting those who share their faiths and their ideals than on actively hunting their enemies.
replaces_summary: Judgement; Stern Gaze; Exploit Weakness; True Judgement

name: Witch Hunter
description: When pursuing justice for their faith, inquisitors sometimes hunt sorcerers, witches, wizards, and other practitioners of arcane magic—but especially witches, since their devotion to a patron is often seen as suspect by many religions.
replaces_summary: Monster Lore; Detect Alignment; Discern Lies; True Judgment; Track; Exploit Weakness

name: Antiquarian
description: Antiquarians are collectors, explorers, and scholars who delve into ruined places in search of lost lore and artifacts.
replaces_summary: Alchemy; Poison Lore; Poison Resistance; Poison Immunity; Swift Alchemy

name: Bonded Investigator
description: Bonded investigators use intelligent familiars to assist them in their investigations.
replaces_summary: Poison Lore; Poison Resistance; Poison Immunity; 4th-level Studied Strike, 7th-level Investigator Talent

name: Cartographer
description: Cartographers are eager trailblazers who explore uncharted terrain, study landmarks, and find new paths through the wilderness.
replaces_summary: Poison Lore; Poison Resistance; Keen Recollection; Swift Alchemy

name: Cipher
description: A cipher trains himself to remain undetected and ignored in order to conduct his investigations without opposition or bothersome questions
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Inspiration; Trapfinding; Poison Lore; Poison Resistance; Poison Immunity; Trap SenseSwift Alchemy; 3rd, 5th, 7th, 9th, 11th-level Investigator Talent; Studied Strike

name: Conspirator
description: A conspirator uses his knowledge of investigations to make sure he avoids any investigations that his rivals perform.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Inspiration; Trap Sense; 7th-level Investigator Talent

name: Cryptid Scholar
description: Cryptid scholars research monsters that lurk secretly at the edge of civilization, developing a deep expertise regarding their anatomy, habits, and ecology.
replaces_summary: Poison Lore; Poison Resistance; Studied Combat; Studied Strike

name: Cult Hunter
description: Cult hunters seek out heretical secret societies, purging their ideological taint and banishing summoned servants.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Poison Resistance; Poison Immunity; Poison Lore; Trap Sense; Swift Alchemy; Studied Combat Studied Strike; 7th, 13th-level Investigator Talent

name: Dread Investigator
description: The dread investigator observes human behavior after the fact, honing their intuition by delving into the mysteries of death.
replaces_summary: Inspiration; Studied Combat; Studied Strike; Poison Lore; Poison Resistance; Poison Immunity; 7th, 13th, and 19th-level Talents

name: Empiricist
description: Champions of deductive reasoning and logical insight, empiricists put their faith in facts, data, confirmed observations, and consistently repeatable experiments—these things are their currency of truth.
replaces_summary: Poison Lore; Poison Resistance; Swift Alchemy; True Inspiration

name: Engineer
description: A number of investigators use their vast knowledge to build machines that replicate the intricacies of the world
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Poison Lore; Poison Resistance; Trap Sense

name: Forensic Physician
description: A forensic physician specializes in analyzing medical details in order to solve crimes and uncover evidence.
replaces_summary: Inspiration; Trapfinding; Trap Sense; 3rd, 5th-level Investigator Talent

name: Gravedigger
description: Gravediggers pursue long buried mysteries, figuratively and literally digging up the truth and finding answers to secrets only moldering corpses and dry bones can tell.
replaces_summary: Weapon and Armor Proficiency; Alchemy; Trapfinding; Trap Sense; Poison Resistance; Poison Immunity; Studied Combat; Studied Strike; Swift Alchemy

name: Guardian of Immortality
description: Guardians of immortality are Thuvian investigators charged with hunting down those who would attempt to steal or learn for themselves the secret to creating the sun orchid elixir.
replaces_summary: Poison Lore; Poison Resistance; 7th, 13th-level Investigator Talent; Poison Immunity

name: Hallucinist
description: A hallucinist imbibes magical drugs to expand his mind into the psychedelic world, extending his awareness to things he could not normally perceive.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Trap Sense; 3rd, 5th, 7th, 9th, 11th, 15th-level Investigator Talent; Poison Lore' Poison Resistance; Poison Immunity; Keen Recollection; Studied Combat

name: Holomog Demolitionist
description: Holomog is an ancient nation built on even more ancient ruins. Its renowned civil engineers develop skills that allow them to take full advantage of their environments.
replaces_summary: Poison Lore; Poison Resistance; 3rd, 9th-level Investigator Talent; Studied Target

name: Jinyiwei
description: Jinyiwei are a special task force of secret police whose mission is to root out, expose, and counteract their government’s existing wrongdoing.
replaces_summary: Inspiration; Alchemy; Trapfinding; Trap Sense; Swift Alchemy

name: Lamplighter
description: Lamplighters are investigators tasked with keeping the night streets safe and lit, and securing darkened areas against whatever dangers lurk in the shadows.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Poison Lore; Poison Resistance; Poison Immunity; Keen Recollection; Trap Sense

name: Lepidstadt Inspector
description: The ability of investigators trained at the University of Lepidstadt to get to the bottom of a mystery is legendary, and troubled settlements throughout the region often send petitions to the university requesting the aid of a formally trained inspector when some mystery threatens the community as a whole.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Trap Sense; Poison Resistance; Poison Immunity; Swift Alchemy; 5th-level Investigator Talent

name: Majordomo
description: In a world of intrigue where the loyalties of staff can be bought and sold, sometimes it takes the skills of an investigator to follow the byzantine paper trails and prevent embezzling and espionage within the estates of the elite.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Alchemy; Swift Alchemy; Trap Sense

name: Malice Binder
description: A lock of hair, a bit of blood, even a footprint is enough to grant a wily witch hunter an edge. Malice binders often rise from the ranks of the common folk rather than from wealthy nobles or the church. Their magic is old and crude, but devastatingly effective against those they hunt.
replaces_summary: Inspiration; Trapfinding; Trap Sense; Alchemy; Poison Resistance; Poison Immunity

name: Natural Philosopher
description: Natural philosophers are scholars, explorers, and survivalists who feel at home in nature, seldom longing for the comforts of the city.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Class Skills; Inspiration; Trapfinding; Alchemy; Trap Sense; 3rd-level Investigator Talent

name: Portal Seeker
description: While most investigators seek answers to mundane mysteries, portal seekers chase more unusual enigmas: portals to other planes.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Poison Resistance; Poison Lore; Studied Strike; Poison Immunity

name: Profiler
description: Profilers understand the human psyche to an intense degree, allowing them to predict the criminals they hunt.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Poison Lore; Trap Sense; Poison Resistance; Poison Immunity; Swift Alchemy; 7th-level Investigator Talent

name: Psychic Detective
description: A psychic detective supplements her keen insight with occult skill to unravel mysteries both ordinary and supernatural.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Alchemy; Poison Lore; Poison Resistance; Swift Alchemy; Poison Immunity; 3rd-level Talent; Investigator Talents

name: Questioner
description: Dabblers in arcane magic and masters of stealth and guile, questioners are investigators who often find themselves mucking about in cases for less-than-savory clientele or that require an extra bit of subtlety.
replaces_summary: Inspiration; Alchemy; Poison Lore

name: Reckless Epicurean
description: Whether an obsessive scientist, a healer determined to ensure the safety of her tinctures, or a seeker of new and interesting potions, a reckless epicurean’s body is saturated with experimental chemicals and magic.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; 5th, 13th-level Investigator Talent

name: Ruthless Agent
description: Ruthless agents are often called upon for their skills at extracting information by any means necessary.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Inspiration; Trapfinding; Trap Sense; Swift Alchemy; 11th, 17th-level Investigator Talents.

name: Scavenger
description: Scavengers are masters of systems: how they fit together, why they work, and how to make the most of their parts.
replaces_summary: Alchemy; Inspiration; Poison Lore; Poison Resistance; Poison Immunity

name: Skeptic
description: The skeptic accepts the existence of the occult world while challenging claims that the supernatural explains all of life’s problems.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Poison Resistance; Swift Alchemy; 7th level Investigator Talent

name: Star Watcher
description: Star watchers study the movements and positions of the sun, the moon, and the stars, searching for secrets written in the sky.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Alchemy; Trapfinding; Trap Sense; Swift Alchemy

name: Tekritanin Arbiter
description: The Tekritanin League facilitated trade and settled disputes between a vast array of diverse cultures. The arbiters were the first line of diplomatic defense, solving problems before they could become disruptive.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Poison Lore; Poison Resistance; Trap Sense

name: Toxin Codexer
description: A toxin codexer meticulously seeks out new poisons and catalogs their effects, both those that are harmful and those that are potentially beneficial.
replaces_summary: Alchemy; Trapfinding; 3rd-level Investigator Talent; Keen Recollection; Trap Sense

name: Aquakinetcist
description: While all hydrokineticists have the ability to control water, aquakineticists’ powers are tied to their determination to explore the world beneath the waves.
replaces_summary: Elemental Focus; Expanded Element; Basic Hydrokinesis; 2nd, 6th-levle Utility Wild Talent; Elemental Defense

name: Arakineticist
description: Those of strong will who live under a dark curse long enough might learn to control and channel the corrupt energy afflicting them.
replaces_summary: 4th, 6th, 8th, and 13th-level Utility Wild Talents

name: Blightburner
description: Darklands geokineticists sometimes exhibit supernatural powers associated with blightburn crystals. Blightburner kineticists harness this radioactivity in pyrotechnic displays, an unusual feat for those connected to the element of earth.
replaces_summary: Elemental Focus; Basic Geokinesis; 2nd-level Utility Power; Elemental Overflow; Flesh of Stone; Gather Power; Internal Buffer

name: Blighted Defiler
description: A few kineticists have developed the ability to steal the life force from the surrounding land in order to infuse themselves with unnatural strength beyond their bodies’ normal capabilities.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Gather Power; Key Ability Score; Internal Buffer; Elemental Defense; Omnikinesis

name: Blood Kineticist
description: To a blood kineticist, the water in a creature’s blood is just like any other sort, and she uses that knowledge to brutal ends.
replaces_summary: 1st, 5th, 9th, and 11th-level Infusions; 6th and 8th-level Utility Wild Talent; Expanded Element; Omnikinesis

name: Cinderlands Adept
description: These kineticists are skilled riders, able to wield both weapons and fire with deadly skill.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Elemental Focus; Expanded Element; Gather Power; 1st-level Infusion; 4th, 8th, 12th-level Utility Wild Talent

name: Dark Elementalist
description: Dark elementalists are loathe to suffer the consequences of channeling their power and study the darkest esoteric energies of the planes to use souls to fuel their occult might.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Key Ability Score; Burn; Elemental Overflow; Internal Buffer

name: Elemental Annihilator
description: For some kineticists, nothing in life is as sweet as destruction and pain. Elemental annihilators pursue only uses of their powers that harm others.
replaces_summary: 1st, 3rd, 5th, and 9th-level Infusions; all utility wild talents

name: Elemental Ascetic
description: Combining the elemental powers of a kineticist with the rigid physical discipline of a monk, an elemental ascetic channels his powers through his body to enhance himself in combat.
replaces_summary: Kinetic Blast; Elemental Overflow; Wild Talents; Elemental Defense; 5th, 9th, 13th-level Infusions

name: Elemental Purist
description: An elemental purist spurns all elements that are not her own, for utter devotion can allow one to accomplish even seemingly impossible heroics.
replaces_summary: Internal Buffer; Expanded Elements; 11th, 19th-level Infusion; Omnikinesis

name: Elysiokineticist
description: Similar to how phytokineticists draw their power from the First World, elysiokineticists trace their abilities to the riotous wilderness of the plane known as Elysium
replaces_summary: Elemental Focus; Class Skills; Expanded Element; Basic Phytokinesis, 1st-level Infusion; Flesh of Wood

name: Ioun Kineticist
description: Some aetherkineticists dedicate themselves to unlocking ioun stones’ cosmic secrets and seemingly limitless potential.
replaces_summary: Elemental Focus; Basic Telekinesis; Internal Buffer; Elemental Overflow

name: Kinetic Chirurgeon
description: While any hydrokineticist or telekineticist can learn the rudiments of healing, some kineticists are virtuosos of the curative arts.
replaces_summary: Infusions; Metakinesis; Infusion Specialization; Internal Buffer

name: Kinetic Knight
description: A kinetic knight dons armor and wields a blade of elemental energy.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Kinetic Blast; Infusions; Metakinesis; Metakinetic Master; 3rd-level Infusion; Supercharge

name: Leshykineticist
description: (Vine Leshy only) Of all those who pursue the path of a kineticist, only leshykineticists are both made of plant matter and born of nature spirits. These curious creatures wield ancient powers unknown to any other kineticists.
replaces_summary: Element; Expanded Element; 6th, 10th-level Utility Wild Talents; Maximize Metakinesis; Basic Phytokinesis; Internal Buffer; Omnikinesis

name: Overwhelming Soul
description: Some kineticists have such a powerful personality that they can seize control of their element with their minds alone, without endangering their bodies.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Wild Talents; Burn; Internal Buffer; Elemental Overflow

name: Psammokinetic
description: Psammokinetics are ascetic kineticists who consider the harsh nature of the burning deserts of Osirion to be a tempering fire that can purify all their imperfections.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Elemental Focus, Elemental Overflow; Expanded Element

name: Psychokineticist
description: Psychokineticists channel elemental power through their minds rather than their bodies and while this energy ravages their minds, it unleashes power locked in their ruptured emotions.
replaces_summary: Key Ability Score; Burn; Elemental Overflow

name: Terrakineticist
description: Terrakineticists have a stunted ability that allows them to access the Elemental Planes, but they’ve learned how to turn this limited talent into an incredible strength by tapping into the ambient elemental nature of the land surrounding them.
replaces_summary: Elemental Focus; Kinetic Blast; Elemental Defense; Expanded Element; Omnikinesis

name: Armored Battlemage
description: Armored battlemages learn to move and cast spells in even the most restrictive armors, and have developed new methods to magically enhance their armor.
replaces_summary: Spell Combat; Arcane Pool; 3rd, 18th-level Magus Arcana; Improved Spell Combat; Greater Spell Combat

name: Beastblade
description: Beastblade magi work in tandem with their familiars, using spell, steel, and claw to clear the battlefield of foes.
replaces_summary: 3rd-level Magus Arcana; Spell Recall; Knowledge Pool; Improved Spell Recall

name: Bladebound
description: A select group of magi are called to carry a black blade—a sentient weapon of often unknown and possibly unknowable purpose.
replaces_summary: Arcane Pool; 3rd-level Magus Arcana

name: Card Caster
description: The card caster is an ancient, martial offshoot of the traditional harrower, learning to not only draw power from the harrow, but also to invest each card with deadly power.
replaces_summary: Arcane Pool; Spellstrike; 3rd-level Arcana

name: Deep Marshal
description: Largely unseen by visitors, the deep marshals have survived into the modern era as keepers, protectors, and repairers of the myriad of stony passages that travel around, through, and under the Five Kings Mountains.
replaces_summary: Armor Proficiencies; Medium Armor; Heavy Armor; Spellcasting; Arcane Pool; Spell Combat; Spellstrike; 3rd-level Magus Arcana

name: Eldritch Archer
description: The eldritch archer rains magical attacks down on her foes from the city walls.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Arcane Pool; Spell Combat; Spellstrike; Counterstrike

name: Elemental Knight
description: (Suli Only) Elemental knights are born with elemental energies surging through their blood and discover the secret of reconciling and focusing this primal power into the arcane.
replaces_summary: Spell Recall

name: Esoteric
description: Esoterics are drawn to the mysticism of the occult and spend their lives delving into forgotten texts and forbidden tomes.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Spellcasting; Arcane Pool; Spellstrike; Spell Recall; Bonus Feats; Medium Armor; Heavy Armor; Improved Spell Recall

name: Fiend Flayer
description: (Tiefling Only) Some tiefling magi can tap the dark energy of their fiendish blood to enhance their arcane and combat talents.
replaces_summary: None

name: Greensting Slayer
description: The blending of martial prowess with elven magic has long been a staple of the Blackash Training Grounds in Erages. More than a few who study at Erages Academy follow this path because of the promises of wealth that the city’s criminal element makes toward practiced brawlers and smugglers.
replaces_summary: Arcane Pool; Medium Armor; Heavy Armor

name: Hexbreaker
description: Hexbreakers—sometimes called witch-hammers for their proclivity toward warhammers—have borne witness to the damage of unrestrained arcane power and hone their own arcane arts to battle such abuse.
replaces_summary: Spell Recall; Improved Spell Recall

name: Hexcrafter
description: A hexcrafter magus has uncovered the secret of using his arcane pool to recreate witch hexes.
replaces_summary: Spell Recall

name: Iron-Ring Striker
description: Practitioners of this martial art, powered by arcane magic, wear an iron ring on the finger of one hand.
replaces_summary: Weapon Proficiency; Arcane Pool; Spellstrike; 5th-level Bonus Feat; Medium Armor; Heavy Armor; Knowledge Pool; Counterstrike

name: Jistkan Artificer
description: As part of studying the secret methods the original Jistkan artificers used to build golems, these magi graft construct parts onto their own arms.
replaces_summary: Spells; Arcane Pool; Cantrips; 3rd-level Magus Arcana; Spellstrike

name: Kapenia Dancer
description: The kapenia dancer laces their deadly scarves with powerful magic.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Diminished Spellcasting

name: Kensai
description: A kensai spends his life focusing his training and meditation into a rapturous perfection of the use of a single weapon, which is usually but not always a sword, channeling his arcane might through it in a dizzying and deadly dance beyond the abilities of even the greatest of mundane warriors.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Spells; Spell Recall; Knowledge Pool; Medium Armor; 9th-level Arcana; Improved Spell Recall; Heavy Armor; True Magus

name: Magic Warrior
description: Trained in a tradition stretching back to Old-Mage Jatembe’s Ten Magic Warriors, magic warriors renounce their identities to master magical might and serve as champions of culture and learning across the Mwangi Expanse and beyond.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; 3rd-level Magus Arcana; Improved Spell Combat; Greater Spell Combat; Greater Spell Access

name: Mindblade
description: A mindblade blends psychic talent and martial skill to lethal effect. By forming weapons with her mind, she always has the right tool for any situation.
replaces_summary: Spellcasting; Arcane Pool; Spell Recall; Knowledge Pool; Improved Spell Recall; Greater Spell Combat; Greater Spell Access; Medium Armor; Improved Spell Combat; Heavy Armor

name: Myrmidarch
description: The myrmidarch is a skilled specialist, using magic to supplement and augment his martial mastery.
replaces_summary: Spells; Spell Recall; Improved Spell Recall; 6th, 12th, 18th-level Magus Arcana; Knowledge Pool; Improved Spell Combat; Greater Spell Combat; True Magus

name: Nature-Bonded Magus
description: A nature-bonded magus synergizes arcane magic and the divine magic traditions of druids into a deadly synthesis.
replaces_summary: Arcane Pool; Spell Recall; Knowledge Pool; Improved Spell Recall

name: Puppetmaster
description: Puppetmasters focus on using charm and illusion spells to control the senses of those for whom they perform.
replaces_summary: Skills; Knowledge Pool; Greater Spell Access; Arcane Pool; Spell Combat; Improved Spell Combat; Greater Spell Combat; Spellstrike; Fighter Training; Counterstrike; Medium Armor; Heavy Armor; True Magus

name: Sigilus
description: A sigilus manipulates the discoveries of wizards and other more sagacious Cyphermages, using those findings to create special sigils that allow her to transfer spell effects into different objects for later use.
replaces_summary: Spellstrike; Medium Armor; Heavy Armor

name: Skirnir
description: Sometimes called a shield-vassal or shieldmaiden, the skirnir has learned to infuse his power into his shield.
replaces_summary: Spells; Spell Combat; Spell Recall; Knowledge Pool; Improved Spell Combat; Greater Spell Combat; Greater Spell Access; Counterstrike

name: Sorrowblade
description: A magus who has suffered greatly over the course ofher life can learn to channel that misery into her weapon and inflict it on others.
replaces_summary: 3rd, 12th-level Magus Arcana; Speed Weapon Property

name: Soul Forger
description: The soul forger has learned the skill of infusing the raw magical essence of his soul into armaments of surpassing power, combining the mystic arts with the arts of war in a unity of steely perfection.
replaces_summary: Spells; Knowledge Pool; Spell Recall; Improved Spell Recall; Counterstrike; Greater Spell Access

name: Spell Dancer
description: (Elf Only) Many elven magi do not consider themselves masters of a blend of martial and magical talents, but rather a sub-category of wizards who study the effect of physical movement and techniques upon spellcasting ability.
replaces_summary: Arcane Pool; 5th-level Bonus Feat; Medium Armor; Heavy Armor

name: Spell Trapper
description: Spell trappers forsake much of their arcane skill and martial knowledge in exchange for the ability to conjure magical traps to hinder their foes.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Spell Recall; Fighter Training

name: Spellblade
description: A spellblade magus can manifest a ghostly blade of force that can be used as an off-hand weapon.
replaces_summary: Spellstrike

name: Spire Defender
description: Magi who train themselves to accompany sages and archaeologists venturing into the Mordant Spire become spire defenders.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Spell Recall

name: Staff Magus
description: While most magi use a one-handed weapon as their melee implement of choice, one group of magi uses the quarterstaff instead.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Medium Armor; Heavy Armor; Fighter Training

name: Fiend Keeper
description: Fiend keepers serve as a vessel to contain one of the world’s evil spirits so that it cannot inflict further harm.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Spirit; Haunt Channeler; Location Channel; Connection Channel; Ask the Spirits

name: Kami Medium
description: Some mediums channel the power of nature spirits called kami rather than the spirits of legend.
replaces_summary: Spirit; Taboo; Spellcasting; Haunt Channeler; Location Channel; Connection Channel; Ask the Spirits; Astral Journey

name: Medium of the Master
description: A medium of the master forgoes a connection with most spirits to form a bond with the spirits of great martial artists.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Armor Proficiency; Spirit; Spirit Surge; Spirit Power (Lesser): Shared Seance; Spirit Power (Intermediate); Trance of Three; Spirit Power (Supreme)

name: Nexian Channeler
description: The archmage Nex long ago created an astral reflection of himself from which he can draw additional power. This echo now serves as an appropriate spirit for well-trained mediums to channel.
replaces_summary: Spirit; Knacks; Taboo; Haunt Channeler; Location Channel; Connection Channel; Propitiation; Ask the Spirits; Astral Journey; Trance of Three; Spacious Soul; Spirit Mastery; Astral Beacon

name: Outer Channeler
description: Outer channelers invite extraplanar entities to inhabit them, allowing the likes of angels and demons to use them as vessels in exchange for power.
replaces_summary: Spirit; Taboo; Haunt Channeler; Connection Channel; Location Channel; Ask the Spirits; Astral Journey

name: Reanimated Medium
description: Sometimes a departed soul destined for legend gains a second chance at life by possessing his own revived body.
replaces_summary: Spirit; Taboo; Haunt Channeler; Location Channel; Connection Channel; Spacious Soul; Astral Beacon

name: Relic Channeler
description: A relic channeler carries relics of legend around with her.
replaces_summary: Spirit; Haunt Channeler; Location Channel; Connection Channel

name: Rivethun Spirit Channeler
description: Rivethun spirit channelers are mediums who practice the ancient dwarven philosophy of the rivethun, which teaches that power lies in the connection between physical bodies and immaterial souls.
replaces_summary: Spellcasting; Spirit; Spirit Bonus; Spirit Surge; Taboo; Connection Channel

name: Spirit Dancer
description: A spirit dancer enacts an exuberant dance to the spirits, accepting many roles in the shifting dance and changing dance partners quickly.
replaces_summary: Spirit; Spirit Bonus; Spirit Surge; Taboo; Shared Seance; Trance of Three; Spacious Soul; Astral Beacon

name: Spirit Eater
description: Guardians of their communities, spirit eaters absorb the dead to channel their power and serve as a line of defense against all manner of incorporeal yokai.
replaces_summary: Haunt Channeler; Location Channel; Connection Channel

name: Storm Dreamer
description: Storm dreamers serve the Storm Kindlers by communing with their mystical predecessors. They serve as spiritual advisors for the organization, and are valued for the lore and wisdom they gain during their trances.
replaces_summary: Spirit Surge; Haunt Channeler; Location Channel; Connection Channel

name: Storyteller
description: While all mediums connect to legendary spirits, storyteller mediums take their obsession with legends to the extreme, suffusing their lives with epic tales at the cost of some of their spiritual power.
replaces_summary: Spirit; Spirit Bonus; Spirit Surge; Shared Seance; Taboo; Haunt Channeler; Propitiation; Trance of Three; Connection Channel; Spirit Mastery; Astral Beacon

name: Uda Wendo
description: Uda wendo are powerful mediums who are sensitive to the presence and desires of the wendo, powerful and mysterious entities that walk Golarion while shaping fate and guiding destiny.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Spirit; Shared Seance; Haunt Channeler; Location Channel; Connection Channel

name: Vessel of the Failed
description: A vessel of the failed channels the spirits of those who attempted the Test of the Starstone but failed; these spirits grant the medium abilities wrought from the spirits’ ultimate shortcomings.
replaces_summary: Spirit; Spirit Mastery; Spirit Surge; Taboo

name: Voice of the Void
description: Some mediums delve deeply into the spirit world and make contact with entities that exist outside the boundaries of the sane multiverse.
replaces_summary: Spirit; Taboo; Haunt Channeler

name: Aromaphile
description: (Ghoran only) Rather than employ the baleful glares of the typical mesmerist, aromaphiles hypnotize others using an array of sickeningly sweet scents developed from their unique ghoran physiology.
replaces_summary: Hypnotic Stare; Painful Stare; Towering Ego; Bold Stare; Touch Treatment; Mental Potency

name: Autohypnotist
description: An autohypnotist has incredibly powerful psychic abilities that he can’t entirely control.
replaces_summary: Hypnotic Stare; Mental Potency

name: Chart Caster
description: Lirgeni astrologists would divine their futures by consulting star charts, but some had no talent for astrology and simply faked their results. When prophecy died with Aroden, a few Lirgeni survivors continued with this “reliable” method.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Mesmerist Tricks; Manifold Tricks; Touch Treatment

name: Cult Master
description: The leaders of some cults are simply charlatans, bathing in the adulation of their followers. Others believe themselves to be truly in touch with an unknown power, bringing their unique messages to those willing to follow and obey them.
replaces_summary: Consummate Liar; Painful Stare; Mesmerist Tricks; 3rd, 6th, and 14th-level Touch Treatments; 7th-level Bold Stare; Masterful Tricks; Rule Minds

name: Dreamstalker
description: Many mesmerists concentrate their studies on conscious, rational thought, but dreamstalkers instead delve into the unleashed unconscious mind that wanders free in dreams.
replaces_summary: Painful Stare; 1st-level Mesmerist Trick; Towering Ego;  Touch Treatment; Manifold Tricks; Glib Lie

name: Enigma
description: An enigma spends his life dedicated to developing psychic abilities that allow him to operate unnoticed.
replaces_summary: Hypnotic Stare; Consummate Liar; Painful Stare; Touch Treatment; Manifold Tricks; Glib Lie; Rule Minds

name: Eyebiter
description: An eyebiter’s eyes become so infused with psychic might that they can leave the eyebiter’s body and move about on their own.
replaces_summary: Consummate Liar; 1st-level Mesmerist Trick; Touch Treatment; Mental Potency; Glib Lie

name: Fey Trickster
description: Most mesmerists draw psychic power from the Astral Plane, but a few gain their powers in an unusual way: from a brush with the fey.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Spellcasting; Towering Ego; Touch Treatment; Rule Minds

name: Gaslighter
description: A gaslighter takes joy in others’ suffering, delighting in driving them to madness.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Consummate Liar; Touch Treatment; Painful Stare;

name: Hate-Monger
description: Hate-mongers are xenophobic and judgmental rabblerousers who whip up mobs against enemies real or imagined.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Consummate Liar; Touch Treatment; Mental Potency; Manifold Tricks; 7th-level Bold Stare; Glib Lie

name: Material Manipulator
description: Rather than waste time limiting himself to ruling minds, a material manipulator forces his whims upon reality and uses his magic to ensure that those around him comply with his demands.
replaces_summary: Spellcasting; Consummate Liar; 2nd-level Mesmerist Trick; Manifold Tricks; Touch Treatment; Rules Minds

name: Mindwyrm Mesmer
description: Mindwyrm mesmers emulate the confidence, swagger, and fear inspired by dragons to bully and browbeat others into obedience.
replaces_summary: Consummate Liar; Painful Stare

name: Projectionist
description: Not content to simply command others from afar, a projectionist is able to use his mesmerizing powers to utterly dominate other creatures and objects by projecting his spirit from his body.
replaces_summary: Spellcasting; 4th-level Mesmerist Trick; Mental Potency

name: Spirit Walker
description: Some mesmerists specialize in dominating and controlling undead rather than the living.
replaces_summary: Spells; Consummate Liar; Mental Potency; 3rd, 6th, 10th, and 14th-level Touch Treatments; Rule Minds

name: Thought Eater
description: Mesmerists that learn to pierce deep into the minds of their victims are known as thought eaters, dangerous individuals capable of devouring thoughts and assuming the knowledge those notions represent for themselves.
replaces_summary: Consummate Liar; Towering Ego; Touch Treatment; Rule Minds

name: Toxitician
description: Toxiticians forgo the mesmerist’s stare, instead combining their psychic power with their alchemical knowledge to craft injections that torment their foes and bolster themselves and their allies.
replaces_summary: Consummate Liar; Hypnotic Stare; Painful Stare; Bold Stare; Touch Treatment; Glib Lie

name: Umbral Mesmerist
description: Trained in secret, umbral mesmerists are clandestine weapons who spend years learning how to shape terrifying creatures from the inky darkness in which they revel.
replaces_summary: Spellcasting; 1st, 4th, 8th, 12th, 16th, and 20th-level Mesmerist Tricks; Towering Ego; Mental Potency

name: Vexing Daredevil
description: Vexing daredevils train their psychic powers for combat, specializing in momentarily blinding and confusing their foes with sudden feints and tricks.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; 1st-level Mesmerist Trick; Touch Treatment; Bold Stare; Glib Tongue

name: Vexing Trickster
description: Vexing tricksters indulge in the various hijinks their power enables.
replaces_summary: Consumate Liar, Touch Treatment

name: Vizier
description: While many mesmerists focus on beguiling their foes or taking overt control of others with their mental powers, viziers carve out a subtler niche for themselves as advisors.
replaces_summary: Consummate Liar; 3rd-level Bold Stare; Towering Ego; Mental Potency

name: Vox
description: Most mesmerists practice their mental manipulation through their entrancing gaze, but others have learned to practice their skill through the lilt and intonation of their voice.
replaces_summary: Spellcasting; Towering Ego; 10th-level Mesmerist Trick; Touch Treatment; Hypnotic Stare

name: Black Asp
description: The black asps are a sinister order of monks who train as assassins and infiltrators with no need for weapons to achieve their goals.
replaces_summary: Stunning Fist; Still Mind

name: Brazen Disciple
description: Brazen disciples use deception in their fighting style, and many go a step further, incorporating fire and smoke both mundane and magical.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; 1st, 6th-level Bonus Feats; Still Mind; Abundant Step; Perfect Self

name: Disciple of Wholeness
description: Disciples of wholeness train to bring themselves and others closer to physical and spiritual perfection.
replaces_summary: Ki Pool; Purity of Body; Diamond Body; Diamond Soul

name: Drunken Master
description: The drunken master finds perfection through excess.
replaces_summary: Still Mind; Purity of Body; Diamond Body; Diamond Soul; Empty Body

name: Elemental Monk
description: Elemental monks draw inspiration from genies to create their fighting style. Their adaptability and versatility make them capable of serving as emissaries of elemental balance.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Stunning Fist; Bonus Feats; Evasion; Purity of Body; Diamond Body; Ki Pool

name: Far Strike Monk
description: Far strike monks are masters of thrown weapons, from shuriken to throwing axes to spears.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Flurry of Blows; Bonus Feats; Stunning Fist; Still Mind; Purity of Body; Diamond Body

name: Flowing Monk
description: The flowing monk is the wind and the river. He knows how the world flows, and forces his enemies to flow with it.
replaces_summary: Stunning Fist; 2nd-level Bonus Feat; Fast Movement; Purity of Body; Diamond Body; Quivering Palm

name: Gray Disciple
description: (Duergar Only) The gray disciple contemplates the inner voice of duergar magic and the silent eternity of stone, mastering these dual mysteries and combining them to deadly effect.
replaces_summary: Slow Fall; Still Mind; High Jump; 6th-level Bonus Feat; Wholeness of Body; Abundant Step; Quivering Palm; Tongue of the Sun and Moon; Empty Body

name: Hamatulatsu Master
description: Once part of the Sisterhood of Eiseth, the Sisterhood of the Golden Erinyes is an order of female monks dedicated to emulating the painful strikes of the barbed devil, leaving targets alive but permanently scarred and broken by pain—a method called hamatulatsu.
replaces_summary: Bonus Feats; Stunning Fist; Ki Pool; Purity of Body

name: Harrow Warden
description: Some who study the mysteries of the harrow do so to restore lost knowledge and pride to the Varisian people. The harrow warden fulfills this role as she seeks to bring her mind and body into alignment, and she protects her people by invoking the folklore that has guided them for so long. In mastering her own place in fate, a harrow warden learns how to misalign the destinies of others.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Stunning Fist; Improved Evasion; Diamond Body; Quivering Palm

name: Hellcat
description: Monks of the hellcat sect follow the teachings of Asmodeus or another infernal power.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Class Skills; Weapon Proficiencies; Bonus Feats; Stunning Fist; Fast Movement; Evasion; Purity of Body; Improved Evasion; Slow Fall; Still Mind; Ki Pool; Wholeness of Body; Diamond Body

name: Hungry Ghost Monk
description: The hungry ghost monk looks to spirits that prey upon the living as models of perfection.
replaces_summary: Stunning Fist; Purity of Body; Wholeness of Body; Diamond Body; Diamond Soul

name: Invested Regent
description: The invested regent can harness a divine spark to perform superhuman stunts, influence others, and escape injury.
replaces_summary: 1st-level Bonus Feat

name: Ironskin Monk
description: (Hobgoblin Only) Through discipline and training, an ironskin monk hardens his body to withstand punishing blows.
replaces_summary: Monk’s AC; Bonus Feats; Evasion; Ki Speed Increase; High Jump; Fast Movement; Slow Fall; Improved Evasion; Tongue of the Sun and Moon; Perfect Self

name: Karmic Monk
description: A karmic monk contemplates the myriad harmonies that guide existence. He learns to use the disharmony in others against them, and to alter his own inner harmonies to exploit weaknesses in his opponents’ defenses.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Class Skills; Stunning Fist; Still Mind; 7th and 10th-level Ki Pool Abilities; Improved Evasion; Slow Fall; Perfect Self

name: Ki Mystic
description: The ki mystic believes that violence is sometimes necessary, but knowing and understanding is the true root of perfection.
replaces_summary: Still Mind; Purity of Body; Diamond Body; Diamond Soul; Empty Body

name: Lifting Hand
description: When facing a master of the lifting hand, an enemy is likely to find itself sailing through the air or locked in a tortuous compliance hold.
replaces_summary: Stunning Fist; Bonus Feats; Abundant Step

name: Maneuver Master
description: The maneuver master specializes in more complicated moves than simple damage-dealing strikes.
replaces_summary: Flurry of Blows; Still Mind; Slow Fall; Purity of Body; Diamond Body; Quivering Palm

name: Martial Artist
description: The martial artist pursues a mastery of pure martial arts without the monastic traditions. He is a master of form, but lacks the ability to harness his ki.
replaces_summary: Still Mind; Slow Fall; Ki Pool; Purity of Body; Diamond Body; Perfect Self; Wholeness of Body; Timeless Body; Tongue of Sun and Moon; Abundant Step; Diamond Soul; Empty Body

name: Master of Many Styles
description: The master of many styles is a collector. For every move, he seeks a counter. For every style, he has a riposte.
replaces_summary: Bonus Feats; Flurry of Blows; Perfect Self

name: Menhir Guardian
description: Menhir guardians are ascetic protectors of sacred druidic sites, less concerned with structure and maintaining internal order than they are with preserving the natural balance of the world.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Unarmed Strike; Ki Pool; Flurry of Blows; Stunning Fist; High Jump; Quivering Palm

name: Monk of the Empty Hand
description: The monk of the empty hand eschews normal weapons in favor of whatever is lying around—rocks, chair legs, flagons of ale, even a simple quill pen all become deadly weapons in the hands of such a monk.
replaces_summary: Monk Weapon Proficiencies; Still Mind; Purity of Body; Diamond Body

name: Monk of the Four Winds
description: The monk of the four winds is connected to the natural world in a way few other creatures—even other monks—can hope to match.
replaces_summary: Stunning Fist; Abundant Step; Timeless Body; Perfect Self

name: Monk of the Healing Hand
description: Monks of the healing hand seek perfection through helping others.
replaces_summary: Wholeness of Body; Diamond Body; Quivering Palm; Perfect Self

name: Monk of the Lotus
description: While a monk of the lotus realizes that combat cannot always be avoided—and is more than capable in a fight—he understands that all creatures are connected, and to harm another is to harm the self.
replaces_summary: Stunning Fist; Abundant Step; Quivering Palm; Tongue of the Sun and the Moon

name: Monk of the Mantis
description: A body contains many points where the flesh, mind, and spirit coincide. A monk of the mantis is skilled at manipulating these points.
replaces_summary: 2nd, 6th, 10th, 14th, and 18th-level Bonus Feats; Wholeness of Body; Diamond Body; Diamond Soul; Quivering Palm

name: Monk of the Sacred Mountain
description: The monk of the sacred mountain finds strength and power in the earth beneath his feet.
replaces_summary: Evasion; Slow Fall; High Jump; Improved Evasion; Tongue of the Sun and the Moon

name: Monk of the Seven Forms
description: In their place, the monks of the isolated sanctuary now teach a style featuring lightning-fast strikes that mimic both dervish dances and the searing winds of the arid desert, favoring mobility and endurance.
replaces_summary: Bonus Feats; Stunning Fist; Maneuver Training; Abundant Step; Quivering Palm

name: Nimble Guardian
description: (Catfolk Only) Some catfolk monks dedicate their graceful prowess to the defense of others, especially those dedicated to a similar ethos or who prove themselves as stalwart allies of the monk’s cause.
replaces_summary: Evasion; Still Mind; Purity of Body; Wholeness of Body; Improved Evasion

name: Nornkith
description: Nornkith hone their bodies and minds to follow the branching threefold paths of fate.
replaces_summary: Key Ability Score; Stunning Fist; 1st-, 10th-level Bonus Feat; Purity of Body; Diamond Soul; Quivering Palm; Empty Body; Perfect Self

name: Ouat
description: (Dwarf Only) The ascetic, desert-dwelling dwarves known as the Ouat have eschewed dwarven traditions, whose unchanging rigidity, they believe, shackles their people to the past and inhibits self-improvement.
replaces_summary: Greed, Hatred, Defensive Training, Stonecunning, Weapon Familiarity (Dwarven Racial Traits); Stunning Fist; Wholeness of Body

name: Perfect Scholar
description: Perfect scholars, often worshipers of Irori, hone their minds and bodies through the accumulation of knowledge
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Still Mind; Slow Fall; Tongue of the Sun and Moon; Perfect Self

name: Qinggong Monk
description: The qinggong monk is a master of her ki, using it to perform superhuman stunts or even blast opponents with supernatural energy.
replaces_summary: Slow Fall; High Jump; Wholeness of Body; Diamond Body; Abundant Step; Diamond Soul; Quivering Palm; Timeless Body; Tongue of the Sun and Moon; Empty Body; Perfect Self

name: Sage Counselor
description: Sage counselors are ascetics and mystics who leave the confines of the monastery walls to advise secular people about spiritual truths and to seek knowledge of the outside world.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; 1st, 2nd, 6th, 10th-level Bonus Feat; Ki Pool abilities

name: Scaled Fist
description:
Scaled fists eschew passive introspection in favor of unshakable confidence.
	Steeped in traditions that trace their origins to the warrior-monks who trained under the tutelage of draconic masters in Tian Xia, scaled fists eschew passive introspection in favor of unshakable confidence. Scaled fists learn to combine brutal intimidation with the brazen ferocity of an ancient wyrm to devastate their foes.
replaces_summary: Bonus Feats; Still Mind; Maneuver Training; Quivering Palm

name: Scarred Monk
description: For monks who seek the truth hidden in pain, the practice of mortifications of the flesh helps to achieve enlightenment and acquire disturbing powers.
replaces_summary: Ki Pool; High Jump; Wholeness of Body; Abundant Step, Empty Body

name: Sensei
description: The sensei is a revered teacher who imparts lessons on the oneness of mind, body, and spirit, along with occasional correction that is subtle and swift.
replaces_summary: Flurry of Blows; Fast Movement; Improved Evasion; Evasion; 2nd, 6th, 12th, 18th-level Bonus Feats

name: Serpent-Fire Adept
description: A serpent-fire adept embraces the secrets of her chakras and mastery of their energetic flow.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Stunning Fist; 1st, 2nd, 6th, 10th, 14th, and 18th-level Bonus Feats; Slow Fall; High Jump; Wholeness of Body

name: Sin Monk
description: {Ex-monk archetype) Martial experts who have allowed such passions to lead them astray may find themselves gaining powers drawn from their newly embraced sins.
replaces_summary: Ki Pool; High Jump; Wholeness of Body; Abundant Step; Empty Body; Perfect Self

name: Softstrike Monk
description: Softstrike monks employ a nonlethal but effective form of combat.
replaces_summary: Weapon Proficiency; Unarmed Strike; 6th, 10th-level Bonus Feats; Wholeness of Body; Quivering Palm; Empty Body

name: Sohei
description: The sohei are masters of the horse and the hunt.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Stunning Fist; Fast Movement; Increased Unarmed Damage; Slow Fall; Abundant Step; Purity of Body; Diamond Body; Quivering Palm; Timeless Body; Tongue of Sun and Moon

name: Soul Shepherd
description: Whether studying at the feet of psychopomps or merely imitating the creatures' philosophy and methods, soul shepherds believe in safeguarding the Cycle of Souls.
replaces_summary: Stunning Fist; Evasion; Improved Evasion; Abundant Step; Quivering Palm

name: Spirit Master
description: Spirit masters are monks who specialize in combating the undead, laying their corpses to rest, and sending their souls to final judgment.
replaces_summary: Still Mind; Maneuver Training; Purity of Body; Wholeness of Body; Quivering Palm; Empty Body; Perfect Self

name: Student of Stone
description: (Oread Only) By following the path of the stone, students of stone give up much of monks’ mobility in favor of sheer resilience.
replaces_summary: Evasion; Fast Movement; Bonus Feat; High Jump; Improved Evasion; Abundant Step

name: Terra-Cotta Monk
description: Possessing a natural affinity for all things earthen, terracotta monks are experts at turning the stone around them against their enemies.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Evasion; High Jump; Improved Evasion; Abundant Step; Quivering Palm; Empty Body

name: Tetori
description: The style of the tetori is that of the majestic wrestler—a warrior with a dizzying array of grabs, holds, and locks with which to bewilder and incapacitate his foes.
replaces_summary: Bonus Feats; Flurry of Blows; Slow Fall; High Jump; Abundant Step; Improved Evasion; Timeless Body; Tongue of Sun and Moon; Diamond Soul; Empty Body

name: Treetop Monk
description: (Vanara Only) While many vanaras follow traditional monastic training and traditions, others learn to blend exotic combat and the mysterious forces of ki with the natural world, allowing them to move through trees and overgrowth to deliver devastating attacks.
replaces_summary: Still Mind; Purity of Body; Abundant Step

name: Underfoot Adept
description: (Halfling Only) An underfoot adept turns his diminutive stature and unorthodox footwork into a powerful weapon.
replaces_summary: 1st-level Bonus Feat; Stunning Fist; High Jump

name: Wanderer
description: (Human Only) Some monks wander the world in humility to learn and to share wisdom and philosophy from their teachers with those they meet, often aiding those who are in need.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; 1st-level Bonus Feat; Still Mind; Slow Fall; High Jump; Wholeness of Body; Abundant Step; Diamond Soul

name: Wasteland Meditant
description: Some monks journey to remote and isolated corners of the world to find serenity and a heightened sense of awareness.
replaces_summary: Stunning Fist; Evasion; Slow Fall; Improved Evasion; Quivering Palm

name: Water Dancer
description: Water dancers derive their martial training from ancient nereid traditions jealously guarded by these enigmatic fey.
replaces_summary: Flurry of Blows; Stunning Fist; Quivering Palm; 1st, 2nd, 6th, 10th, 14th, and 18th-level Bonus Feats; Unarmed Strike; Evasion; Slow Fall; Improved Evasion; Abundant Step

name: Weapon Adept
description: While all monks train in both unarmed combat and with weapons, the weapon adept seeks to become one with his weapons, transforming them into perfect extensions of his own body.
replaces_summary: Stunning Fist; Evasion; Improved Evasion; Timeless Body; Perfect Self

name: Windstep Master
description: Most monks are agile and fleet of foot, but few can rival a windstep master’s lightness of step.
replaces_summary: Stunning Fist; Slow Fall; Ki Pool

name: Zen Archer
description: The zen archer takes a weapon most other monks eschew and seeks perfection in the pull of a taut bowstring, the flex of a bow’s limbs, and the flight of an arrow fired true.
replaces_summary: Stunning Fist; Evasion; Maneuver Training; Still Mind; Purity of Body; Improved Evasion; Diamond Body; Tongue of the Sun and the Moon

name: Black Asp
description: The black asps are a sinister order of monks who train as assassins and infiltrators with no need for weapons to achieve their goals.
replaces_summary: Stunning Fist; Still Mind

name: Brazen Disciple
description: Brazen disciples use deception in their fighting style, and many go a step further, incorporating fire and smoke both mundane and magical.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; 1st, 6th-level Bonus Feats; Still Mind; 12th-level Ki Power; Perfect Self

name: Disciple of Wholeness
description: Disciples of wholeness train to bring themselves and others closer to physical and spiritual perfection.
replaces_summary: Ki Pool; Purity of Body; 4th, 12th, 14th level Ki Power

name: Elemental Monk
description: Elemental monks draw inspiration from genies to create their fighting style. Their adaptability and versatility make them capable of serving as emissaries of elemental balance.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Stunning Fist; Bonus Feats; Evasion; Purity of Body; 10th-level Ki Power; Ki Pool

name: Invested Regent
description: The invested regent can harness a divine spark to perform superhuman stunts, influence others, and escape injury.
replaces_summary: 1st-level Bonus Feat

description: When facing a master of the lifting hand, an enemy is likely to find itself sailing through the air or locked in a tortuous compliance hold.

description: A body contains many points where the flesh, mind, and spirit coincide. A monk of the mantis is skilled at manipulating these points.
replaces_summary: 2nd, 6th, 10th, 14th, and 18th-level Bonus Feats; 6th, 10th, 12th-level Ki Powers; Quivering Palm

	Steeped in traditions that trace their origins to the warrior-monks who trained under the tutelage of draconic masters in Tian Xia, scaled fists eschew passive introspection in favor of unshakable confidence. description: Softstrike monks employ a nonlethal but effective form of combat.
replaces_summary: Weapon Proficiency; Unarmed Strike; 6th, 10th-level Bonus Feats; Wholeness of Body; Quivering Palm; Empty Body

description: Whether studying at the feet of psychopomps or merely imitating the creatures' philosophy and methods, soul shepherds believe in safeguarding the Cycle of Souls.
replaces_summary: Stunning Fist; Evasion; Improved Evasion; 12th, 16th-level Bonus Feat

name: Windstep Master
description: Most monks are agile and fleet of foot, but few can rival a windstep master’s lightness of step.
replaces_summary: Stunning Fist; 4th-level Ki Power; Ki Pool

name: Frozen Shadow
description: Frozen shadows belong to a rare ninja clan operating in Avistan and they work as thieves, power brokers, and spies loosely guided by secretive masters with unknown agendas.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Ki Pool; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Gunpowder Bombardier
description: Ratfolk assassins and other ninjas with an affinity for gunpowder demonstrate masterful control of bombs, using the explosives to inflict major damage or impair their foes
replaces_summary: Ki Pool; 2nd-level Ninja Trick; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Hunting Serpent
description: Hunting serpents are specially trained killers to that hunt relentlessly and fight from the shadows.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; No Trace; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge; 10th, 12th, 16th-level Ninja Trick

name: Mask of the Living God
description: When the Living God needs to silence the voices of those heretics who question too loudly, these enforcers are the tools he employs.
replaces_summary: Weapon and Armor Proficiency; Poison Use; Ki Pool; No Trace

name: Petal Ninja
description: (Ghoran Only) Petal ninjas are a secretive clan of ghoran infiltrators, in touch with their plantlike nature to the point where they can change their forms, allowing them access into places that were previously impossible to reach.; ;  Petal ninja is an archetype available only to ghorans.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; 2nd-level Ninja Trick; No Trace; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge; Light Steps

name: Ancestral Aspirant
description: It is common for a noble to possess a deep pride for his family’s past, but when such self-importance gives way to obsession, an ancestral aspirant is born.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Implements; Object Reading; Outside Contact

name: Battle Host
description: Well versed in military history, battlefield lore, and the occult, a battle host forms a supernatural bond with a chosen weapon, suit of armor, or shield, from which he can channel psychic energy to cast spells, conjure the spirit of the object’s former owner, increase his own physical might, and produce a number of other remarkable abilities.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Implements; Mental Focus; Spellcasting; Implement Mastery; Magic Item Skill; Object Reading; Shift Focus; Magic Circles; Binding Circles; Fast Circles; Aura Sight; Outside Contact

name: Construct Collector
description: Construct collectors draw out a construct's spiritual presence and use it to gain unlikely power in the battlefield.
replaces_summary: 3rd, 9th, 15th-level Focus Power; Magic Circles; Outside Contact; Binding Circles; Fast Circles

name: Curator
description: Most occultists acquire and study antiques at a steady pace, learning new techniques one at a time. A lucky few join wealthy organizations replete with relics or inherit undocumented vaults full of historical treasures, and dabble in a wide variety of implements while mastering few
replaces_summary: Implements; 3rd-level Focus Power; Shift Focus; Outsider Contact; Magic Circles; Binding Circles; Fast Circles

name: Esoteric Initiate
description: While most occultists learn to draw power from a variety of implements and objects, esoteric initiates have focused their study entirely on items of antiquity connected to the teachings of the Esoteric Order of the Palatine Eye, believing such items to be more powerful than mundane items.
replaces_summary: Mental Focus; Implements; Shift Focus; Aura Sight

name: Extemporaneous Channeler
description: Extemporaneous channelers study the power of transformation and use items in unintended ways to awaken their potential.
replaces_summary: Weapon Proficiencies; Mental Focus; Shift Focus; Magic Circles; Outside Contact; Binding Circles; Fast Circles

name: Geomancer
description: A geomancer studies every type of land, deriving power from the differences between types of terrain.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Mental Focus; Implements; Magic Item Skill; Aura Sight; 7th and 13th-level Focus Powers

name: Haunt Collector
description: Haunt collectors use items haunted by their former owners to create ghostly presences imbued with psychic power.
replaces_summary: Implements; Aura Sight; Outside Contact; Magic Circles; Binding Circles; Fast Circles

name: Naturalist
description: A naturalist eschews the contact with outsiders that other occultists use as the core of their work. Instead, he attunes his mind to the natural spirits that exist everywhere, just out of sight for the unawakened mind.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Mental Focus; Magic Circles; Fast Circles; Outside Contact; Binding Circles

name: Necroccultist
description: Necroccultists’ fascination with death and the undead drives them to explore the forbidden necromantic arts as they search for secrets they can use to manipulate the natural cycle of life and death.
replaces_summary: Implements; Implement Mastery; Object Reading; Aura Sight; Outside Contact

name: Occult Historian
description: Many wilderness areas contain the remnants of ancient civilizations, with some waiting to be discovered and some held by fierce monsters or devious cults. An occult historian seeks these ruins not only for their esoteric artifacts but also to learn the structures’ secrets.
replaces_summary: Object Reading; Aura Sight; 3rd-level Focus Power; Outside Contact 2, 3, and 4

name: Panoply Savant
description: Some occultists specialize in a particular panoply, fully dedicating themselves to mastering the secrets of the psychic resonance of each of its component implements, as well as the way they interact with one another.
replaces_summary: Shift Focus; Outside Contact; Magic Circles; Binding Circles; Fast Circles

name: Planar Harmonizer
description: Planar harmonizers are masters of the planar symphony, forging bonds with tuning forks to unlock the true potential of these instruments
replaces_summary: Implements; Implement Mastery; Magic Item Skill; Shift Focus; 5th, 7th-level Focus Power; Magic Circles; Aura Sight

name: Psychodermist
description: Rather than tapping the psychic energy residing within esoteric items, psychodermists form supernatural bonds with trophies taken from creatures they have slain. Through these mementos, these occultists manifest not only their own magic, but also the unique powers of their fallen foes.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Implements; Magic Item Skill; Object Reading; Aura Sight; Outside Contact; Binding Circles; Fast Circles

name: Reliquarian
description: Not all occultists derive their power from psychic impressions left on objects. Some find faith first, and draw out the divine potential in religious relics.
replaces_summary: Weapon Proficiencies; Spells; Focus Powers; Implements; Knacks; Mental Focus

name: Secret Broker
description: Whether as spymasters, extortionists, political fixers, or puppet masters, secret brokers use their talents with objects to gain information, then leverage that information for their own purposes.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Implements; Implement Mastery; Magic Item Skill; Shift Focus; Magic Circles; Binding Circles; Fast Circles

name: Sha'ir
description: Sha’irs delve into the occult not through power over objects but instead via their connection with minor servitor genies from the elemental planes.
replaces_summary: Implements; Mental Focus; Spellcasting; Implement Mastery; Magic Item Skill; Object Reading; Aura Sight; Outside Contact

name: Silksworn
description: Silksworn draw their power from wearing luxurious garments and can be found in many noble courts throughout Golarion
replaces_summary: Spellcasting; Class Skills; Knacks; Implements; Weapon and Armor Proficiency; Outside Contact; Magic Circles; Binding Circles; Fast Circles; Implement Mastery

name: Talisman Crafter
description: Talisman crafters specialize- in the creation of seals, constructing master talismans to use as implements and inscribing wards with esoteric geometry.
replaces_summary: Implements; Magic Item Skill; Object Reading; Shift Focus; Aura Sight; 5th, 17th-level Focus Powers

name: Tome Eater
description: Tome eaters have learned how to tap into the latent psychic energy of the written word by physically devouring books and scrolls to create magical effects and gain mystical insights.
replaces_summary: Implements; Mental Focus; Spellcasting; Implement Mastery; Shift Focus; 6th-level Implement; Magic Circles; Outside Contact; Binding Circles; Fast Circles; Aura Sight

name: Ancient Lorekeeper
description: (Elf Only) The ancient lorekeeper is a repository for all the beliefs and vast knowledge of an elven people.
replaces_summary: Mystery Bonus Skills; 4th, 6th, 8th, 10th, 12th, 14th, 16th, and 18th-level Bonus Spells

name: Black-Blooded Oracle
description: The black-blooded oracle has been infused with the eerie influence of strange fluids that seep from the rock in the deepest pits of the Darklands, the so-called Black Blood of Orv.
replaces_summary: Oracle’s Curse

name: Community Guardian
description: (Halfling Only) The community guardian is chosen to protect and succor the weak and innocent within her community.
replaces_summary: Mystery Class Skills; 2nd, 4th, 6th, 10th, 12th level Mystery Bonus Spells; 1st, 3rd-level Revelations

name: Cyclopean Seer
description: A cyclopean seer draws power from the mysterious prophetic abilities of ancient giants.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Curse; Bonus Spells; 1st and 7th-level Revelations; Final Revelation

name: Divine Numerologist
description: Divine numerologists use esoteric mathematics and numbers traditions to peer into the past and future and extract meaningful portents.
replaces_summary: 10th, 12th, 14th, 16th, 18th-level Mystery Bonus Spells; 1st, 7th-level Revelation; Final Revelation

name: Dual-Cursed Oracle
description: All oracles are cursed to some degree, but some oracles bear an even heavier burden.
replaces_summary: Oracle’s Curse; Mystery Class Skills; Mystery Bonus Spells

name: Elementalist Oracle
description: An elementalist oracle has an affinity with the elements and elemental creatures. She can communicate with elemental creatures, and can eventually become an elemental herself.
replaces_summary: 4th, 6th, 8th, 10th ,12th, 18th-level Mystery Bonus Spells; 1st, 11th-level Revelation; Final Revelation

name: Enlightened Philosopher
description: The enlightened philosopher seeks enlightenment through compassion, moderation, and humility.
replaces_summary: Mystery Class Skills; Mystery Bonus Spells; 7th-level Revelation; Final Revelation

name: Hermit
description: A hermit is a recluse who gained her oracular powers from isolation in a deep desert, on a mountain peak, or in another secluded location.
replaces_summary: Mystery; Curse; 4th, 8th, 12th, 16th-level Bonus Spells; 1st, 7th-level Revelation

name: Inerrant Voice
description: An inerrant voice serves as a spiritual advisor to either a monarch or noble.
replaces_summary: 2nd, 4th, 8th, 14th-level Bonus Spells; 3rd-level Revelation

name: Keleshite Prophet
description: Keleshite prophets help guide and serve the vast Padishah Empire of Kelesh and its interests across Golarion.
replaces_summary: Mystery Skills; 6th, 10th, 12th, 16th, 18th-level Bonus Spells; 1st-level Revelation

name: Ocean's Echo
description: Although many merfolk claim deep connections to both art and the natural world, a rare few merfolk can manipulate the forces of nature and weave them into song.
replaces_summary: Mystery Bonus Skills; 4th, 8th, 10th, 12th, 14th-level Bonus Spells; 1st, 3rd, 15th-level Revelation

name: Pei Zin Practitioner
description: In distant Tian Xia, herbalists practice an obscure alchemical art known as Pei Zin herbalism, which involves medicinal and restorative techniques that are used throughout much of that continent.
replaces_summary: Mystery Class Skills; 1st-, 7th-level Revelation

name: Planar Oracle
description: A planar oracle has an affinity with one of the Outer Planes.
replaces_summary: Mystery Bonus Spells; 3rd-level Revelation; Final Revelation

name: Possessed Oracle
description: Some oracles are possessed by spirits, demons, or similar beings.
replaces_summary: Oracle’s Curse; Mystery Bonus Spells; 1st-level Revelation

name: Purifier
description: (Aasimar Only) The purifier seeks out signs of possession or mind control that manifest from unwilling (and often unwitting) servants for fiendish corruptors and their mortal minions.
replaces_summary: Bonus Spells; Spellcasting; 3rd, 7th, 11th-level Revelation

name: Reincarnated Oracle
description: (Samsaran Only) A reincarnated oracle draws her knowledge and power from the experiences of her previous lives.
replaces_summary: Oracle’s Curse; Bonus Spells; 1st, 3rd, or 7th-level Revelations (must pick two)

name: River Soul
description: River soul oracles derive their powers from their bonds with rivers, whether these waterways are rapid cascades or lazy channels plied by boats.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; 2nd, 6th, 8th, 10th ,18th-level Mystery Bonus Spells; Oracle's Curse; 1st, 11th-level Revelation; Final Revelation

name: Seeker
description: The seeker is obsessed with learning about their heritage and history, researching ancient texts and obscure ruins for clues.
replaces_summary: Mystery Class Skills, 3rd-level Revelation, 15th-level Revelation

name: Seer
description: While all oracles possess some ability at divination, the seer is a true prophet, able to see things as they really are, or will be.
replaces_summary: Mystery Bonus Spells; 1st-level Revelation; 3rd-level Revelation

name: Shigenjo
description: (Tengu Only) The shigenjo walks the path of enlightenment and transcendence by seeking oneness with the celestial spirits.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Bonus Spells; 7th-level Revelation; Final Revelation

name: Stargazer
description: Some oracles seek meaning in the pattern of the stars across the night sky, the trails of comets, and the eternal orbits of the planets.
replaces_summary: Mystery Bonus Spells; 1st-level Revelation; 7th-level Revelation

name: Tree Soul
description: Tree souls develop an affinity not just with trees but also with objects carved from them, feeling the essence of the tree’s spirit living within.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Weapon and Armor Proficiency; 2nd, 10th, 12th, 14th, 16th, 18th-level Mystery Bonus Spells; Oracle's Curse; 1st, 11th-level Revelation; Final Revelation

name: Banishing Warden
description: The banishing warden is an expert at fighting all types of evil outsiders and sending them back to their native planes.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; 3rd-level Mercy

name: Champion of the Cascade
description: Champions of the cascade are paladins who defend the freedom and purity of waterways, protecting the very existence of the civilizations they belong to.
replaces_summary: Armor Proficiency; Class Skills; Divine Health; Mercy; Divine Bond

name: Chaos Knight
description: (Ganzi only) Despite the entropic forces inherent in ganzis, some forgo a life of chaos, instead harnessing their heritage to enforce the power of law
replaces_summary: Lay on Hands; Mercy; Aura of Faith

name: Chosen One
description: Most paladins train for years at a temple to attain a holy status, but rarely, an emissary of the divine appears to one of humble origins and calls her directly to the charge.
replaces_summary: Divine Bond; Class Skills; Divine Grace; Smite Evil; Lay on Hands; Channel Positive Energy

name: Combat Healer Squire
description: The combat healer squire is always ready with bandages and potions.
replaces_summary: Detect Evil; Divine Grace; Divine Health

name: Divine Defender
description: Some paladins see themselves as the last line of defense between the teeming hordes of evil and the innocent folk trying to make a living in a harsh, unforgiving world.
replaces_summary: Mercy; Divine Bond

name: Divine Guardian
description: The divine guardian devotes herself to the protection and guardianship of one creature at a time, be that her liege lord, an allied cleric, or an innocent facing overwhelming danger at the hands of evil.
replaces_summary: Spellcasting; Detect Evil; Lay on Hands; Aura of Courage

name: Divine Hunter
description: The divine hunter prefers to engage evil from afar, striking down her foes before they can threaten her allies.
replaces_summary: Heavy Armor Proficiency; Aura of Courage; Divine Bond; 6th-level Mercy; Aura of Resolve; Aura of Justice; Aura of Righteousness

name: Dusk Knight
description: Some paladins focus their training to combat the shadows and use darkness to their advantage.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Smite Evil; Channel Positive Energy; Divine Bond; Aura of Resolve; Aura of Justice

name: Empyreal Knight
description: The empyreal knight dedicates her life to serving the celestial beings that guide mortals in their struggle toward the light.
replaces_summary: Divine Grace; Mercy; Lay on Hands; Channel Positive Energy; Divine Bond; Holy Champion

name: Faithful Wanderer
description: A faithful wanderer is self-sufficient and unobtrusive and learns to pick her battles carefully, lest her mission end abruptly in a blaze of glory
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Detect Evil; Aura of Good; Aura of Justice; Aura of Courage; Aura of Resolve; Aura of Faith; Aura of Righteousness; Smite Evil; Divine Bond; Holy Champion

name: Forest Preserver
description: Forest preservers are sacred defenders of the woodlands and of the wild creatures and natural beauty within them.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Aura of Courage; Divine Health; Channel Positive Energy; Aura of Resolve; Aura of Justice; Aura of Faith

name: Forgefather's Seeker
description: Torag sends his seekers to locate constructs that are used for evil or that become uncontrollable
replaces_summary: Deity; Detect Evil; Smite Evil; Aura of Justice; Aura of Faith; Holy Champion

name: Ghost Hunter
description: Ghost hunters devote their lives to eliminating malevolent spirits and putting them to rest.
replaces_summary: Smite Evil; 6th and 9th-level Mercies

name: Gray Paladin
description: Gray paladins have discovered that in a world of intrigue, a strict code of honor hinders their options
replaces_summary: Alignment Restriction; Code of Conduct; Aura of Good; Class Skills; Smite Evil; Divine Grace; Aura of Courage; Aura of Resolve; Aura of Rightousness; Divine Health; Channel Positive Energy; Aura of Justice

name: Holy Gun
description: Not all paladins are knights in shining armor. Holy guns roam the world searching for evil. And where they find it, they put it down.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Detect Evil; Smite Evil; Divine Bond

name: Holy Tactician
description: The holy tactician inspires her allies on the field of battle.
replaces_summary: Smite Evil; Divine Health; Divine Bond; Aura of Courage; Aura of Resolve; Aura of Justice; Holy Champion

name: Hospitaler
description: Paladins are known for their charity and for tending to the sick. The hospitaler takes to this calling above all others, spending much of her time healing the poor, and giving aid and succor to those in need.
replaces_summary: Smite Evil; Channel Positive Energy; Aura of Justice

name: Hunting Paladin
description: Hunting paladins are tenacious trackers and stealthy stalkers in pursuit of evildoers.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Armor Proficiencies; Smite Evil; Detect Evil; Spells; Aura of Resolve

name: Invigorator
description: Invigorators devote themselves to keeping their allies bodily whole by bolstering their health and spirits.
replaces_summary: Smite Evil; Aura of Justice; Holy Champion

name: Iomedaen Enforcer
description: Iomedaean enforcers have altered abilities that allow them to stand against the forces of chaos.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Detect Evil; Smite Evil; Aura of Faith; Aura of Righteousness; Holy Champion

name: Iroran Paladin
description: Iroran paladins meditate on self-perfection and train relentlessly, knowing that their example can inspire others to excel.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Aura of Good; Detect Evil; Smite Evil; Aura of Courage; Channel Positive Energy; Divine Bond; Aura of Justice

name: Knight of Coins
description: Knights of coins promote fair trade and stable commerce between cities and between nations.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Skill Ranks; Detect Evil; 3rd, 9th, 15th-level Mercy

name: Kraken Slayer
description: Triton kraken slayers oppose all underwater threats, but they especially target the tentacled monstrosities called krakens.
replaces_summary: Smite Evil; Divine Health; Divine Bond; Aura of Faith

name: Legate
description: Though they hold to the same strict paladin code, legates present themselves not as armed crusaders, but as peaceful envoys and advisors, bringing wisdom garnered from the long, stable history of their nation.
replaces_summary: Mercy

name: Martyr
description: Martyrs shoulder the blackest suffering of the world onto themselves in order to help others and inspiring their allies to achieve heights of valor rather than seeking the glory for themselves.
