replaces_summary: Smite Evil; Divine Grace; Aura of Courage; Aura of Resolve; Aura of Righteousness; Divine Health; Lay on Hands; Mercy

name: Mind Sword
description: Veterans of mystical battles against demons of the Worldwound, mind swords merge psychic and divine power, probing and cleansing minds while their telekinetic blades cleave demonic flesh.
replaces_summary: Lay on Hands; Mercy; Channel Positive Energy

name: Pearl Seeker
description: Some paladins are called to their faith by strange empathic visions and dreams that beckon them to the oceans and beyond.
replaces_summary: Armor Proficiency; Class Skills; Detect Evil; Aura of Courage; Aura of Resolve; Spells; Divine Bond; Channel Energy

name: Redeemer
description: (Half-Orc Only) As most half-orcs are outcasts, a half-orc paladin recognizes that often those who are monstrous are not necessarily evil and that sometimes even those who are evil became that way because of circumstances and misfortune.
replaces_summary: Smite Evil; Detect Evil; Aura of Resolve; Aura of Justice

name: Sacred Servant
description: Paladins as a general rule, venerate the gods of good and purity, but some take this a step further, dedicating themselves to a specific deity and furthering the cause of the faith.
replaces_summary: Smite Evil; Divine Bond; Aura of Resolve

name: Sacred Shield
description: When faced by evil, the sacred shield reaches first not for a weapon, but for her trusty shield. With her faith, she can ward others from harm.
replaces_summary: Smite Evil; Channel Positive Energy; Divine Bond; Aura of Justice; Holy Champion

name: Scion of Talmandor
description: A scion of Talmandor serve by offering assistance to the oppressed and facilitating the peaceful transfer of power from the few to the many.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Divine Grace; Divine Bond; Aura of Resolve; Aura of Justice

name: Shining Knight
description: While paladins often are seen mounted atop a loyal steed, the shining knight is the true symbol of mounted bravery.
replaces_summary: Divine Health; Aura of Justice

name: Silver Champion
description: Drake-riding paladins who serve as priests of Apsu.
replaces_summary: Divine Bond; Channel Energy; Aura of Justice; Aura of Righteousness; Spells

name: Soul Sentinel
description: Soul sentinels use the strength of their resolve to stem the tide of corruption and insanity, as they believe that the greatest tragedy is a soul lost to evil when it was within their power to save it.
replaces_summary: 6th, 12th-level Mercy; Aura of Justice

name: Stonelord
description: (Dwarf Only) A stonelord is a devoted sentinel of dwarven enclaves, drawing the power of the earth and ancient stone to protect her people.
replaces_summary: Smite Evil; Divine Grace; 3rd, 9th, 12th, 15th, 18th-level Mercies; Spells; Channel Positive Energy; Divine Bond; Aura of Justice; Holy Champion

name: Sword of Valor
description: A paladin who chooses to follow in Iomedae's footsteps can become a sword of valor.
replaces_summary: Divine Grace; 6th-level Mercy; Aura of Justice

name: Tempered Champion
description: Tempered champions are trained in the Tempering Hall in Absalom to master their deities’ favored weapons.
replaces_summary: Spellcasting

name: Tortured Crusader
description: Though tortured crusaders may be beaten and battered, body, mind, and soul, they still somehow continue through torment and hardship. Despite setbacks and impossible odds, they shine like candles even in the darkest times
replaces_summary: Key Ability Score; Skills; Detect Evil; Divine Grace; Smite Evil; Lay on Hands; Channel Energy; Divine Bond; Aura of Courage; Aura of Resolve; Aura of Faith; Aura of Righteousness; Aura of Justice ; Lay on Hands

name: Tranquil Guardian
description: (Aasimar Only) A tranquil guardian is a missionary of peace and tranquility, a soothing voice of succor in a violent and dangerous world.
replaces_summary: Smite Evil; Aura of Courage; Divine Bond; Aura of Resolve; Aura of Justice; Holy Champion

name: Undead Scourge
description: Undead are an abomination in the eyes of the just and righteous. It is no surprise then that there are some paladins that dedicate themselves to wiping these unholy terrors from the world.
replaces_summary: Aura of Resolve; Aura of Justice

name: Vindictive Bastard
description: {Ex-paladin archetype) A vindictive bastard is a paladin that strikes out for retribution and revenge, far more interested in tearing down those who have harmed her or her companions than furthering a distant deity’s cause.
replaces_summary: Aura; Detect Evil; Smite Evil; Divine Grace; Lay on Hands; Divine Health; Mercy; Channel Energy; Divine Bond; Aura of Justice; Aura of Faith; Aura of Righteousness; Holy Champion

name: Virtuous Bravo
description: Virtuous bravos rely on their wit and grace rather than might and strong armor.
replaces_summary: Armor Proficiency; Smite Evil; Mercy; Spellcasting

name: Warrior of the Holy Light
description: Some paladins use their gifts to focus on the holy light that shines within their souls.
replaces_summary: Spellcasting; Aura of Faith

name: Wilderness Warden
description: Wilderness wardens are vigilant guardians of natural places of all kinds, from mountain peaks to tangled thickets.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Divine Grace; Smite Evil; Aura of Courage; 3rd, 9th, 15th-level Mercy; Spells; Aura of Resolve; Aura of Justice

name: Amnesiac
description: The amnesiac once possessed great psychic power, but mental blocks—resulting from either a traumatic event or intentional implantation—have caused her to forget what she knew before
replaces_summary: Spellcasting

name: Esoteric Starseeker
description: An esoteric starseeker is a psychic who focuses her studies on uncovering the secrets of the sky and the constellations of the Cosmic Caravan.
replaces_summary: Discipline Spells; 1st, 11th-level Phrenic Amplifications; Major Amplifications

name: Formless Adept
description: Formless adepts master their psychic powers to unshackle themselves from the confines of the flesh and empower their spellcasting.
replaces_summary: Phrenic Pool; Psychic Discipline; Discipline Powers; 1st and 15th-level Phrenic Amplification; Discipline Spells; Remade Self

name: Magaambyan Telepath
description: Followers of Old-Mage Jatembe’s more esoteric wisdom instead focus on the occult connection between all living creatures and the resonance between the natural and supernatural world.
replaces_summary: Discipline Spells; 1st, 11th-level Phrenic Amplifications; Telepathic Bond; Telepathy

name: Mutation Mind
description: Exposure to unintended spell effects, curses, or sources of radiation cause some to manifest psychic powers. When a mutation mind uses her psychic abilities, her physical body changes under the stress, and she risks losing control if she pushes too far.
replaces_summary: 1st-level Phrenic Amplification; Phrenic Amplifications; Major Amplifications

name: Psychic Duelist
description: Psychic duelists hone their mental powers for combat, much as warriors do with their blades.
replaces_summary: 4th-level Discipline Spell; 7th-level Phrenic Amplification; Phrenic Amplifications; Telepathic Bond; Telepathy

name: Psychic Marauder
description: The psychic marauder’s powers come hand in hand with madness, either from exposure to alien psyches or from terrifying glimpses into the cosmos that the psychic can’t suppress.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Detect Thoughts; 3rd, 11th, 19th-level Phrenic Amplification; Telepathic Bond; Remade Self

name: Terror Weaver
description: A number of exceptional Darklands half-orcs have developed potent psychic abilities, unlocked by generations of exposure to a specific combination of mind-altering fungi and radiation
replaces_summary: Detect Thoughts; Telepathic Bond; 11th, 15th, 19th-level Phrenic Amplifications

name: Abendego Diver
description: Named for the vicious and unending storm in the southern Arcadian Ocean, Abendego divers have pushed their bodies to their limits, holding their breath to expand their lung capacity and swimming until ocean travel is second nature to them.
replaces_summary: Weapon and Armor Proficiencies; Track; Wild Empathy; Favored Terrain; Woodland Stride; Swift Tracker; Master Hunter

name: Battle Scout
description: Though regular outdoorsmen might suffice in many cases, some large forces look to battle scouts to keep the body of their troops safe and prepared for the terrain and whatever dangers that terrain hides.
replaces_summary: Hunter’s Bond; 2nd, 3rd, 4th Favored Enemies; Master Hunter

name: Beast Master
description: Some rangers, particularly those in primitive lands or who were raised by animals, have unusually strong bonds with animals.
replaces_summary: Hunter's Bond; 6th-level Combat Style Feat; Camouflage

name: Blightwarden
description: Some rangers feel so closely connected to their homelands that they stand as guardians against pervasive corruptions that pervert those lands beyond the point of recognition.
replaces_summary: Favored Enemy; Wild Empathy; 1st Favored Terrain; Endurance;

name: Bow Nomad
description: (Kasatha Only) Thanks to their four arms, kasathas can master a combat form native to their home world and rarely witnessed on Golarion—wielding two bows at once.
replaces_summary: Wild Empathy; Combat Style; Endurance; Favored Terrain; 6th-level Combat Feat; Camouflage; Hide in Plain Sight

name: Cinderwalker
description: Cinderwalkers traverse burning wastelands and restless mountains, thriving in lands most creatures avoid.
replaces_summary: 1st Favored Terrain; Wild Empathy; Hunter’s Bond; Woodland Stride

name: Code Runner
description: A code runner specializes in the subtle and speedy transfer of secret messages.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Wild Empathy; Hunter's Bond

name: Corpse Hunter
description: Many rangers count the undead among their favored enemies, but some make a full-time job out of hunting down and destroying these walking corpses.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Spells; Favored Enemies; Woodland Stride; Swift Tracker

name: Dandy
description: The antithesis of the gruff and wild woodsmen, a dandy takes effort to master the subtle etiquette of noble courts, the whispers of dark rumors, and the customs of the cultured world.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Favored Enemy; Wild Empathy; Favored Terrain; Hunter's Bond; Spellcasting; Woodland Stride

name: Darklands Sailor
description: Those plying the lightless waters of the Darklands learn to use sound to augment their compromised vision, providing insight into the hazards lurking below the surface.
replaces_summary: Track; Favored Terrain; Woodland Stride; Swift Tracker; Camouflage; Hide in Plain Sight

name: Deep Walker
description: Some rangers devote their lives to the woods, becoming hunters, protectors, and wilderness guides among its diverse terrain types.
replaces_summary: Favored Terrain; Woodland Stride; Camouflage; Hide in Plain Sight

name: Dragon Hunter
description: The quintessential dragonslayers, dragon hunters are experts at forging through the wilds in search of great wyrms.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Track; 5th, 10th, 15th, 20th-level Favored Enemies

name: Drake Warden
description: Some rangers specialize in dealing with rambunctious younger drakes and thanks to their methods, their drakes are both fiercely loyal and extremely useful for scouting and stealth missions.
replaces_summary: Hunter's Bond; 2nd Favored Enemy; Swift Tracker

name: Dungeon Rover
description: These rangers specialize in surviving hostile subterranean environs. They are well equipped to avoid traps, spot secret passages, and deal with inimical dungeon tenants.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Track; Wild Empathy; Hunter’s Bond; Woodland Stride; Swift Tracker

name: Dusk Stalker
description: (Fetchling Only) Hunters and guides through the Shadow Plane, dusk stalkers are rangers that thrive in shadow.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Favored Terrain; Hunter’s Bond; Camouflage

name: Elemental Envoy
description: An elemental envoy travels the Elemental Planes. He has learned to protect himself from their many dangers, and he models his fighting style after their teachings.
replaces_summary: Combat Style; Favored Terrain

name: Falconer
description: Rangers have always enjoyed a special bond with a specific animal, but the falconer takes this bond to a deeper level.
replaces_summary: Wild Empathy; Hunter’s Bond; 6th-level Combat Style Feat

name: Flamewarden
description: Emulating the blazing phoenix, flamewardens sweep through the world like a selective forest fire, burning away corruption, evil, and those who cling to decay.
replaces_summary: Hunter's Bond; Spells; Evasion; Camouflage; Improved Evasion; Master Hunter

name: Fortune-Finder
description: Vanaras often leave home to seek fortune and sate their curiosity regarding the mysterious lands that lie beyond their native territories. The most skilled of these thrillseekers are called ba-sadhaks, or fortune-finders.
replaces_summary: Track; Hunter's Bond; Woodland Stride; Quarry; Improved Quarry; Master Hunter

name: Freebooter
description: A freebooter is a natural leader, a pirate who works well with a variety of people and in a variety of roles.
replaces_summary: Favored Enemy; Hunter's Bond; Woodland Stride

name: Galvanic Saboteur
description: Many Kellid locals blame Kevoth-Kul’s change on the influence of the Technic League and view both the League and their mechanical minions with a mix of fear and distrust. Some Kellid rangers have since developed skills to defeat the League’s robotic enforcers and devious arcanists.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Favored Enemy; Wild Empathy; Favored Terrain; Woodland Stride; Swift Tracker; Camouflage; Hide in Plain Sight

name: Groom
description: The groom attends to the needs of the party’s mounts and beasts of burden during their adventures, and guards these animals while the party goes underground.
replaces_summary: Track; Endurance; 1st Favored Terrain

name: Guide
description: Many rangers are loners, but some choose to use their familiarity with the land to guide others safely through the wilderness.
replaces_summary: Favored Enemy; Hunter's Bond; Evasion; Quarry; Improved Quarry; Improved Evasion

name: Guildbreaker
description: Guildbreakers stoke their enmity for their foes, and they train to track the movements of rival organizations, and even to infiltrate these groups to gather secret intelligence.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Favored Enemy; Wild Empathy; Hunter's Bond; Woodland Stride

name: Horse Lord
description: Rangers of the plains use horses or other riding beasts to hunt their lands, forging a near-mystical relationship with their mounts.
replaces_summary: Hunter's Bond; Camouflage; Hide in Plain Sight

name: Ilsurian Archer
description: Ilsurian archers maintain the strong tradition of archery first developed by soldiers in the service of Ilsur, who raised a small army with which he intended to overthrow the monarch of Korvosa after the fall of the Chelish Empire.
replaces_summary: Track; Wild Empathy; Combat Style; Spellcasting; Swift Tracking; Quarry; Improved Quarry

name: Infiltrator
description: Some rangers study their favored enemies and learn their ways, applying this knowledge to their own abilities and using their foes’ strengths against them.
replaces_summary: Favored Terrain

name: Jungle Lord
description: Jungle lords are tempered by their harsh and unforgiving environments into something more than ordinary men and women.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Weapon and Armor Proficiency; Favored Enemy; Favored Terrain; Hunter's Bond; Spells; 5th, 10th, 15th, 18th-level Favored Terrain; Quarry; Improved Quarry; Camouflage; Master Hunter

name: Lantern Lighter
description: Lantern lighters were originally entrusted with secretly eliminating the drow and quarantining any knowledge of their existence, but in the new Lantern Bearer era, the lantern lighters have refocused their efforts on helping to rescue those imprisoned by the drow, or even to help individual drow who seek to escape the cruelties of their society for a chance at redemption on the surface.
replaces_summary: Wild Empathy; Endurance; Favored Terrain; Woodland Stride; Camouflage; Hide in Plain Sight

name: Nirmathi Irregular
description: An irregular troop from Nirmathas, this ranger chooses to focus on the magic of stealth.
replaces_summary: Normal Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Favored Enemy; Favored Terrain; Altered Spell List

name: Planar Scout
description: Planar scouts are rangers specializing in traversing the planes—and surviving their journeys.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Wild Empathy; Favored Terrain; Hunter's Bond; Evasion; Improved Evasion

name: Poison Darter
description: In dense jungles, foliage and tight spaces hinder all but the most deceptively humble weapons: blowguns and poison.
replaces_summary: Favored Enemy; Wild Empathy; Combat Style; Hunter's Bond; Master Hunter

name: Raven Master
description: Raven masters serve as messengers and spies for the Silver Ravens, eschewing a ranger’s usual connection to the natural world to form a mystic bond with their birds and to grant both animal and master a number of specialized abilities
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Wild Empathy; Hunter's Bond; Woodland Stride; Swift Tracker; Camouflage; Spellcasting

name: Realm Wanderer
description: These rangers take to heart the lessons they’ve learned from their vastly contrasting experiences and know that creatively embracing this wisdom is sometimes the difference between life and death.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Track; Hunter's Bond; Swift Tracker; Master Hunter

name: Sable Company Marine
description: Sable Company marines receive their training at the elite Endrin Military Academy in Korvosa. A large portion of their education is the handling and riding of hippogriffs, the iconic mounts of members of the company.
replaces_summary: Hunter’s Bond

name: Sentinel
description: A sentinel specializes in heightening all of her senses to expose spies and those who work for opposing factions, rooting out all who attempt to slip past her evervigilant watch.
replaces_summary: Wild Empathy; Hunter's Bond; Woodland Stride; 6th-level Combat Style Feat; Quarry; Improved Quarry

name: Shapeshifter
description: Most rangers venture into the wilderness, but there are some who let the wilderness seep into them.
replaces_summary: Favored Terrain; Camouflage; Master Hunter

name: Skirmisher
description: Many rangers rely on spells, but there are some who eschew aid from divine powers for their own reasons.
replaces_summary: Spells

name: Spirit Ranger
description: Some rangers nurture a connection with the spirits that reside in all things.
replaces_summary: Hunter's Bond; Camouflage

name: Stormwalker
description: Rangers who walk in the tempest unafraid draw the power of the storm into themselves and become stormwalkers.
replaces_summary: Combat Style; Hunter's Bond; Swift Tracker; Quarry; Improved Evasion; Improved Quarry

name: Summit Sentinel
description: Some rangers eschew the path of the swift skirmisher and embrace the implacable strength of the mountain.
replaces_summary: Combat Style; Favored Terrain; Swift Tracker; Hide in Plain Sight

name: Sword-Devil
description: Agile, vengeful, and deadly, a sword-devil ﬁghts with precision and grace, channeling the difficult lessons of a star-crossed life into unparalleled battle prowess.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Favored Enemy; Favored Terrain; Hunter's Bond; Spells; Quarry; Improved Quarry; Master Hunter

name: Tanglebriar Demonslayer
description: Though they are sworn foes of Treerazer and his kin, Tanglebriar demonslayers are known to venture forth from Kyonin to broaden their expertise in the destruction of demons.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Favored Enemy; Endurance; Spells; Quarry; Improved Quarry

name: Tidal Hunter
description: The tidal hunter draws strength from the fish and other wildlife of the deep.
replaces_summary: Wild Empathy; 6th-level Combat Style Feat; Track; Camouflage; Favored Terrain; Woodland Stride; Improved Evasion

name: Toxic Herbalist
description: Saviors and destroyers in equal measure, toxic herbalists use nature’s bounty to craft remedies and poisons.
replaces_summary: Wild Empathy; Endurance; Hunter's Bond; Swift Tracker; Quarry; Improved Quarry

name: Toxophilite
description: The toxophilite emulates the sharpshooting rangers of legend, who could pin a fly to the wall with a single shot or split one arrow with another.
replaces_summary: Wild Empathy; Combat Style; Endurance; Quarry; Improved Quarry

name: Transporter
description: Transporters specialize in smuggling people as efficiently as possible through harsh and difficult environments.
replaces_summary: Wild Empathy; Favored Terrain; Hunter's Bond; Woodland Stride

name: Trapper
description: A trapper is a ranger who focuses exclusively on traps, rather than learning conventional magic.
replaces_summary: Spells

name: Trophy Hunter
description: Some rangers have taken up the mysteries of black powder in order to become big game hunters.
replaces_summary: Wild Empathy; Combat Feat Style; Hunter’s Bond

name: Urban Ranger
description: For the urban ranger, the streets and sewers of the city are just as dangerous as the barren wastelands or the deep forests.
replaces_summary: Favored Terrain; Endurance; Woodland Stride; Camouflage; Hide in Plain Sight

name: Warden
description: All rangers have a bond with the wilderness, but the warden may have the strongest. This protector sits guard in the middle of the wilderness, keeping a lookout for any dangers that might spill from the deeper wilderness beyond, and protecting civilization from the savagery of nature—and vice versa.
replaces_summary: Favored Enemy; Combat Style Feats; Hunter’s Bond; Master Hunter

name: Wave Warden
description: (Merfolk Only) The wave warden patrols beneath the sea, preserving the safety and secrets of merfolk communities.
replaces_summary: Track; Combat Style; Favored Terrain; Woodland Stride; Swift Tracker

name: Wild Shadow
description: (Half-Elf Only) The isolation that some half-elves feel leads them to live a life of isolation amid the wild places of the world.
replaces_summary: Track; Wild Empathy; Favored Terrain; Hunter’s Bond; Woodland Stride; Quarry; Camouflage; Improved Quarry

name: Wild Soul
description: Wild souls hail from primitive roots in the wild and venture to more civilized lands and learn their ways.
replaces_summary: Code of Conduct; Favored Enemy; Swift Tracker; Quarry; Improved Quarry; Master Hunter

name: Wild Stalker
description: The wild stalker forsakes the bonds of community and lives in the trackless wilds far from others of his kind, or perhaps grew up there, never knowing of civilization as anything more than his enemy.
replaces_summary: Favored Enemy; 2nd-level Combat Style Feat; Hunter’s Bond

name: Wilderness Explorer
description: Wilderness explorers are adept at making peaceful contact with remote societies.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Wild Empathy; Hunter's Bond; Swift Tracker; Favored Terrain; Quarry; Improved Quarry

name: Wilderness Medic
description: Wilderness medics know a variety of ways they can supply and preserve a rebellion or similar movement without requiring any resources that might make them easier to track down.
replaces_summary: Wild Empathy; Endurance; Evasion; Hunter's Bond; Improved Evasion

name: Witchguard
description: Witchguards are the sworn defenders of the White Witches of Irrisen.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Hunter’s Bond; Endurance; Spells; Woodland Stride

name: Yokai Hunter
description: Yokai hunters possess a supernatural tether to the spirit worlds that shroud mortal existence.
replaces_summary: Favored Enemy; Woodland Stride; Camouflage

name: Acrobat
description: Whether they are daring thieves, infiltrating assassins, or intrepid spies, proper training in acrobatics is a valuable boon for rogues.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Trap Sense

name: Bandit
description: Bandits tend to have a variety of skills; sometimes these skills are similar to a ranger’s, but a bandit’s tactics always deal with surprise followed by intimidation, with the clear threat of naked force if intimidation does not do the trick.
replaces_summary: Uncanny Dodge

name: Bekyar Kidnapper
description: A Bekyar kidnapper is not only adept at hiding herself in thick jungles and quickly subduing her prey, but also at spreading rumors of hidden ruins, buried treasure, and other such valuables that might lure an unwary foreigner into her traps.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Trap Sense

name: Bellflower Irrigator
description: Rather than focusing on saving slaves, Bellflower irrigators seek to defeat slavery by aiming to eliminate the source.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; 4th-level Rogue Talent; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Burglar
description: Adept at infiltration, trap removal, and lock picking, burglars prey on the homes of the wealthy and forgotten tombs alike.
replaces_summary: Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Carnivalist
description: Carnivalists train their miniature minions to perform acts of larceny and often hide their true talents behind theatrical sideshows.
replaces_summary: 2nd, 4th, 6th-level Rogue Talents; Sneak Attack; Trap Sense

name: Cat Burglar
description: (Catfolk Only) Cat burglars are masters of breaking and entering, using their feline grace to make it seem as though no crime was ever committed in the first place.
replaces_summary: Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Chameleon
description: An absolute master of disguise, a chameleon effortlessly blends into any environment.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Trap Sense

name: Charlatan
description: A charlatan is a master of lies and deception.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Trap Sense

name: Consigliere
description: Consiglieres act as trusted mediators and administrators for crime families and various criminal organizations, and many serve as the right hand to the leaders of such groups.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Trapfinding; Evasion; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge; Trap Sense; 10th-level Rogue Talent

name: Construct Saboteur
description: The construct saboteur harnesses arcane energy to combat the golems and eldritch guardians that prowl the depths of ancient ruins and forgotten tombs.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Trapfinding; 2nd-level Rogue Talent; Trap Sense

name: Cutpurse
description: Cutpurses are often trained by guilds to collect the guild’s tax from local businesses on a daily basis, whether through intimidation or pickpocketing. Some find themselves taking up the mantle of adventurer, and their talents are generally appreciated in this role, but the cutpurse is still the first person her companions look to when an item goes missing.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Trap Sense

name: Dark Lurker
description: Dark lurkers use darkness as an ally.
replaces_summary: Evasion; 2nd, 8th, 14th, and 20th-level Rogue Talents

name: Deadly Courtesan
description: (Vishkanya Only) Skilled at manipulation and diversion, the deadly courtesan builds up those around her and periodically takes them down.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; 2nd-level Rogue Trick; Trap Sense; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Desert Raider
description: Desert raider learn the secrets of the deserts and become inured to their dangers.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Trapfinding; Trap Sense

name: Discretion Specialist
description: Discretion specialists deal with bodies, inconvenient witnesses, and other loose ends.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Trap Sense/Danger Sense; 4th, 12-th level Rogue Talents; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Dreamthief
description: Each dreamthief bears a focused fragment of emotion, known as a dreamshard, within her soul. This metaphysical crystal grants a dreamthief supernatural powers, which she supplements with skill and grace.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Sneak Attack; Master Strike; Trap Sense; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge; 12th-level Rogue Talent

name: Driver
description: A driver makes her living driving vehicles in the service of those who can pay for her considerable talents.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Trap Sense

name: Earthshadow
description: In the centuries since the Quest for the Sky, the mystic lore the dwarves call earthcraft has been passed down through the generations, and its practitioners are called earthshadows.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Evasion; 4th, 8th, 12th, 16th-level Rogue Talents

name: Eldritch Raider
description: (Gillman Only) An eldritch raider is a rogue who seeks to unravel the mysteries of the destruction of the gillmen’s homeland.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Skill Ranks; 2nd-level Rogue Talent; Trap Sense

name: Eldritch Scoundrel
description: Students of arcane magic, legerdemain, and stealth, eldritch scoundrels are a rare breed of adventurer most commonly found seeking lost and valuable arcane writings in the ruins of fallen empires, such as Thassilon or the Jistka Imperium.
replaces_summary: Armor Proficiency; Skills; Trap Sense; Sneak Attack; Rogue Talents; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Escapologist
description: The escapologist excels at escaping any confinement, whether physical, magical, or mental. Few restraints can hold an escapologist for long.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: False Medium
description: The false medium is a master of pretending to have occult powers in order to manipulate the emotions and atmosphere of the people and places around him, often reaping a healthy reward from the bereaved, who find solace in his comforting lies.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; 2nd-level Rogue Talent; Trap Sense

name: Fey Prankster
description: (Gathlain) Fey pranksters specialize in tricking others to teach a lesson or for their own entertainment.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; 2nd, 6th, 8th, and 12th-level Rogue Talents; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Filcher
description: (Halfling Only) Whether cutting purses in the midst of combat or replacing prized items with fakes under the noses of their owners, the filcher is the master of the quick and quiet steal.
replaces_summary: Evasion; Trap Sense 1-6; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Galtan Agitator
description: Galtan agitators fight mightily to cast down the enemies of their ideals within the shambles of the tumultuous nation of Galt.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Trap Sense; 4th, 8th, 12th-level Rogue Talent

name: Guerrilla
description: Guerrillas fight against oppressive leadership and governments including Chelish colonization in Sargava and the power structures in Cheliax proper, Nidal, and Razmiran
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Evasion; Trap Sense; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Guild Agent
description: Guild agents are members of a thieves’ guild or other shadowy organization. What they lose in independence, they more than make up for in benefits gained due to their association’s infrastructure.
replaces_summary: Evasion; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Evasion; Master Strike

name: Gun Smuggler
description: Gun smugglers are adept at procuring firearms and concealing their secret weapons
replaces_summary: Weapon Proficiencies; Trapfinding; Sneak Attack; Trap Finding; 6th-level Rogue Talent; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Heister
description: Notorious for their stealth, imagination, and elaborately planned thefts and capers, heisters specialize in breaking into secure locations, stealing items of value, and escaping undetected.
replaces_summary: 2nd, 4th, 8th-level Rogue Talent; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Investigator
description: Not all rogues work against the law. Investigators use their skills to serve the law, often in the employ of nobles or in the pursuit of noble causes.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding

name: Kintargo Rebel
description: Whenever the Chelish navy sails into the port, these rebels are quick to appear agreeably subservient in order to conceal their noncompliance and avoid retribution.
replaces_summary: Trap Sense; Uncanny Dodge

name: Kitsune Trickster
description: (Kitsune Only) The kitsune trickster combines her sharpened wit with minor arcane powers of charm and persuasion.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Trap Sense

name: Knife Master
description: The knife master is a trained killer who specializes in close-up combat and the wave and weave of knife fighting.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Sneak Attack; Trap Sense

name: Makeshift Scrapper
description: Some combatants specialize in the study of certain weapons, but those without formal training often learn to survive a fight by making brilliant use of whatever is lying around.
replaces_summary: Weapon Proficiencies; Trapfinding; Trap Sense; 12th-level Rogue Talent

name: Master of Disguise
description: A master of disguise believes that never letting her adversaries know her true identity protects her from bad luck or missteps on a job.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; 2nd, 10th-level Rogue Talent

name: Nameless Shadow
description: Where bandits and tyrants prey on merchants and caravans, the nameless shadow preys on bandits and tyrants.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; 2nd, 4th, 6th, 8th, 10th, 14th, 18th-level Rogue Talents

name: Needler
description: Needlers use subtlety and misdirection to deliver deadly poisons to unsuspecting victims in creative and unorthodox ways.
replaces_summary: Evasion; Uncanny Dodge; 6th-level Rogue Talent; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Numerian Scavenger
description: A Numerian scavenger lives for the discovery of new technological wonders scraped from the depths of the strange ruins, metal caverns, and outlandish junkyards of Numeria.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Okeno Liberator
description: The sight of yellow sails on the Inner Sea signals the approach of a ship from Okeno, home port of the most ruthless and feared of all slaving operations. Most who see these sails flee in terror, but some freedom fighters actually endeavor to be captured, knowing that being taken to Stonespine Island in manacles is the easiest way to infiltrate the city, liberate other slaves, and lead them to freedom.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Trap Sense; Uncanny Dodge

name: Phantom Thief
description: Phantom thieves come from the ranks of the elite, having grown bored with their finery, and seek thrills from acts of daring, skill, and crime.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Sneak Attack; Trapfinding; Trap Sense; Master Strike

name: Pirate
description: A pirate breaks from the confines of country and king to commit her crimes upon the high seas.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; 2nd-level Rogue Talent; Trapfinding

name: Planar Sneak
description: A planar sneak finds creative ways to defy conventional limitations when trying to overcome extraplanar obstacles.
replaces_summary: Trapsense; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Poisoner
description: The poisoner knows poison is just a tool toward an end, and is no different than any other weapon.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Trap Sense

name: Rake
description: The rake is a rogue who is open about her skills and talents, often to the point of being boastful.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Trap Sense

name: Relic Raider
description: Relic raiders are rogues who specialize in recovering treasures from ruins and tombs, some in a quest for knowledge, others out of a desire for riches.
replaces_summary: Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: River Rat
description: River rats are skilled at hiding amid reeds and roots, striking the unwary from the shallows as others would from the shadows.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Trapfinding; Trap Sense

name: Roof Runner
description: A specialized urban acrobat, the roof runner makes her home high atop the spires and gables of great cities.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Trap Sense

name: Rotdrinker
description: Poisons are common tools in a rogue’s trade, and some poison-using rogues are so frequently exposed to these toxins that they have developed resistances to particular poisons.
replaces_summary: Evasion; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Sanctified Rogue
description: The sanctified rogue serves a higher purpose, acting as a representative of a church or cult, or following the tenets of a specific faith or deity.
replaces_summary: Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Sapper
description: The sapper is a trained siege engineer and can deal with traps and other obstacles given plenty of time.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; 2nd and 4th-level Rogue Talents

name: Scout
description: Scouts frequently roam the wilderness, often banding together as bandits, but sometimes serving as guides, as trailblazers, or as companions to a ranger or barbarian warrior.
replaces_summary: Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Scroll Scoundrel
description: Using knowledge from extensive research, the scroll scoundrel exploits overconfidence and predictability to better stay alive.
replaces_summary: 2nd-level Rogue Talent, Trap Sense, Uncanny Dodge, Improved Uncanny Dodge, 10th-level Rogue Talent

name: Sczarni Swindler
description: If life is a game, the Sczarni play to win. When skill and luck aren’t enough, cheating is often the best option.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Evasion; Trap Sense; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Seeker of the Lost
description: Aquatic elven adventurers descending into the ruins of sunken Azlant developed techniques to ease their explorations, and they now share these techniques among a loose group of acquaintances calling themselves the seekers of the lost.
replaces_summary: 2nd-level Rogue Talent; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Shadow Scion
description: Shadow scions have learned the secrets of light and dark and draw power from the shadows themselves.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; 2nd, 8th, 14th-level Rogue Talent; Master Strike

name: Shadow Walker
description: Shadow walkers are comfortable in light, darkness, and the shadows in between.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; 2nd-level Rogue Talent; Trap Sense; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Sharper
description: Sharpers use trickery and deception to part an owner from his valuables.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; 2nd, 4th, 6th, 8th, 10th, 16th-level Rogue Talent; Trap Sense;

name: Skulking Slayer
description: (Half-Orc Only) Pushed into a life of crime by the society around them, half-orcs gravitate toward criminal activities that suit them best.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiency; Class Skills; Trapfinding; Rogue Talent; Advanced Rogue Talent; Trap Sense 1-4

name: Sly Saboteur
description: Sly saboteurs are irregular soldiers who specialize in sabotage and surprise attacks.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Trap Sense; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge;

name: Smuggler
description: Smugglers specialize in moving contraband from one area to another while avoiding detection by authorities.
replaces_summary: Trap Sense; Trapfinding; Evasion

name: Snare Setter
description: (Kobold Only) Deep in darkness, the snare setter constructs his deadly traps and brutal mechanisms to confound and destroy any who try to invade his domain.
replaces_summary: Sneak Attack; Trapfinding; Master Strike

name: Sniper
description: Some say that the sniper is the worst kind of assassin: a killer who waits silently in the shadows and then strikes from a distance without remorse.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Trap Sense

name: Snoop
description: Snoops act as information peddlers, specializing in gathering secrets through investigation, subterfuge, and coercion, and selling or trading those secrets for personal gain.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Evasion; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge; 8th-level Rogue Talent

name: Spy
description: The best spies are like chameleons, but not only can they change their appearances to fit the situation, they can also change their personalities, allegiances, and even loves if that’s what it takes to achieve their clandestine goals.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Trap Sense

name: Survivalist
description: The survivalist focuses her talents on surviving harsh and unforgiving conditions that would kill a lesser rogue.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Trap Sense

name: Swamp Poisoner
description: (Grippli) Swamp poisoners use their own toxins to defend their homes and level the playing field
replaces_summary: Racial Traits; Trapfinding; 2nd-level Rogue Talent; Trap Sense

name: Swashbuckler
description: A paragon of mobile swordplay, the swashbuckler is a rogue who focuses almost exclusively on honing her skill at arms and perfecting daring acrobatic moves and elaborate flourishes that border on performance.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Trap Sense

name: Swordmaster
description: (Tengu Only) A swordmaster meditates to strengthen her spiritual connection to her blade. She strives to perfect her skills by mastering six deadly trances.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Trap Sense

name: Sylvan Trickster
description: Sylvan tricksters are rogues who model themselves after the mischievous fey of legend.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Trapfinding; Rogue Talents; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Thug
description: Some criminals steal with finesse, their victims only discovering the crime when the rogue is long gone and the coin already spent. A thug, on the other hand, cares nothing for finesse.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Trap Sense

name: Tidal Trickster
description: Tidal tricksters roam the seas, using their skillful mastery of the currents to move across aquatic battlefields and throw their enemies off-balance.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Trap Sense; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge; 4th, 8th-level Rogue Talent

name: Toxic Talon
description: A toxic talon spends resources and time combining easy-to-obtain materials into dangerous toxins to aid in the defense of Andoran.
replaces_summary: Weapon Proficiencies; Trapfinding; Trap Sense; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Trapsmith
description: Some rogues are not content with just disabling traps—they love to build them, finding a captivating beauty in the turning of gears and the slither of ropes over pulleys.
replaces_summary: Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Vexing Dodger
description: Vexing dodgers take advantage of their smaller size to outmaneuver larger foes such as giants.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; 2nd-level Rogue Talent; Trap Sense; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Waylayer
description: A clever rogue knows the easiest battle to win is the one that’s over before your foe knows it has begun.
replaces_summary: Trapfinding; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge; 12th and 20th-level Rogue Talents

name: Brawling Blademaster
description: Brawling blademaster samurai use weapon strikes and unarmed attacks in equal measure
replaces_summary: Weapon and Armor Proficiency; Mount; Weapon Expertise; Mounted Archer; Banner; Greater Banner

name: Ironbound Sword
description: The ironbound sword’s fighting style, focused on incapacitating opponents rather than killing them, can subdue a foe without causing permanent harm, leaving the soul of the samurai unstained by deaths of her enemies.
replaces_summary: Weapon Expertise; Banner; Greater Banner

name: Sovereign Blade
description: In Tian Xia, many samurai are tutored in the ways of honor using lessons that originate from sovereign dragons and their dedication to safeguarding harmony.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Mount; Mounted Archer; 6th, 12th, 18th-level bonus feats

name: Sword Saint
description: Sword saints hail from lands where samurai are prevalent, and are often ronin who wander the world seeking new challenges to perfect their intricate style of swordplay called iaijutsu.
replaces_summary: Samurai's Mount; Mounted Charge; Banner; Greater Banner

name: Ward Speaker
description: The ward speaker draws power from simple rituals to honor the spirits found throughout the world so that he might better protect those entrusted to his care.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Resolve; Greater Resolve; True Resolve; Honorable Stand

name: Warrior Poet
description: Warrior poets often study calligraphy, flower arrangement, poetry, and other courtly arts, but when called to battle, they treat combat as its own art form, fighting with beauty and grace.
replaces_summary: Weapon and Armor Proficiency; Mount; Weapon Expertise; Banner; Greater Banner; Challenge; Mounted Archer; Demanding Challange; Bonus Combat Feats

name: Yojimbo
description: Yojimbo are highly trained bodyguards favored by the nobles and warlords of distant Minkai.
replaces_summary: Mount; Weapon Expertise; Mounted Archer

name: Benefactor
description: A benefactor seeks to improve the lives of others by primarily offering powerful supportive abilities and magic to her allies and others she deems worthy.
replaces_summary: Spellcasting; Hexes;

name: Crystal Tender
description: A crystal tender focuses on the spirits of metal and stone, using crystals as a means to focus the power of the spirits she venerates.
replaces_summary: Spirit Animal; Wandering Spirit; 8th-level Shaman Hex; Manifestation

name: Deep Shaman
description: Deep shamans are tied to spirits from the depths.
replaces_summary: Spirit; Spirit Animal; Class Skills; Spirit Abilities

name: Draconic Shaman
description: Some shamans draw their powers from the might of dragons. These shamans each gain a powerful drake as an ally, and view caring for that drake as a sacred duty
replaces_summary: Spirit; Spirit Animal; 4th, 10th-level Hexes; Spirit Magic

name: Grasping Vine
description: Grasping vine shamans are typically adherents of one of several scattered sects dedicated to the Green Mother. They revere plant life in all its forms, with a special fondness for poisonous or carnivorous plants.
replaces_summary: Spirit Animal; Spirit Magic; 2nd, 8th-level Hex

name: Name-Keeper
description: Name-keepers have delved into the storied histories etched into the Wall of Names and communed with the departed spirits of those whose names are memorialized on the monument.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Spirit Animal; Spirit Magic; Wandering Spirit; Wandering Hex

name: Overseer
description: While all shamans use their connection to the spirits of the world to draw upon otherworldly magic powers, the shamans of the Lands of the Linnorm Kings have a unique tradition in which they use the power of patron spirits to directly control their enemies.
replaces_summary: Spirit Magic; Deliver Touch Spells

name: Primal Warden
description:
A
shaman who communes with spirits that linger in areas where primal magic reigns, learning to stabilize
 these erratic energies and draw upon them to her benefit.
replaces_summary: Spirit Animal; Spirit Magic; 4th, 8th, 12th-levle Hex

name: Serendipity Shaman
description: The serendipitous, shamanistic rites associated with the traditional catfolk worship of the so-called “spirits of creation”  focus upon attracting good fortune and banishing ill fortune in the name of these spirits.
replaces_summary: Spirit Magic spells

name: True Silvered Throne
description: Members in good standing who have risen to a prestigious rank within the Esoteric Order of the Palatine Eye, true silvered thrones have managed to discover rituals and occult secrets within the lore of their order.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Spirit Animal; Wandering Spirit; Wandering Hex; 8th-level Hex

name: Adaptive Shifter
description: Rather than emulate other animals entirely, some shifters learn to reshape their forms on the fly in response to a range of stimuli
replaces_summary: Shifter Claws; Shifter Aspect; Chimeric Aspect; Greater Chimeric Aspect; Final Aspect; Wild Empathy; Track; Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Wild Shape

name: Dragonblood Shifter
description: Dragonblood shifters draw upon the inherent strength and majesty of the true dragons, gaining a measure of their powers from their association.
replaces_summary: Shifter Aspect; Wilder Shape; Chimeric Aspect; Greater Chimeric Aspect; Final Aspect

name: Elementalist Shifter
description: Rather than drawing power from bestial aspects, elementalist shifters channel power from the Inner Sphere and can take on powerful elemental forms at the cost of the greater diversity available to traditional shifters.
replaces_summary: Languages; Shifter Aspect; Wild Empathy; Shifter Claws; Wild Shape; Chimeric Aspect; Greater Chimeric Aspect

name: Feyform Shifter
description: Feyform shifters are intimately linked to the First World, and can draw from it to gain otherworldly powers.
replaces_summary: Shifter Aspect; Wild Shape; Chimeric Aspect; Greater Chimeric Aspect; Final Aspect

name: Fiendflesh Shifter
description: By forging dark pacts with extraplanar forces, fiendflesh shifters invoke sinister powers to assume the strengths of daemons, demons, or devils.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Shifter Claws; Wild Shape; Shifter Aspect; Defensive Instinct; Chimeric Aspect; Greater Chimeric Instinct

name: Holy Beast
description: Holy beast shifters pledge to hunt down their deity’s enemies to earn that deity’s blessing.
replaces_summary: Deity; Shifter Claws; Wild Empathy; Track; Woodland Stride; Trackless Step

name: Leafshifter
description: (Ghoran only) Rather than assume aspects taken from the traits of natural animals, a leafshifter transforms into plant-animal hybrids that resemble living topiaries.
replaces_summary: Shifter Claws; Shifter Aspect

name: Oozemorph
description: While most shifters are trained in druidic traditions that allow them to tap into animalistic powers, others look instead to the simplest forms of life for inspiration. Known as oozemorphs, these shifters focus on the ooze—a form of life as simple in construction as it is dangerous in combat.
replaces_summary: Weapon and Armor Proficiencies; Chimeric Form; Greater Chimeric Form; Wild Shape; Shifter Aspect; Shifter Claws; Wild Empathy; Defensive Instinct; Woodland Stride

name: Rageshaper
description: The rageshaper is a destructive force of nature brought to bear—a wild and uncontrollable engine of annihilation fueled by wrath.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Wild Shape; Shifter Aspect; Shifter Claws; Defensive Instinct; Chimeric Aspect; Greater Chimeric Aspect; Woodland Stride; Trackless Step

name: Style Shifter
description: Style shifters have been able to perfect their combat techniques to the point where they can augment a combat style by partially transforming into the creature that inspired it.
replaces_summary: Armor Proficiencies; Shifter Aspect; Chimeric Aspect; Greater Chimeric Aspect; Final Aspect; Wild Shape

name: Swarm Shifter
description: Swarm shifters channel the essence of the basest and most overlooked animals, vermin.
replaces_summary: Wild Shape; Shifter Aspect; Chimeric Aspect; Greater Chimeric Aspect; Final Aspect

name: Verdant Shifter
description: Verdant shifters have an affinity for plants rather than animals and gain a plantlike form that grows in power as they do.
replaces_summary: Wild Empathy; Shifter Aspect; Defensive Instinct; Wild Shape; Chimeric Form; Greater Chimeric Form

name: Weretouched
description: Weretouched shifters are scions of lycanthropic forces, whether hereditary or supernaturally imposed. They can assume both animal and hybrid forms, as a lycanthrope does.
replaces_summary: Shifter Aspect; Wild Empathy; Wild Shape

name: Wild Effigy
description: A wild effigy still takes on the shapes of natural animals, but rather than transforming her body into flesh and muscle, she transforms into constructed effigies of her chosen aspects.
replaces_summary: Wild Empathy; Track; Woodland Stride; Trackless Step; Defensive Instinct

name: Augur
description: Tales of Merivesta Olinchi are surprisingly common among the Bekyar of the Mwangi Expanse, who claim she spent some time studying their traditions and history.
replaces_summary: Well-Versed; Versatile Performance; Spell Kenning

name: Bacchanal
description: Bacchanals are skalds who use ecstatic dancing and ribald songs to influence the primal instincts of listeners.
replaces_summary: Scribe Scroll; Versatile Performance; Song of Marching; Dirge of Doom

name: Battle Scion
description: The battle scion possesses a unique mixture of courtly grace alongside martial and magical prowess.
replaces_summary: Bardic Knowledge; Dirge of Doom; Song of the Fallen; Master Skald

name: Bekyar Demon Dancer
description: While the twisted power of the demon-worshiping Bekyar’s skalds is great, it comes at a terrible price.
replaces_summary: Skills; Versatile Performance; 3rd, 6th, and 9th-level Rage Powers

name: Belkzen War Drummer
description: These fierce drummers are equally adept at tapping out a driving rhythm and rapping enemies upside the head with the same massive clubs they use to beat the crude hidecovered drums they carry into battle.
replaces_summary: Weapon Proficiencies; Bardic Knowledge; Scribe Scroll; Lore Master; 7th-level Versatile Performance

name: Boaster
description: Boasters use incredible tales and claims to challenge themselves and their allies to accomplish remarkable feats.
replaces_summary: Scribe Scroll; Uncanny Dodge; Song of Marching; Lore Master; Song of the Fallen; Rage Powers; 6th-level Rage Power

name: Bold Schemer
description: The bold schemer combines wrath, wits, and daring to win battles and wars in unconventional ways, often through infiltration and deception.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Bardic Knowledge; Uncanny Dodge; Improved Uncanny Dodge; Damage Reduction; Dirge of Doom

name: Court Poet
description: Court poets elevate the skald’s love of history and poetry to an aristocratic ideal, captivating courts with complicated poetic traditions and inspiring others with their craft.
replaces_summary: Inspired Rage; Song of Strength; Well-Versed

name: Dragon Skald
description: The Ulfen prize skalds on sea raids and linnorm hunts. Dragon skald performances involve song, whistling, or blowing mighty horns, and viking sailors often talk about having a skald along to whistle up a wind or sing away the mists on the morning of a momentous raid.
replaces_summary: Scribe Scroll; Bardic Knowledge; Well-Versed; Song of Marching

name: Elegist
description: Some skalds internalize the saddest and must mournful  of stories, so much that they are able to manifest the emotions caused by these tales in physical form.
replaces_summary: Raging Song; Rage Powers; Well-Versed; Damage Reduction

name: Hunt Caller
description: Hunt callers are skalds whose songs draw their allies into the animal world.
replaces_summary: Well-Versed; Song of Strength; Song of the Fallen; 6th, 18th-leve Rage Power; Spell Kenning

name: Instigator
description: he instigator influences the will of the people, turning them toward his own purposes.
replaces_summary: Spell Kenning; Dirge of Doom; Lore Master

name: Red Tongue
description: The tengu tendency toward dramatic flourishes and rhetoric creates an environment in which political clubs led by hot-blooded firebrands—referred to as red tongues in polite company—dominate the political scene by swaying emotions in the moment and wielding magic in the shadows.
replaces_summary: Bardic Knowledge; 7th, 12th, 17th-level Versatile Performance

name: Serpent Herald
description: Warrior bands devoted to Ragadahn are often led by a serpent herald, whose deep roars shake the earth itself.
replaces_summary: 3rd-level Rage Power; Spell Kenning

name: Sunsinger
description: Qadiran sunsingers are particularly religious skalds of Sarenrae who call down their goddess’s glory to fill soldiers with fire.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Song of Marching; Spell Kenning;

name: Totem Channeler
description: Some Shoanti skalds discover deeper truths in their tribes’ totems. These skalds, known as totem channelers, learn how to share the totems’ gifts with their allies in the heat of battle.
replaces_summary: Bardic Knowledge; Rage Powers; Spell Kenning; Damage Reduction

name: Twilight Speaker
description: (Elf Only) Twilight speakers are elven skalds of Findeladlara who consider it a sacred duty to seek out and befriend non-elven communities
replaces_summary: Alignment; Deity; Bardic Knowledge; Inspired Rage; Song of Strength; Dirge of Doom; Versatile Performance; Well-Versed; Lore Master; Master Skald

name: Undying Word
description: Some who have survived the Mana Wastes cannot accept defeat, and those who have learned to speak the undying word can lend others the strength to endure
replaces_summary: Scribe Scroll; Lore Master; Inspired Rage; Song of Strength; Dirge of Doom; Spell Kenning

name: Urban Skald
description: The urban skald finds that challenging and mocking foes is sometimes more effective than inspiring uncontrolled rage in a city.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Inspired Rage; Song of Marching; Damage Reduction; Dirge of Doom

name: War Painter
description: The gripplis of the Valashmai Jungle exhibit savage strength for their size—attributed in part to the frightful magical pigments applied to their skin by the tribes’ mystical lore keepers.
replaces_summary: Raging Song; Spell Kenning; Lore Master

name: Warlord
description: The warlord wields his force of personality like a weapon, intimidating his allies and ensuring that his followers heed his commands.
replaces_summary: Scribe Scroll; Well-Versed; Spell Kenning; Lore Master; Improved Uncanny Dodge

name: Wyrm Singer
description: Wyrm singers spin fragments of the story of the ongoing struggle between noble Apsu and wicked Dahak.
replaces_summary: Inspired Rage; Song of the Fallen; 12th-level rage power

name: Ankou's Shadow
description: Ankou’s shadow practice the deadliest skills of the First World’s most feared enforcers: terrifying, winged fey assassins dispatched by the greatest lords of the First World to eliminate their rivals
replaces_summary: Studied Target; Stalker; Quarry; Improved Quarry

name: Avalancher
description: Avalanchers attack from overhead cliffs and mesas like a landslide of arrows and blades.
replaces_summary: Studied Target; Track; Swift Tracker; Stalker; Quarry; Improved Quarry; Master Slayer

name: Bloody Jake
description: Bloody jakes are cruel backwoods folk who prey upon their country cousins while terrorizing civilized people who venture into their rural range.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Weapon and Armor Proficiency; Studied Target; 4th, 6th, 8th, 12th, 16th-level Slayer Talents; Swift Tracker; Slayer's Advance

name: Butterfly Blade
description: Butterfly blades work in the shadows, eliminating threats with their namesake weapons and intimidating troublesome nobles.
replaces_summary: Studied Target; Track; Sneak Attack; Stalker; Swift Tracker; Quarry; Improved Quarry

name: Covenbane
description: Covenbane slayers are scarred by arcane magic at a young age, and that resonance gives them supernatural insight into the psychic bonds between others.
replaces_summary: Track; 2nd-level Slayer Talent; Stalker; Swift Tracker

name: Dune Rider
description: Mounted harriers, saboteurs, and skirmishers, dune riders can disrupt even the most organized opposition.
replaces_summary: Track; Swift Tracker; Studied Target; Stalker, 4th, 8th-level Slayer Talent; Master Slayer

name: Family Hunter
description: Most slayers focus their efforts on single targets, but family hunters try to root out every branch of a tainted tree of life.
replaces_summary: 2nd, 6th-level Slayer Talent; Sneak Attack; Studied Target

name: Guerrilla
description: Slayers characterized by cunning and patience, guerrillas specialize in ambush and harassment of enemies.
replaces_summary: Track; Swift Tracker; 6th-level Slayer Talent; Quarry; Improved Quarry

name: Pureblade
description: In Numeria, Pureblades are skilled at slaying naturally arising aberrations and any creatures that have been mutated by exposure to the bizarre alien technology that pervades the land. Pureblades from Alkenstar and Nex lead forays into the magic-starved Mana Wastes to eliminate aberrations before the creatures can threaten their lands.
replaces_summary: Track; 2nd and 8th-level Slayer Talents; Stalker; Master Slayer

name: Sczarni Executioner
description: These professional killers are trained and used almost exclusively by the various Sczarni crime families.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Studied Target; 4th-level Slayer Talent; Swift Tracker; Quarry; Improved Quarry

name: Spawn Slayer
description: Generations of Ninshaburian warriors fought the Spawn of Rovagug, holding the line against impossible odds. Some of their ancient techniques persist to this day.
replaces_summary: Studied Target; Stalker; Master Slayer

name: Spire Diver
description: Peerless divers, assassins, and scouts, spire divers operate in and out of water using the same weapons and equipment.
replaces_summary: Studied Target; Stalker; Track; Swift Tracker; Slayer's Advance; Quarry; Improved Quarry

name: Spiritslayer
description: Slayers that focus in culling ghosts, shadow demons, and other incorporeal creatures.
replaces_summary: 2nd level Slayer Talent; Stalker

name: Toxic Sniper
description: Followers of the daemonic harbinger Cixyron hide throughout the Mana Wastes, scavenging for guns. With significant firepower in hand, the Furious Thunder’s snipers bring poisonous death to the wastes.
replaces_summary: Weapon and Armor Proficiencies; Track; Studied Target; Slayer Talents

name: Turncoat
description: The turncoat’s natural talents for deception and treachery are fitting when negotiating with the untrustworthy individuals and his ability to adapt allows him to preempt opponents’ schemes.
replaces_summary: Track; Stalker; Quarry; Improved Quarry

name: Velvet Blade
description: Velvet blades specialize in infiltrating the upper echelons of society, mingling with nobles and courtiers, and killing them.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Armor Proficiency; Studied Target; Track; Swift Tracker

name: Witch Killer
description: Born into cultures where magic users are hated and feared, witch killers devote their lives to purge the arcane taint from the world.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Studied Target; 4th, 10th-level Slayer Talent

name: Woodland Sniper
description: Woodland snipers are guardians of forest and grove, keeping vigil on their borders and hunting bounties and trespassers from the shelter of sturdy branches.
replaces_summary: Track; Sneak Attack; Stalker; Swift Tracker; Slayer's Advance

name: Crossblooded
description: A crossblooded bloodline combines the powers of two distinct heritages.
replaces_summary: Spells Known

name: Dragon Drinker
description: (Requires Draconic Bloodline) The blood of dragons not only flows through a dragon drinker’s veins, but also empowers her spells and magical abilities when consumed.
replaces_summary: Bloodline Arcana; Claws Bloodline Power; 7th, 13th, and 19th-level Bloodline Feats

name: Nine-Tailed Heir
description: (Kitsune only) Tian stories often tell of kitsune with multiple tails who have a magical quirk in their blood or have been blessed by their race’s deific matron, Daikitsu
replaces_summary: Bloodline Spell

name: Razmiran Priest
description: The Razmiran priest is a magical charlatan, missionary servant of the Living God.
replaces_summary: Eschew Materials; 3rd-level Bloodline Spell; 5th-level Bloodline Spell; 9th-level Bloodline Power

name: Seeker
description: The seeker is obsessed with learning about their heritage and history, researching ancient texts and obscure ruins for clues.
replaces_summary: Eschew Materials, 3rd-level Bloodline Power, 15th-level Bloodline Power

name: Sorcerer of Sleep
description: Some sorcerers have trouble accessing their innate gifts and must find another way to unlock them. For a sorcerer of sleep, the mind-altering drug pesh is the key.
replaces_summary: Bloodline Arcana; Eschew Materials; 1st-level Bloodline Power

name: Stone Warder
description: Stone warders have an innate ability to create runes that allow them to ward areas with magic traps built from their spells. These sorcerers are most common among dwarves, but have been known to appear among other races who have strong ties to earth, rock, stone, mountains, and the Darklands.
replaces_summary: Eschew Materials; 3rd-level Bloodline Spell; 7th-level Bloodline Feat

name: Tattooed Sorcerer
description: The tattooed sorcerer has embraced the ancient traditions of Varisian spellcasting, and uses colorful and intricate tattoos to enhance her magical powers.
replaces_summary: 1st-level Bloodline Power; Eschew Materials; 7th-level Bloodline Feat; 9th-level Bloodline Power

name: Umbral Scion
description: Umbral scions are sorcerers who are able to expertly control their especially potent shadow heritage. The majority of umbral sorcerers hail from the Darklands.
replaces_summary: Bloodline (Shadow Only); Spellcasting; Shadowstrike Bloodline Power; 7th and 13th-level Bloodline Feats

name: Wildblooded
description: A wildblooded sorcerer has a mutated version of a more common bloodline, with one arcana and at least one bloodline power that are different from those of an unmutated bloodline.
replaces_summary: Bloodline

name: Wishcrafter
description: (Ifrit Only) Wishcrafters are born with a talent for manipulating reality inherited from their efreeti ancestors.
replaces_summary: Bloodline Arcana; Bloodline Bonus Spells; 7th, 13th, 19th-level Bloodline Bonus Feats

name: Drowned Channeler
description: When a drowned spirit doomed to a watery grave descends toward the evil of undeath and seeks refuge in a mortal shell, the result is an unusual spiritualist with powers that resonate with the drowned spirit’s demise.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Phantom; Shared Consciousness; Etheric Tether; Bonded Manifestation; Bonded Senses; Detect Undead; Calm Spirit; See Invisibility; Call Spirit

name: Ectoplasmatist
description: Instead of calling upon a phantom from the Ethereal Plane, an ectoplasmatist infuses herself with the mysterious substance called ectoplasm.
replaces_summary: Etheric Tether; Phantom; Shared Consciousness; Fused Consciousness; Spiritual Bond; Empowered Consciousness; Bonded Manifestation; Phantom Recall; Dual Bond; Spiritual Interference; Greater Spiritual Interference

name: Exciter
description: The phantom that accompanies the exciter fills him with unbridled exultation, as he lets feeling and passion rule and sharpen his mind and body into a glorious fusion.
replaces_summary: Phantom; Emotional Focus; Phantom Recall; Etheric Tether; Bonded Senses; Spiritual Bond; Spiritual Interference; Fused Consciousness; Spiritual Interference;

name: Fated Guide
description: Not all phantoms are restless dead escaping the afterlife. On rare occasions, Pharasma returns a judged soul to the Material Plane—linking the spirit to a devout believer—to complete some final task, make amends for a crime, or grant a sharply divided soul a second chance.
replaces_summary: Antagonistic; Bonded Manifestation; See Invisibility; Call Spirit

name: Fractured Mind
description: Most spiritualists harbor the spirits of the deceased in their psyches, but a small number of them—known as fractured minds—draw their powers instead from a fraction of their own souls that resonates with extremely powerful emotions.
replaces_summary: Spellcasting; Detect Undead; Calm Spirit; See Invisibility; Call Spirit

name: Geist Channeler
description: Some phantoms retain less of their personalities and memories than others. Though most phantoms maintain some semblance of their former selves, others of their kind, known as geists, came close to being lost forever before they became phantoms.
replaces_summary: Phantom; Emotional Focus; Bonded Manifestation

name: Grim Apostle
description: Some devotees of the Four Horsemen can form spiritual manifestations of the daemonic demigods.
replaces_summary: Phantom; Emotional Focus; Shared Consciousness; Fused Consciousness

name: Hag-Haunted
description: A hag who dies with a curse on her breath is often anchored to the Ethereal Plane by the power of her hatred— similar to vile and angry mortal souls—and some even claw their way back to the living world through the souls of those they despised or ruined... or those unfortunate souls they birthed. Hag-haunted spiritualists are tethered to these spiteful spirits, anchoring them once again in the world of the living.
replaces_summary: Phantom; Shared Consciousness; Fused Consciousness; Spells; Spiritual Interference; Greater Spiritual Interference

name: Haunted
description: The haunted are a dangerous breed of spellcasters bound to temperamental and unpredictable phantoms that leech power from their spiritualists.
replaces_summary: Bonded Manifestation; Dual Bond

name: Involutionist
description: Rather than bond with an existing spirit as most Rivethun eventually do, the involutionist creates a spirit from a piece of her own soul.
replaces_summary: Spellcasting; Phantom; Emotional Focus; Bonded Manifestation; Detect Undead, 11th, 15th, 17th-level uses of Calm Spirit

name: Necrologist
description: The vile spiritualists known as necrologists reach toward the Negative Energy Plane and for whatever evil purpose, they bring back malevolent spirits whose passions have turned to vengeance and hate for all life.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Share Consciousness; Share Spells; 5th, 13th-level slam damage increase; Devotion; Bonded Manifestation; Spiritual Interference; Greater Spiritual Interference

name: Onmyoji
description: Though most spiritualists are chosen by their phantoms, others deliberately call phantoms to them through years of careful preparation and study in obscure divine traditions.
replaces_summary: Spellcasting; Shared Consciousness; Fused Consciousness; Spiritual Interference; Greater Spiritual Interference

name: Phantom Blade
description: A small selection of spiritualists are connected to spirits of combat and warfare, and manifest a weapon instead of a phantom.
replaces_summary: Weapon and Armor Proficiencies; Phantom; Phantom Recall; Spiritual Bond; Dual Bond; Etheric Tether; Shared Consciousness; Fused Consciousness; Empowered Consciousness; Bonded Senses; Bonded Manifestation; Spiritual Interference; Greater Spiritual Interference

name: Plague Eater
description: A plague eater bears the weight of many souls within her consciousness, spirits that remember the devastation of disease and seek to protect their host from infections, cleanse others of plague, and use disease itself as a weapon.
replaces_summary: Phantom; Shared Consciousness; Spiritual Interference; Detect Undead; Calm Spirit; See Invisibility; Fused Consciousness; Greater Spiritual Interference; Call Spirit

name: Priest of the Fallen
description: A priest of the fallen is a spiritualist who channels her land’s legends and provides a vessel for mighty heroes to perform heroic acts once more.
replaces_summary: Phantom; Shared Consciousness; Bonded Manifestation; Phantom Recall; Fused Consciouness; Dual Bond; Empowered Consciousness

name: Quintessentialist
description: The quintessentialist learns to project her best self—her exemplar—as an independent being, but in doing so leaves only the weakest and basest aspects behind in her body.
replaces_summary: Spellcasting; Phantom

name: Scourge
description: Scourges are students of pain and have a rare connection to tormented and wracked spirits.
replaces_summary: Spiritual Interference; Devotion; Calm Spirit; Greater Spiritual Interference

name: Seeker of Enlightenment
description: Sometimes a spiritualist is so haunted by a past life’s failure to reach enlightenment that the past life is reborn not as a new creature but as a phantom in a spiritualist’s consciousness.
replaces_summary: Shared Consciousness; Detect Undead; Calm Spirit; See Invisibility; Fused Consciousness; Call Spirit

name: Shadow Caller
description: While most spiritualists form a bond with the remnants of a creature’s soul, some explore ways to bind ephemeral shadows to their own souls in exchange for power.
replaces_summary: Phantom; Emotional Focus; Shared Consciousness; Etheric Tether; Phantom Recall; See Invisibility; Fused Consciousness; Empowered Consciousness

name: Soul Warden
description: Soul wardens have helped their phantoms pass on and have become wardens of the soulstream, serving Pharasma and working with psychopomps to protect vulnerable souls from those who seek to capture, corrupt, or devour them.
replaces_summary: Phantom; Etheric Tether; Shared Consciousness; Spiritual Interference; Fused Consciousness; Greater Spiritual Interference; Bonded Senses; Phantom Recall; Bonded Manifestation; Dual Bond; Empowered Consciousness

name: Totem Spiritualist
description: These specialized spiritualists call upon phantoms that are manifestations of animals and personifications of nature.
replaces_summary: Phantom; Shared Consiousness; See Invisibility; Dual Bond

name: Usher of Lost Souls
description: Ushers of lost souls are spiritualists who focus on bringing the souls of the dead to their final judgments at the end of the River of Souls.
replaces_summary: Bonded Senses; Spiritual Interference; Greater Spiritual Interference; See Invisibility; Spiritual Bond

name: Ward Spiritualist
description: A ward spiritualist ventures into the wilderness and petitions a kami to help her in her travels, either taking an existing ward with her or becoming the kami’s new ward.
replaces_summary: Phantom; Spellcasting; Bonded Senses; Phantom Recall; Greater Spiritual Interference

name: Zeitgeist Binder
description: Zeitgeist binders channel the emotional connections of a group of people into a unique phantom that personifies a community.
replaces_summary: Phantom;  Detect Undead; Calm Spirit; See Invisibility; Call Spirit; Bonded Senses; Fused Consciousness

name: Blood God Disciple
description: (Half-Orc) A half-orc summoner who devotes himself to one of the bloody orc gods may believe his eidolon is an avatar of that god rather than a mere supernatural creature.
replaces_summary: Summon Monster

name: Blood Summoner
description: A blood summoner conjures an eidolon born from violence and blood.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Shield Ally; Transposition; Greater Shield Ally; Merge Forms

name: Broodmaster
description: Most summoners bond with one eidolon for their entire lives, but the broodmaster forges a link with multiple smaller eidolons that make up his brood.
replaces_summary: Eidolon; Life Link; Life Bond; Merge Forms

name: Counter-Summoner
description: While most summoners excel at conjuring monsters, there are those who focus instead on disrupting the conjurations of opponents.
replaces_summary: Summon Monster; Bond Senses; Aspect; Greater Aspect

name: Evolutionist
description: An evolutionist possesses greater power over his eidolon’s form, and is able to evolve his eidolon and its abilities to meet any challenge or face any threat as it comes up, seemingly on a whim.
replaces_summary: Maker’s Call; Transposition; Greater Shield Ally

name: First Worlder
description: A first worlder summoner has a more primal connection to nature, their power being tied to the First World.
replaces_summary: Summon Monster I; Eidolon

name: God Caller
description: God callers follow a fractured tradition that originated in the now-lost nation of Sarkoris. They speak to an assortment of ancient divine powers to lead their communities and sometimes stir up reformations in faiths with ideals similar to their personal convictions.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Life Link; Transposition; Aspect; Greater Aspect; Twin Eidolon

name: Leshy Caller
description: A leshy caller summons creatures from the First World and forges a bond with a leshy eidolon.
replaces_summary: Eidolon; Summon Monster

name: Master Summoner
description: The master summoner sacrifices the power of his eidolon in favor of summoning a plethora of otherworldly creatures to aid him.
replaces_summary: Summon Monster I; Shield Ally; Bond Senses

name: Morphic Savant
description: A morphic savant is an agent of change who draws power from the Abyss, Elysium, or the Maelstrom to fulfill whatever goals strike his fancy.
replaces_summary: Eidolon; Summon Monster; Spells

name: Pyroclast
description: Pyroclasts harness the primal elemental energy of magma. Their eidolons often resemble elemental beings.
replaces_summary: Spellcasting; Eidolon; Maker’s Call; Transposition

name: Shadow Caller
description: (Fetchling Only) While most summoners can call any manner of creature from across the planes to serve them in combat, supplementing the skills of their eidolon with a diverse range of creatures, others eschew this broad utility and instead concentrate upon calling forth entities from the Shadow Plane.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Summon Monster; Eidolon

name: Shaitan Binder
description: (Oread Only) Shaitan binders call upon a reflection of their genie ancestors to serve as their eidolons.
replaces_summary: Share Spells; Shield Ally; Greater Shield Ally; Aspect; Greater Aspect; Twin Eidolon

name: Storm Caller
description: Many summoners beckon monsters from the Elemental Planes, but few are as specialized as storm callers, who channel the elemental energies of the Plane of Air.
replaces_summary: Eidolon; Summon Monster; Shield Ally; Greater Shield Ally; Maker's Call; Transposition

name: Story Summoner
description: Story summoners rely on the harrow to weave the paths they walk, turning their tales into reality. They channel magic through their harrow decks to influence the strange extraplanar beings they summon, bringing familiar and fateful characters to life.
replaces_summary: Eidolon; Bond Senses; Transposition

name: Synthesist
description: Rather than summon an eidolon to serve by his side, the synthesist fuses his eidolon’s essence to his own.
replaces_summary: Eidolon; Life Link; Shield Ally; Maker’s Call; Transposition; Greater Shield Ally; Merge Forms

name: Twinned Summoner
description: Reflecting the connected nature of Imbrex, a twinned summoner and his eidolon appear identical save for any obvious differences, such as evolutions or equipment.
replaces_summary: Shield Ally; Greater Shield Ally; Maker's Call; Transposition; Merge Forms

name: Unwavering Conduit
description: Unwavering conduits work to keep everything ordered and regimented, drawing on power from the Eternal City of Axis, Heaven, or Hell to enforce whichever laws the summoner finds paramount.
replaces_summary: Eidolon; Summon Monster; Spells

name: Wild Caller (ARG)
description: (Half-Elf Only) The wild caller calls eidolons that take more natural and savage forms and summons from nature rather than the Great Beyond.
replaces_summary: Summon Monster; Eidolon

name: Wild Caller (HotW)
description: A wild caller summons creatures from the First World and forges a bond with an eidolon with a plant body.
replaces_summary: Eidolon; Summon Monster; Aspect; Greater Aspect

name: Construct Caller
description: Construct callers use their mechanical and planar understandings to work planar energies as a tinkerer works parts
replaces_summary: Subtype; Summon Monster

name: Devil Binder
description: A devil binder borrows extraplanar quintessence from her eidolon, gaining strange abilities for herself and evolving her eidolon’s magical powers.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Summon Monster I; Bond Senses; Shield Ally; Maker's Call; Transposition; Greater Shield Ally; Merge Forms; Twin Eidolon; Devotion

name: Devil Impostor
