description: Some summoners can’t abide the cruelty of devils, or simply can’t outwit them, but must appear to do so to get by in Cheliax, the Outer Planes, or similar areas of infernal influence. Rather than risk binding true devils, they reshape their eidolons into deceptive forms.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Bond Senses; Eidolon; Summon Monster; Shield Ally; Greater Shield Ally; Transposition

name: Fey Caller
description: Fey callers are summoners whose voices reach out into those strange faerie realms and call forth the fey who dwell there.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Summon Monster; Eidolon Subtype

name: God Caller
description: God callers follow a fractured tradition that originated in the now-lost nation of Sarkoris. They speak to an assortment of ancient divine powers to lead their communities and sometimes stir up reformations in faiths with ideals similar to their personal convictions.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Life Link; Transposition; Aspect; Greater Aspect; Twin Eidolon

name: Leshy Caller
description: A leshy caller summons creatures from the First World and forges a bond with a leshy eidolon.
replaces_summary: Eidolon; Summon Monster

name: Soulbound Summoner
description: Soulbound summoners, as a rule, never set out to bind their soul to a dangerous and enigmatic power. Rather, they have found themselves unintentional masters of eidolons through incredible circumstances.
replaces_summary: Eidolon; Life Link; Summon Monster

name: Storm Caller
description: Many summoners beckon monsters from the Elemental Planes, but few are as specialized as storm callers, who channel the elemental energies of the Plane of Air.
replaces_summary: Eidolon; Summon Monster; Shield Ally; Greater Shield Ally; Maker's Call; Transposition

name: Twinned Summoner
description: Reflecting the connected nature of Imbrex, a twinned summoner and his eidolon appear identical save for any obvious differences, such as evolutions or equipment.
replaces_summary: Shield Ally; Greater Shield Ally; Maker's Call; Transposition; Merge Forms

name: Arrow Champion
description: Arrow champions combine a swashbuckler’s agile style of combat with mastery over the bow
replaces_summary: Panache; Opportune Parry and Riposte; Precise Strike; Swashbuckler's Initiative; Swashbuckler Weapon Training; Swashbuckler Weapon Mastery

name: Azatariel
description: Azatariels are the champions of Elysium, dedicated to spreading its unpredictable whimsy and capricious goodness across the planes.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Menacing Swordplay; Precise Strike; Targeted Strike; Bleeding Wound; Charmed Life; Nimble; Bonus Feats

name: Courser
description: Coursers move with grace and ease, racing across rooftops, dashing up walls, and clearing vast gaps between buildings.
replaces_summary: Opportune Parry and Riposte; Nimble; Menacing Swordplay; Superior Feint; Bleeding Wound

name: Dashing Thief
description: The dashing thief relies on swift swordplay, dazzling charm, and spirited courage to commit audacious acts of thievery.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Derring-Do; Swashbuckler's Edge; Panache; Bonus Feats; Menacing Swordplay; Dizzying Defense

name: Guiding Blade
description: Instead of parrying attacks against her, a guiding blade redirects her enemies’ ire away from her allies, interfering with enemies who attack her allies, whether that attack comes by blade or by spell.
replaces_summary: Bonus Feats; 1st, 7th, 15th-level Deeds; Charmed Life; Nimble; Kip-up

name: Noble Fencer
description: Noble fencers use their quick wits and panache in both physical and social confrontations, and they rely on their training and discipline to emerge victorious, rather than merely trusting to luck.
replaces_summary: Dodging Panache; Superior Feint; Subtle Blade; Charmed Life

name: Okayo Corsair
description: An Okayo corsair practices a unique acrobatic martial art that draws inspiration from both the mysticism of monastic combat traditions and the flair of a swashbuckler.
replaces_summary: Weapon and Armor Proficiency; Swashbuckler Finesse; Panache; Menacing Swordplay; Superior Feint; Bleeding Wound; Dizzying Defense; Bonus Feats

name: Rondelero Swashbuckler
description: Rondelero swashbucklers are Taldan specialists in an aggressive, lightly armored fighting style that utilizes the falcata and buckler.
replaces_summary: Derring-Do; Kip-Up; Bleeding Wound; Perfect Thrust; Charmed Life

name: Rostland Bravo
description: While some duelists favor more technical approaches, others study flashier maneuvers, wielding the curved blade with artful flair. Disdainfully called “bravos” by classically trained rivals, students of this approach have claimed the label with pride.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Armor Proficiency; Menacing Swordplay; Superior Feint; Bleeding Wound; Swashbuckler's Edge

name: Shackles Corsair
description: A Shackles corsair’s debonair charm and style make her a paradoxical celebrity even among the nations whose ships she plunders.
replaces_summary: Nimble; Swashbuckler Initiative; Targeted Strike

name: Veiled Blade
description: Veiled blades specialize in subtlety, not through clandestine sneaking but simply by appearing to be someone harmless and unarmed.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Opportune Parry and Riposte; Menacing Swordplay; Swashbuckler's Grace; Swashbuckler's Edge

name: Whirling Dervish
description: In Qadira and throughout the Padishah Empire, Sarenrae’s worshipers praise the Dawnflower through dance. Her more warlike followers adapted these dances into graceful martial forms, and their swashbuckling style is feared throughout the Inner Sea region and beyond for its ability to devastate foes with a scimitar through motion and agility regardless of strength of arms.
replaces_summary: Swashbuckler Finesse; Panache; Superior Feint; Targeted Strike; Bleeding Wound; Deadly Stab

name: Wildstrider
description: Most swashbucklers call cities their home, but some prefer deserts, marshlands, mountains, or woods, where the rough terrain grants them an advantage, and have little interest in the comforts of an urban lifestyle.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Dodging Panache; Derring-Do; Swashbuckler's Edge; Kip-Up; Subtle Blade

name: Agathiel
description: Agathiels surrender a portion of their immortal souls to Nirvana in exchange for a measure of animalistic might to aid them in their righteous crusades.
replaces_summary: Dual Identity; Vigilante Specialization; 1st-level Social Talent; 6th-level Vigilante Talent

name: Anaphexia Thought-Killer
description: Anaphexia thought-killers cut out their own tongues to gain magical protection from exposure and hunt down secrets to steal before they can be spread
replaces_summary: 2nd, 6th-level Social Talent; 7th, 15th-level Vigilante Talent

name: Avenging Beast
description: Some vigilantes turn to natural traditions and supernatural forces to transform into beasts, unleashing a primal fury on their foes before returning to an ordinary guise.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Skill Ranks; Dual Identity; 4th, 7th, 10th ,14th, 16th-level Vigilante Talents; Vigilante Specialization; Startling Appearance; Frightening Appearance; Stunning Appearance

name: Bellflower Harvester
description: Bellflower harvesters are the front-line operatives of the secretive Bellflower Network
replaces_summary: Dual Identity; Vigilante Specialization; 1st-level Social Talent; 2nd-level Vigilante Talent

name: Brute
description: Some vigilantes can transform into brutish versions of themselves, becoming larger, more terrifying in appearance, and far more formidable in combat—though at a price.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Base Saving Throws; Weapon and Armor Proficiency; Vigilante Specialization; Vengeance Strike

name: Cabalist
description: Practice with blood sacrifices, necromancy, and shadow magic gives a cabalist a reputation as a sinister and dangerous vigilante.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Skill Ranks; Weapon and Armor Proficiency; 4th, 8th, 10th, 14th, 16th-level Vigilante Talent; Vigilante Specialization; Startling Appearance; Frightening Appearance; Stunning Appearance

name: Chu Ye Enforcer
description: Donning terrifying masks, these vigilantes forsake their humanity to become more like the evil oni they mimic.
replaces_summary: Dual Identity; 1st-level Social Talent; 4th, 6th, 12th, 14th, 18th-level Vigilante Talent

name: Darklantern
description: Select members of the Lantern Bearers undergo a horrific ritual allowing them to temporarily experience the dark transformation into a drow, but at the cost of their sanity.
replaces_summary: Creature Type; Dual Identity; 1st-level Social Talent; 4th-level Vigilante Talent

name: Dragonscale Loyalist
description: Dragonscale loyalists serve the throne of Brevoy. When not acting as courtiers, these agents don fearsome draconic masks, becoming anonymous protectors of the king-regent’s rule.
replaces_summary: 1st-level Social Talent; Unshakable; Startling Appearance; Frightening Appearance; Stunning Appearance

name: Experimenter
description: Some vigilantes adopt lives of secrecy to hide their strange experiments from public view.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Vigilante Specialization; 1st-level Social Talent; Unshakable; Startling Appearance; Frightening Appearance; Stunning Appearance

name: Faceless Enforcer
description: Unyielding warriors, faceless enforcers lock their identities behind implacable helms.
replaces_summary: Weapon and Armor Proficiencies; Dual Identity; 2nd, 6th-level Vigilante Talent; 5th, 11th, 17th-level Social Talent

name: Ferocious Hunter
description: Some half-orcs have such distant orc ancestry that they have the ability to move through the world without experiencing the same discrimination many of their kin, yet many of these half-orcs still take great pride in their heritage and have a powerful desire to show the world that half-orcs are deserving of respect
replaces_summary: Dual Identity; 2nd, 8th-level Vigilante Talent; Unshakable

name: Gunmaster
description: Gunmasters brandish firearms like extensions of their will, taking down their foes with deadly accuracy and skill.
replaces_summary: Weapon and Armor Proficiency; Vigilante Specialization; Unshakable

name: Half-Elf Double Scion
description: The deception of hiding their true heritage can take a toll on some half-elves, and some form alternate identities to let the suppressed side of their heritage be known.
replaces_summary: Dual Identity

name: Hangman
description: Wherever the guilty walk free, the hangman brings judgment.
replaces_summary: Weapon and Armor Proficiencies; Vigilante Specialization; 2nd, 4th-level Vigilante Talent; Unshakable; Startling Appearance; Frightening Appearance; Stunning Appearance

name: Hidden Current
description: The hidden current operates above and below the sea.
replaces_summary: 1st-level Social Talent; 2nd-level Vigilante Talent; Frightening Appearance

name: Imperial Agent
description: Imperial agents are often the descendants of nobility and generals from Lung Wa, devoted to reuniting the empire by conquering Lung Wa’s successor states
replaces_summary: 1st, 5th-level Social Talent; Unshakable

name: Magical Child
description: Some vigilantes, no matter their age, carry a spark of capricious whimsy under which flows a powerful current of magic and wonder.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Skill Ranks; Weapon and Armor Proficiency; 4th, 8th, 14th, 16th-level Vigilante Talents; Vigilante Specialization

name: Masked Maiden
description: Masked maidens find themselves leading a double life: ordinary (albeit troubled) citizen by day, faceless warrior by night. In the most tragic cases, the identities entirely disassociate, with the maiden experiencing unexplained exhaustion and injuries upon awakening.
replaces_summary: Weapon and Armor Proficiencies; Dual Identity; Seamless Guise; 3rd, 7th, 11th, 15th, 19th-level Social Talents

name: Mounted Fury
description: Some vigilantes form special bonds with the creatures they use as steeds, considering these loyal and trusted mounts just as much heroes or antiheroes as they might be.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Vigilante Specialization; 6th, 12th-level Vigilante Talent; Vengeance Strike

name: Mutated Defender
description: Occasionally a mutation takes hold of the body of a vigilante, but he learns to hide his maladies, usually through alchemical concoctions or magical concealment
replaces_summary: Vigilante Specialization; Vigilante Talents

name: Psychometrist
description: Psychometrists aren’t spellcasters; instead, they collect strange items or create their own uncanny gadgets that seem to only work for them.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; 1st-level Social Talent; 2nd, 6th, 12th, 18th-level Vigilante Talent; Unshakable

name: Serial Killer
description: Some vigilantes adopt an innocent guise to cloak their acts of brutal murder.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Vigilante Specialization; Unshakable; 4th, 6th, 12th, 14th-level Vigilante Talent; 7th, 9th, 19th-level Social Talent; Startling Appearance; Frighting Appearance; Stunning Appearance

name: Splintersoul
description: A splintersoul pushes the boundaries of what it means to have two separate identities.
replaces_summary: Dual Identity; Unshakable; 3rd, 7th-level Social Talents; Startling Appearance; Frightening Appearance; Stunning Appearance

name: Teisatsu
description: Teisatsu are specialized vigilantes who focus on infiltrating social scenes and high-society gatherings on behalf of the feuding lords of Minkai.
replaces_summary: Weapon and Armor Proficiency; Vigilante Specialization; 2nd-level Vigilante Talents; Vigilante Talents

name: Warlock
description: Practicing magic in secret, the warlock obscures her arcane scholarship from public view.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Skill Ranks; Armor Proficiency; 4th, 8th, 10th, 14th, 16th-level Vigilante Talent; Vigilante Specialization; Unshakable

name: Wildsoul
description: Within some vigilantes are incredible innate abilities that, although normally the domain of natural beasts, inexplicably aid them in fighting their enemies.
replaces_summary: Vigilante Specialization; 2nd, 6th, 12th, 18th-level Vigilante Talent

name: Zealot
description: Dedicated to a single deity, the zealot hunts the enemies of his faith in secret, often because his religion is outlawed or persecuted in the region.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Skill Ranks; Weapon Proficieny; 4th, 8th, 10th, 14th, 16th-level Vigilante Talent; Alignment; Vigilante Specialization;

name: Calamity Caller
description: (Elf Only) While all elves feel a deep connection to nature, some elven priests take this bond further, using their link to call down the wrath of nature upon their enemies
replaces_summary: Blessings; Focus Weapon; Sacred Weapon; Bonus Feats; Aspect of War

name: Divine Champion
description: Divine champions are unswervingly devoted to their causes, and specialize in bringing battle to the enemies of their faiths.
replaces_summary: 3rd, 6th, 9th, 12th, 18th-level Bonus Feat

name: Feral Champion
description: When a warpriest devotes himself to a god of the natural world, he is sometimes blessed with supernatural powers that allow him to evoke animalistic power and fury.
replaces_summary: Blessings; Sacred Weapon; Sacred Armor

name: Fist of the Godclaw
description: Devoted to the divine control of law above all else, these zealots seek to establish absolute order.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Deities; Blessings; 3rd, 6th, 12th-level Bonus Feat; Aspect of War

name: Jistkan Magistrate
description: Some warpriests study the ancient magic that the Jistkan magistrates used to bind elementals and make pacts with genies.
replaces_summary: Class Skilss; Blessings; Spontaneous Casting; Bonus Languages; Channel Energy

name: Liberty's Blade
description: There are some who find in the call to freedom something primal and spiritual, even more so than other Andorens.
replaces_summary: Blessings; Sacred Weapon; Channel Energy; Sacred Armor

name: Mantis Zealot
description: Among the Red Mantis worshipers of Achaekek, some hold such strong faith in their assassin god that they gain divine power.
replaces_summary: Deity; Alignment; Weapon and Armor Proficiencies; Sacred Weapon; Sacred Armor; Aspect of War

name: Molthuni Arsenal Chaplain
description: Molthuni arsenal chaplains are warpriests trained in the Arsenal District in Korholm. They bolster their nation’s military by focusing on the militant aspects of their gods.
replaces_summary: Blessing; Sacred Armor; Sacred Weapon; Channel Energy

name: Proclaimer
description: A proclaimer rushes into Abyss-twisted wastes with nothing but his weapon in his hand and his faith in his heart, shouting his deity’s name to the skies
replaces_summary: Alignment; Deity; Spellcasting; Fervor; Sacred Armor; Channel Energy

name: Proselytizer
description: The proselytizer is the perfect militant missionary. He seeks to win the hearts and lay claim to the souls of those he meets, and—failing that—spreads the word of his deity at the edge of the sword.
replaces_summary: Weapon/Armor Proficiencies; Sacred Armor; 3rd-level Bonus Feat; Sacred Weapon; Blessings; Blessings (Major)

name: Shieldbearer
description: Shieldbearers are divine warriors who stand on the front lines of battle, shoulder to shoulder with the soldiers of their people.
replaces_summary: Focus Weapon; Sacred Weapon; Sacred Armor;

name: Sixth Wing Bulwark
description: The Sixth Wing bulwark are followers of Ragathiel that train to hold the line and defend against the incursion of evil forces, fortifying herself and her allies to stand strong in the face of any opposition, no matter how dire.
replaces_summary: Armor Proficiencies; Focus Weapon; Sacred Weapon; Sacred Armor; 6-th, 12th-, 18th-level Bonus Feats

name: Alley Witch
description: Some witches find their spark of magic in the long, dark shadows and forgotten places within the city.
replaces_summary: Familiar; 1st and 6th-level Hexes

name: Ashiftah
description: Known in Taldane as a “battle witch,” an ashiftah drifts like a phantom among the enemy armies, calling down disaster upon their heads and weakening their resolve.
replaces_summary: Familiar; 2nd, 6th-level Hex

name: Beast-Bonded
description: While all witches are intimately tied to their familiars, a beast-bonded witch’s craft focuses specifically on her familiar bond and developing the relationship with her patron through her familiar.
replaces_summary: 4th-level Hex; 8th-level Hex; 10th-level Major Hex

name: Bonded Witch
description: (Half-Elf Only) While all witches commune with the unknown, the blend of human ingenuity and adept learning mixed with elven blood gives some half-elves a unique conduit to channel the powers of the arcane.
replaces_summary: Familiar

name: Bouda
description: These strange and often solitary witches have a strong connection to curses, the evil eye, and hyenas.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Familiar; 1st-level Hex; 10-level Major Hex

name: Cartomancer
description: A witch who serves the spirits of the harrow in exchange for mystical power is known as a cartomancer.
replaces_summary: Familiar; 2nd-level Hex

name: Coral Witch
description: Coral witches use their magic to fashion familiars from living coral and emulate the unyielding tenacity of these durable marine organisms.
replaces_summary: Patron; Familiar; 8th-level Hex

name: Demon-Sworn
description: In the depths of the Darklands, as well as similarly inhospitable locales on Golarion, many seek the succor of demons as a means of survival.
replaces_summary: Patron Spells; 6th-level Hex

name: Dimensional Occultist
description: The dimensional occultist sacrifices some of her mastery over hexes in return for much increased planar lore.
replaces_summary: 2nd-level Hex, 8th-level Hex, 12th-level Hex

name: Dreamweaver
description: (Changeling Only) A changeling dreamweaver draws upon her hag heritage to ply the dream realms in order to touch mortal minds and souls, for good or ill.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Patron Spells; 2nd, 6th, 10th-level Hexes

name: Flood Walker
description: Witches who dwell in floodplains or along the banks of predictably dangerous rivers can tap into the underlying potential of surging waters in order to enhance their eerie powers.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Class Skills; 4th, 10th, 16th, 18th-level Patron Spells; 1st, 6th, 10th-level Hex

name: Gingerbread Witch
description: A sweet tooth lures the gingerbread witch’s victims to doom.
replaces_summary: Familiar; 1st, 4th, 8th, 10th, 12th, 16th-level Hex

name: Gravewalker
description: Having much in common with necromancers, the gravewalker is obsessed with the occult manipulations of the dead, particularly mindless undead such as zombies.
replaces_summary: Patron Spells; Familiar; 1st-level Hex; 4th-level Hex; 8th-level Hex

name: Hag of Gyronna
description: Only female witches dare dedicate themselves to the Angry Hag, and those who do so are feared for their ability to bend minds and sow discord.
replaces_summary: Patron; Spells; 2nd, 8th, 12th-level Hexes

name: Hagbound
description: Whether a changeling in the process of succumbing to her mother’s call, a witch cursed by a hag to become her instrument of torment, a vain and petty witch overeager for power, or some other unfortunate soul, a hagbound witch finds its soul has been infected by a hag’s spite and powerful, corrupt arcane magic.
replaces_summary: 1st, 2nd, 4th, 8th, 10th, 12th, 14th, and 20th--level Hexes

name: Havocker
description: Although most witches are guided to subtle curses and debilitating hexes by their mysterious patrons, some are instead taught the secrets of harnessing raw, destructive elemental power.
replaces_summary: Hexes

name: Hedge Witch
description: Among witches, there are those who devote themselves to the care of others and restrict their practices to the healing arts.
replaces_summary: 4th-level Hex; 8th-level Hex

name: Herb Witch
description: Herb witches brew foul-tasting medicines, sweet poisons, and other concoctions from the untamed plants of the wild.
replaces_summary: Patron; 1st, 2nd, 10th-level Hex

name: Hex Channeler
description: A hex channeler is a witch who devotes herself to either life—healing the wounded and destroying the undead—or death, slaying the living and aiding undead.
replaces_summary: 2nd-level Hex

name: Invoker
description: The invoker uses her familiar to summon facets of her mysterious patron directly into her body, enhancing her skills and granting her powerful abilities.
replaces_summary: 1st, 8th, 10th, and 16th level Hex

name: Jinx Witch
description: Jinx witches specialize in calling down subtle misfortunes and deceiving enemies and allies alike. While they lack any ability to truly eliminate bad luck, they are uniquely gifted at making others believe otherwise.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; 2nd, 6th, 10th-level Hex

name: Ley Line Guardian
description: Some witches tap into the power of their patrons not through a special connection with a familiar, but rather directly through the vast network of ley lines that crosses the planes.
replaces_summary: Spellcasting; Familiar; 1st and 8th-level Hexes

name: Medium
description: Mediums tap into the spiritual power of those who have yet to find their final rest. Their ability to interact with these souls allows mediums to learn about the physical world through the experiences of those who came before them.
replaces_summary: Patron; 2nd, 6th, 20th-level Hexes

name: Mirror Witch
description: Mirror witches eschew animal familiars and isntead talk to their patrons via mirror magic
replaces_summary: Familiar

name: Mountain Witch
description: Mountains can be sanctuaries for witches hunted by society. Here they form bonds with the spirits of the lofty reaches.
replaces_summary: Spells; Hex; 2nd-level Hex

name: Nexian Spellspy
description: Nexian spellspies are witches who use their familiars and divining talents to spy on their rivals and protect themselves from similar intrusions.
replaces_summary: Deliver Touch Spells; 4th-level Hex

name: Pact Witch
description: Though all witches forge bonds with mysterious powers known as patrons, a pact witch takes this bond to an extreme by forging an inexorable pact with the Outer Planes.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Patron; Familar; 6th-level Hex

name: Putrefactor
description: Some witches find their patrons while in the throes of despair and grief, when all they desire is to see the veneer of civilization peel back to reveal the filth and rot that underlies all things.
replaces_summary: Familiar; Patron; 1st, 2nd, 4th, 6th, 10th, and 16th-level Hexes

name: Rhetorician
description: Rhetoricians feel driven to engage in debate, learn as much as they can about their potential foes, and seek ways to use information and quick talking to handle problems before resorting to raw violence.
replaces_summary: 1st-level Hex; Spells

name: Scarred Witch Doctor
description: (Orc Only) The scarred witch doctor draws power from her ability to endure pain and suffering.
replaces_summary: Spellcasting; Hexes; Familiar; 1st-level Hex

name: Sea Witch
description: A sea witch’s affinities are tied to the vast oceans and the rolling waves.
replaces_summary: Patron Spells; 1st-level Hex

name: Season Witch
description: Season witches gain their power from the cyclical and mystical exchange of energy passed from one season of nature to another.
replaces_summary: Patron; 1st-level Hex

name: Seducer
description: These charismatic witches, often devotees of the Green Mother, rely on their otherworldly charms to achieve their aims.
replaces_summary: Patron; Key Ability Score; 1st, 6th, 8th-level Hex

name: Synergist
description: Synergists gain the ability to combine their forms with their familiars’ in order to create something more powerful than either alone.
replaces_summary: 1st, 8th, and 14th-level Hexes

name: Tatterdemalion
description: Some witches can bend the warp and weft of fabric and thread.
replaces_summary: Weapon Proficiencies; Cantrips; 1st, 4th, 8th, 12th, 16th-level Hex

name: Vellemancer
description: While the popular stereotype envisions witches as wicked spellcasters sowing misery, many people turn to the arcane to heal others and better the world. The vellemancer is a guide, using her witchcraft to empower and teach others.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; 2nd, 6th, and 8th-level Hexes

name: Veneficus Witch
description: Veneficus witches specialize in poisons—brewing both magical and traditional concoctions, and applying them to weapons, traps or even her potent hexes.
replaces_summary: 2nd and 10th-level Hexes

name: Venom Siphoner
description: Witches are no strangers to poisons, using them alongside their various brews, curses, spells, and other tools of the trade.
replaces_summary: 1st, 2nd, 6th-level Hex; Alertness

name: White-Haired Witch
description: A white-haired witch concentrates her mysterious powers on improving her prowess in melee, using feats of agility and her prehensile hair to deal extreme damage.
replaces_summary: Hex; Major Hex; Grand Hex

name: Winter Witch
description: The descendents of Baba Yaga rule the frozen realm of Irrisen, and possess a unique power stemming from their otherworldly origin and their ties to cold magic.
replaces_summary: Familiar; Patron; 4th-level Hex

name: Witch-Watcher
description: For reasons known only to them, witch-watchers offer protection to nobles across Golarion.
replaces_summary: Spells per Day

name: Wyrm Witch
description: Some witch covens have learned to use treasure to access other draconic powers.
replaces_summary: Patron; Familiar

name: Absalom: Arcanamirium Crafter
description: One trained by the Arcanamirium, a college specializing in the universalist school of magic and the art of crafting magical items.
replaces_summary: Hand of the Apprentice

name: Arcane Bomber
description: To many wizards, the experimentation of the alchemist seems quaint, if not dangerous or frightening. A few wizards take up the secrets of the bomb, however, fusing alchemy with their already considerable magical power.
replaces_summary: Arcane Bond; Cantrips; Arcane School

name: Arcane Physician
description: Arcane physicians use their scholarly knowledge and arcane mastery to achieve incredible feats of medicine.
replaces_summary: Arcane School; Scribe Scroll

name: Arcane Warden
description: Arcane wardens are wizards who specialize in the discovery or generation of places of safety and refuge.
replaces_summary: Arcane School; Class Skills; Scribe Scroll; Bonus Feats; Hand of the Apprentice; Metamagic Mastery

name: Bonded Wizard
description: Many wizards form an arcane bond with an item, but for some this bond becomes a powerful mystic union.
replaces_summary: Scribe Scroll; 5th, 10th, and 15th-level Bonus Feats

name: Cheliax: Egorian Academy Infernal Binder
description: A wizard trained by the prestigious Egorian Academy, best known for its Infernal Binding program in the school of conjuration.
replaces_summary: Acid Dart, Dimensional Steps

name: Chronomancer
description: Rare arcane scholars known as chronomancers demonstrate the ability to shift themselves in short bursts between the past, future, and alternate presents.
replaces_summary: Arcane Bond; 10th, 15th, 20th-level Bonus Feats

name: Clocksmith
description: Clocksmiths are oddities among wizard communities, pursuing the study and creation of clockwork constructs to near obsession
replaces_summary: Arcane Bond; Arcane School; Scribe Scroll; Bonus Feats

name: Cruoromancer
description: (Dhampir Only) A cruoromancer infuses his necromantic magic with the power of his unique mixture of living blood and undead ichor.
replaces_summary: Arcane Bond; 5th, 10th, 15th, 20th-level Bonus Feat

name: Elder Mythos Scholar
description: Elder Mythos scholars risk their sanity to seek knowledge of alien and awful entities from beyond the stars.
replaces_summary: Arcane Bond; 1st, 8th-level Arcane School Ability; Spellbook; Scribe Scroll; 5th, 10th-level Bonus feats

name: Familiar Adept
description: Many wizards employ familiars to assist them, but only a few have unlocked the true power of their school of magic through the familiar itself.
replaces_summary: Scribe Scroll; 5th and 10th-level Bonus Feats; Arcane Bond; Spellbooks

name: First World Caller
description: Some wizards, typically First World gnomes, have mystical ties with the First World, the primordial home of the fey
replaces_summary: Arcane Bond; Arcane School; Scribe Scroll; 10th, 15th-level Bonus Feats

name: Hallowed Necromancer
description: Many wizards study necromancy to create undead, but some study the same arts to purge the stain of undeath.
replaces_summary: Arcane School; Spells; Power Over Undead; Grave Touch; Scribe Scroll; 5th, 10th, 15th-level Bonus Feat

name: Instructor
description: Whether serving as staff at a formal school of magic or simply acting as a wandering teacher, an instructor has an apprentice who trades service to the instructor for lessons in magic.
replaces_summary: Arcane Bond; 5th, 10th, 15th, and 20th-level Bonus Feats

name: Pact Wizard (FF)
description: Some wizards make bargains with beings from other realms in order to gain arcane power. These pact wizards have unparalleled access to extraplanar allies, but these bonds never come without strings attached.
replaces_summary: Arcane Bond; Arcane School

name: Pact Wizard (HHH)
description: Wizards who seek mastery of arcane power without tedious study and monotonous research.
replaces_summary: Spellcasting; Scribe Scroll; 5th, 10th, 15th, and 20th level Bonus Feats

name: Poleiheira Adherent
description: Poleiheira adherents are wizards who wish to emulate the explorations and discoveries of Arustun and partake in great odysseys as they seek to imitate his achievements.
replaces_summary: Arcane Bond; Arcane School

name: Primalist
description: A primalist is a wizard who has spent a considerable amount of time studying the chaos that is primal magic.
replaces_summary: Arcane Bond, 5th-level Bonus Feat, 10th-level Bonus Feat

name: Qadira: Mage of the Veil
description: Rather than controlling the elements or transforming the environment, mages of the veil focus on much more subtle magic.
replaces_summary: Blinding Ray, Invisibility Field

name: Runesage
description: Runesages draw upon the mystic energies of ancient Thassilon.
replaces_summary: Arcane Bond; Arcane School

name: Scroll Scholar
description: Those who trade some of their potential to better understand ancient texts and scrolls can become learned scroll scholars.
replaces_summary: Diviner's Fortune (diviner) or Hand of the Apprentice (universalist), 5th-level Bonus Feat, 4th-level spell slot
replaced_features: diviner's fortune (diviner) or hand of the apprentice (universalist)

name: Scrollmaster
description: To some wizards, a scroll is not just a written form of a spell, it is a physical weapon meant to be used in combat like a sword or a shield.
replaces_summary: Arcane Bond; 10th-level Bonus Feat

name: Shadowcaster
description: Trained in the dark mysteries of Nidal's Umbral Court, the shadowcaster harnesses the power of shadows to bolster their spellcasting.
replaces_summary: Arcane Bond; 5th-level Bonus Feat; 10th-level Bonus Feat

name: Siege Mage
description: The siege mage combines his arcane mastery with a supernatural link to siege engines.
replaces_summary: Scribe Scroll; Arcane Bond; Cantrips; Arcane School

name: Spellbinder
description: (Elf Only) A spellbinder is an elven wizard who forges an arcane bond between himself and one or more wizard spells.
replaces_summary: Arcane Bond

name: Spellslinger
description: While few contest the seductive allure of commanding arcane and occult powers, there are those wizards who become obsessed with the natural mysteries of black powder.
replaces_summary: Arcane Bond; Scribe Scroll; Cantrips; Arcane School

name: Spirit Binder
description: While most wizards learn their arts through gradual study, spirit binders have made a sudden arcane breakthrough due to the traumatic experience of losing a loved one.
replaces_summary: Arcane Bond; Arcane School; Scribe Scroll; Bonus Feats

name: Sword Binder
description: Sword binders follow a tradition of martial wizards who often worked with the Church of Aroden and the crowns of Taldor and then Cheliax.
replaces_summary: Arcane Bond; Arcane School; Bonus School Spell Slots; 10th-level Bonus Feat

name: Thassilonian Specialist
description: Specialist in a Thassilonian school of magic, this specialist sacrifices his connection with two schools in order to greatly strengthen his chosen specialty.
replaces_summary: Lose access to two schools of magic
replaced_features: lose access to two schools of magic

name: Undead Master
description: Undead masters have great power over undeath.
replaces_summary: Alignment; Arcane School; Arcane Bond; Scribe Scroll; 5th, 10th, 15th, 20th-level Bonus Feats

name: Wind Listener
description: (Sylph Only) The wind listener takes a sylph’s natural curiosity to the extreme, enhancing his natural skill at subterfuge and eavesdropping with potent arcane magic.
replaces_summary: Class Skills; Arcane School; Arcane Bond; 5th, 10th, 15th-level Bonus Feat

name: Worldseeker
description: Worldseekers are wizards who travel to all corners of the Great Beyond.
replaces_summary: Scribe Scroll; 6th/8th-level School Ability; Arcane Bond; 5th, 15th-level Bonus Feat

name: Aberrant Companion
description: There’s something oddly wrong about aberrant companions. Yet though they’re touched by eldritch magic or mutated by strange influences, they are still loyal, if unnatural, allies.
replaces_summary: Share Spells; Devotion; Multiattack; Natural Armor Progression

name: Accursed Companion
description: When a character loses an animal companion to a disturbingly gruesome death, the soul of the slain animal companion sometimes remains bound to the character. In such cases, this scarred soul infests the next companion the character takes.
replaces_summary: See text

name: Ambusher
description: Ambushers sneak up on unsuspecting prey, pouncing on targets when they least expect it.
replaces_summary: Share Spells; Improved Evasion; Evasion; Devotion; Multiattack

name: Apex Species
description: Some animals have no serious competitors and no real predators.
replaces_summary: Share Spells; Evasion; Devotion; Improved Evasion; Multiattack

name: Augmented Companion
description: Augmented companions have suffered an injury, such as the loss of a wing, and parts of their bodies have been replaced by a master construct crafter. This procedure grants them unusual abilities.
replaces_summary: Share Spells; Devotion

name: Auspice
description: Auspices were born with a birthmark or other feature that seems to be in the shape of their master’s deity’s holy symbol; they are usually animal sacred to that deity’s religion.
replaces_summary: Share Spells; Evasion; Improved Evasion

name: Bodyguard
description: Some companions live lives of faithful devotion and steady vigilance, standing watch through long hours and always ready to leap into action to protect their masters.
replaces_summary: Share Spells; Evasion; Multiattack; Improved Evasion

name: Bully
description: Bigger than others of its kind, a bully is used to winning fights and displays of dominance for its choice of mates, territory, or other privileges.
replaces_summary: Share Spells; Multiattack

name: Charger
description: The mighty charger is a boon to heavily armored and armed champions, bearing them and their ironmongery into battle without hesitation.
replaces_summary: Share Spells; Multiattack; Devotion; Improved Evasion

name: Daredevil
description: Daredevil companions join the fray with graceful leaps or swooping dives, heedless of the danger.
replaces_summary: Share Spells; Devotion; Multiattack

name: Deathtouched Companion
description: Whether the result of a partially successful attempt at revival, a strange blight, or repeated exposure to undead, deathtouched companions are living animals with a trace of the undead, somewhat like dhampirs.
replaces_summary: Share Spells; Devotion

name: Draconic Companion
description: Draconic companions bear a faint trace of dragon blood that grants them special abilities.
replaces_summary: Share Spells; Evasion; Devotion; Multiattack; Improved Evasion

name: Elemental Companion
description: The heart of this companion beats with primal energy.
replaces_summary: Evasion; Improved Evasion; Multiattack

name: Feytouched Companion
description: (Must be size Small) Feytouched companions are strangely colored fauna native to the First World, the primal realm of the fey. They gain strange abilities tied to the fey.
replaces_summary: 4th or 7th-level Advancement; Share Spells; Multiattack; Evasion; Devotion; Improved Evasion

name: Precocious Companion
description: Precocious companions are able to learn far more tricks than other companions; this ability to learn allows their masters to use them for a wider variety of tasks.
replaces_summary: Evasion; Improved Evasion; 4th or 7th-level Advancement

name: Racer
description: Some companions have uncanny speed, providing their masters with swift transport.
replaces_summary: Share Spells; Devotion

name: Totem Guide
description: Totem guides embody the wisdom and spirituality of the natural world, providing guidance as well as aid in combat.
replaces_summary: Evasion; Devotion; Multiattack; Improved Evasion

name: Tracker
description: (Must have scent) Some companions are expert trackers, able to use their scent ability to follow any trail.
replaces_summary: Share Spells; Devotion

name: Unexpected Intellectual
description: Sometimes, when an insect or other vermin is chosen to be an animal companion, it proves cleverer than expected. On occasion, it proves much cleverer.
replaces_summary: Share Spells

name: Verdant Companion
description: Favorites of druids who balance their responsibilities between flora and fauna, verdant companions are animals with some of the abilities and physical aspects of plants.
replaces_summary: Share Spells; Evasion; Devotion; Improved Evasion

name: Wrecker
description: Like unruly pets, some companions have a tendency to destroy nearby objects while unsupervised, and their masters can channel these destructive impulses into an advantage in battle.
replaces_summary: Share Spells; Multiattack

name: Ambassador
description: An ambassador speaks on its master’s behalf and sometimes on behalf of its master’s patron or other extraplanar contacts.
replaces_summary: Alertness; Intelligence Score Progression

name: Animal Exemplar
description: An animal exemplar is a paragon of its species, able to command the loyalty of others of its kind.
replaces_summary: Speak with Animals of its Kind; Deliver Touch Spells; Spell Resistance; Scry on Familiar
replaced_features: speak with animals of its kind

name: Arcane Amplifier
description: The secret of the arcane amplifier familiar was first discovered in Irrisen, where the winter witches used white-furred foxes or hares to strengthen their spells.
replaces_summary: Alertness; Improved Evasion; Speak with Animals of its Kind; Spell Resistance; Scry on Familiar
replaced_features: speak with animals of its kind

name: Bloodline Familiars
description: Those with an inherent connection to magic often attract creatures who feel a similar instinctive pull toward magical forces.
replaces_summary: (N/A; not technically an archetype, affects the familiar’s owner) 1st-level Bloodline power; Spells
replaced_features: affects the familiar’s owner) 1st-level bloodline power

name: Decoy
description: A decoy misdirects its master’s enemies, allowing the master to strike by surprise.

replaced_features: speak with animals of its kind

name: Egotist
description: An egotist believes itself to be the real master in the relationship—the power behind the throne. It often attempts to communicate “orders” for its master as best it can, interfering in matters ranging from spell choices and tactical combat decisions to its master’s love life.
replaces_summary: Alertness; Variable Familiar Bonus; Speak with Master; Deliver Touch Spells; Scry on Familiar

name: Elemental Familiar
description: The raw power of an elemental plane suffuses the body of an elemental familiar.
replaces_summary: Improved Evasion; Speak with Animals of its Kind
replaced_features: speak with animals of its kind

name: Emissary
description: The emissary is touched by the divine, serving as a font of wisdom and a moral compass for its master.
replaces_summary: Alertness; Share Spells; Deliver Touch Spells

name: Figment
description: Figments are born from their masters’ imaginations rather than being ordinary creatures that are awakened.
replaces_summary: Improved Evasion; Deliver Touch Spells; Speak with Animals of its Kind; Scry on Familiar
replaced_features: speak with animals of its kind

name: Infiltrator
description: Familiars can be skilled spies, and infiltrators are the very best of their kind at the subtle art of espionage.

replaced_features: speak with animals of its kind

name: Mascot
description: A familiar sometimes serves as the centerpiece of an adventuring party. Known as a mascot, this type of familiar eventually treats the entire party as its master.

replaced_features: speak with animals of its kind

name: Mauler
description: While most familiars are scouts and assistants, the mauler familiar cares only for the thrill of battle. A mauler often serves a bloodthirsty or martial-minded master.

replaced_features: speak with animals of its kind

name: Occult Messenger
description: Some familiars are messengers of greater powers than themselves.
replaces_summary: Alertness; Deliver Touch Spells

name: Parasite
description: Most familiars are still something akin to normal animals, conduits for power rather than powers themselves. The parasite is one of the more disturbing exceptions.

name: Patron Familiars
description: Witches’ familiars are often tied to their patrons, enhancing and reinforcing the spellcasters’ connections to the sources of their magical might.

name: Pilferer
description: Some familiars are stealthy pilferers that perform tricks of thievery or simple spying on their master’s behalf.

replaced_features: speak with animals of its kind

name: Prankster
description: Some familiars love to perform pranks on their unsuspecting masters, as well as on those around their masters—allies and enemies alike. While good-aligned pranksters’ tricks are usually good natured except against foes, evil pranksters play tricks that are mean spirited or even downright cruel.

name: Protector
description: Protector familiars are so devoted that they would give their lives for their masters. A tumor familiar can’t be a protector.

description: Sages are masters of useful facts, able to recall them for their masters’ benefit, though this leads many to become haughty and proud.

description: A valet is a consummate personal servant, able to fetch, deliver, and perform for its master’s every need.
